Root cause was **NOT** binary size (a 16 MB binary is fine with the >256 MB RAM work) but a VA collision: the per-process interrupt stacks (`proc_rsp0`, phys `0xE00000‚Äì0xF00000`) **and** the per-CPU syscall kstacks (`pcpu_syscall_kstack_top` `0xF10000+`, `pcpu_syscall_kstack_top2` `0xF90000+`) lived in **region 7 (14‚Äì16 MB)**, inside the low identity window where `elf_load` maps user PT_LOADs. A large binary's segment (ark's reaches ~20 MB) made `proc_map_page` **override `PD[7]`** in that proc's CR3 ‚Üí the kstack mapping vanished ‚Üí the next CPL3‚ÜíCPL0 / syscall stack push faulted. This is the **same bug class** the 1.50.9/1.50.10 PMM-bitmap fix named ("any kernel datum touched under a per-proc CR3 must be reached via the direct-map, never a ‚â§256 MB identity VA") ‚Äî and the 1.50.10 "bitmap was the unique victim" audit **missed it**, because it only counted the fixed `PD[2]` ring-3-code override, not that large *segments* override `PD[2..N]`. **Fix:** point `RSP0` and both syscall-kstack tables at the **direct-map alias** of the same region-7 phys (`DIRECTMAP_BASE + phys`, 8 GB+ ‚Äî `PDPT[8..71]`, mirrored into every per-proc CR3, far above any user VA and never segment-overridden). Data-only: the syscall stub already loads RSP via `mov rsp,[r8*8 + &table]` (a 64-bit load), so 8 GB+ VAs need no stub change; phys stays region-7-reserved (no PMM change). The kstacks were **NOT** shrunk and ark was **NOT** shrunk ‚Äî this reuses the 1.49.x direct-map infrastructure exactly as intended. Files: `kernel/core/proc.cyr`, `kernel/arch/x86_64/syscall_hw.cyr`.

### Added
- **(a) symlink#63 ‚Äî cyrius peer + on-agnos round-trip = COMPLETE.** The cyrius `sys_symlink`#63 peer landed **cyrius 6.3.6** (current 6.3.9), completing the two-sided item the 1.51.0 arm opened. New deterministic regression `scripts/symlink-smoke.sh` (+ `SYMLINK_SELFTEST` gate, `tests/symlink/symtest.cyr`): a `--agnos` program calls `sys_symlink("/etc/hostname","/hn_link")`, the symlink lands (`debugfs`: `Type: symlink`, `Fast link dest: "/etc/hostname"`), `open()` follows it to the target bytes, and `e2fsck -fn` is clean. ark's install path is wired to the peer (`ark/src/portable.cyr`).
- **`scripts/ark-run-smoke.sh` + `ARK_SELFTEST` gate** ‚Äî deterministic `run /bin/ark` large-binary exec probe (the standing regression for the kstack fix; PASS = ark's CLI output reaches the console + no fault).

### Notes
- Validation: `ark-run-smoke` PASS (ark parses argv, prints usage, exits clean); **no regression** ‚Äî `ring3-smoke`, `exec-smoke` (incl. execwait#37 nested exec, which uses the *second* relocated kstack), `agnsh-smoke`, `check.sh` 11/11 all green. The ark M3 on-agnos `.ark`-install round-trip is now runtime-unblocked; remaining is the symlink-bearing `.ark` fixture + `ark install` on agnos.

## [1.51.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.51.0 ‚Äî OPENS the sovereign-package-manager kernel-surface arc** (the ark v2 / agnova prerequisite gaps; roadmap "Future minors" ‚Üí Active). The 1.50.x RAM full-usage continuation arc is **CLOSED + iron-validated** (1.50.0‚Äì1.50.10 on real 64 GB Zen ‚Äî RAM stress, boot-CR3‚Üíown-PML4, mmap-arena lift, process-isolation hardening, and the agnsh-`&` fix all burned green). This arc adds the five kernel asks the sovereign-ark chain (`ark ‚Üí agnova ‚Üí server-stage exit`) needs, surfaced + adversarially verified by the 2026-06-29 ark v2 sovereignty research ([`agnosticos/.../planning/ark-v2-sovereignty-path.md`](https://github.com/MacCracken/agnosticos/blob/main/docs/development/planning/ark-v2-sovereignty-path.md)). Sequenced by which ark milestone each unblocks ‚Äî near-term first, the build-half trio (c/d/e) gated on the ark self-host milestones approaching.

### Added
- **(a) DO-FIRST ‚Äî `symlink`#63 syscall (agnos arm).** `symlink(target, targetlen, linkpath, linkpathlen=a4)` creates a symbolic link `linkpath` whose contents are the **TEXT** `target` ‚Äî NOT a path to resolve (a symlink may point at a nonexistent/relative target), so `target` is validated as a bounded **user buffer** (`is_user_range`, 1..one ext2 block), while `linkpath` is a real path (`sc_path_ok` + ext2-only; FAT/exFAT have no inodes ‚Üí -1). Mirrors the #32 hardlink handler's shape (`vfs_resolve_mount` ‚Üí `fs_spin_lock` ‚Üí `vfs_ext2_parent` for parent+basename ‚Üí backend call); the inode/dirent work is the **pre-existing** `ext2_symlink` (1.33.3 ‚Äî fast `<60 B` inline / slow = one data block, e2fsck-clean). Returns 0 / -1. Unblocks **ark M3** = on-agnos install of a prebuilt signed `.ark` (= agnova's install minimum); `ark_pkg_install` pass-2 creates `.so ‚Üí .so.N` symlinks. **Free number:** #0-62 were all taken (#44 `sched_yield` dispatches in `syscall_hw.cyr`), so symlink takes the next free **#63**; the planned 1.52.x audio band ("next free contiguous band") shifts accordingly to **#64-69**.
  - ‚öÝ **TWO-SIDED ‚Äî NOT complete at this cut.** Ring 3 cannot call `symlink`#63 until **cyrius** adds the matching `sys_symlink`#63 peer in `lib/syscalls_x86_64_agnos.cyr` (which today has `sys_link` but **no `sys_symlink`**). Cyrius is hands-off ([[feedback_cyrius_hands_off]]) ‚Äî the kernel arm alone is a no-op to userland, so this is **flagged to the user**, not landed here. **(a) is marked done only when both sides ship AND an on-agnos `.ark`-with-symlinks install round-trips** ([[feedback_qemu_test_agnos_userland]] ‚Äî compiling ‚âÝ working).

### Notes
- Validation: kernel builds clean at 1.51.0, `check.sh` 11/11. The symlink arm has no ring-3 exerciser yet (blocked on the cyrius peer); `ext2_symlink` itself is already covered by the ext2-write selftest (`Wsym fast`/`Wsym slow`, e2fsck-clean). End-to-end (ring-3 `syscall(63)` ‚Üí on-disk symlink) validates once the cyrius peer lands.
- **Remaining arc items (filed, not started):** **(b)** atomic system-update / boot-slot primitive (ark M4 ‚Äî design call owed: A/B image-swap vs ark-driven in-place re-materialize); **(c)** nested/recursive exec from a spawned proc (`execwait`#37 refuses re-entry ‚Äî gated on ark M5/M6, takumi self-host); **(d)** argv/env length-cap raise (#37/#43 cap path+argv at 127 B ‚Äî pairs with c); **(e)** build confinement (converges with Phase 20's sandbox primitive ‚Äî don't double-build). Per [[project_ark_v2_sovereignty_path]].

## [1.50.10] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.10 ‚Äî hardening pass over the 1.50.9 agnsh-`&` fix: regression guards + edge-config warning + sibling audit.** No functional change to the production path ‚Äî the 1.50.9 fix is unchanged; this adds the guards that *would have caught it* and bounds the bug class so it can't silently return.

### Added
- **Always-on `bitmap-dm` guard (`kernel/core/main.cyr`).** After the bitmap rebase, every boot now asserts (when RAM > 256 MB) that `pmm_bitmap_ptr >= DIRECTMAP_BASE` ‚Äî i.e. the bitmap is reached via the **direct-map**, not its per-proc-shadowed identity VA ‚Äî and prints the live free-2 MB count (`pmm: bitmap-dm OK free2mb=N`). `N` was exactly **0** under the 1.50.9 bug (a per-proc CR3 shadowed the bitmap), so a non-zero count is the symptom-absent signal. Cheap (mirrors the always-on directmap probe); the agnsh/exec smokes get it for free and can grep it.
- **KEEP_GNOBOOT + >256 MB warning.** In a `BOOTCR3_KEEP_GNOBOOT_CR3` build the rebase is gated off (the boot CR3 has no direct-map), so the bitmap stays on its identity VA and >256 MB user `mmap` would fault under a per-proc CR3. That config now warns loudly at boot (`pmm: WARN bitmap on identity VA (KEEP_GNOBOOT >256MB) ‚Ä¶`) instead of shipping the shadow silently ‚Äî switch to the own-PML4 default for >256 MB.
- **`PPID_SELFTEST` extended with the reap-off-cpu fence self-owned check (`kernel/core/selftests.cyr`).** Hermetically sets pid 1's `on_cpu` to this CPU (the kernel_resume-terminated state), calls `proc_reap_off_cpu_fence(1)`, and asserts it **returns** (clearing `on_cpu`) rather than spinning ‚Äî the 1.50.9 box-hang. If the self-owned clause regresses, the box hangs inside the selftest and `ppid: child-gate PASS` never prints (so the existing marker also guards the fence). Save/restored; no proc churn.

### Notes
- **Sibling audit (in `pmm.cyr`).** Confirmed the bitmap was the **unique** victim of the per-proc-CR3 identity-VA shadow: the only PDE override inside the kernel-identity range `PD[0..127]` is `PD[2]` (ring-3 code @ `0x400000`); the user stack (`PD[510]`) and mmap arena (`PD[128..510]`) are above it, and `proc_create_address_space` copies `PD[0..127]` verbatim. So the bitmap ‚Äî the sole `pmm_alloc_2mb`'d kernel structure read by its identity VA during a syscall, able to land on region 2 ‚Äî was the only exposure. Documented the general rule: **any kernel datum touched under a per-proc CR3 (syscall/fault) must be reached via the direct-map**, never a ‚â§256 MB identity VA.
- Validation (QEMU, RAM > 256 MB): `pmm: bitmap-dm OK free2mb=228`, `ppid: child-gate PASS` (incl. the fence), `agnsh-bg-test` / `agnsh-multijob-test` PASS, `check.sh` 11/11. Production (plain) build carries only the always-on `bitmap-dm` guard; the fence check is `PPID_SELFTEST`-gated. Follow-up offered but not taken: a `PMM_CR3SHADOW_SELFTEST` that builds a throwaway CR3 shadowing `0x400000` with a poison page and reads the bitmap under it (the end-to-end reproduction) ‚Äî deferred because the functional smokes already exercise that path and a boot-time CR3-switching selftest carries more risk than the always-on guard.

## [1.50.9] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.9 ‚Äî agnsh `&` background jobs restored: the migrated PMM bitmap was shadowed by the ring-3 code VA, and the SMP reap-fence deadlocked on the just-faulted proc.** `agnsh` died with a #PF right after its banner on any >256 MB-RAM boot ‚Äî its first heap allocation (`Interpreter_new` ‚Üí `alloc` ‚Üí `sys_mmap`#27) returned `0`, so the null-pointer store faulted ‚Äî and the box then **hung** instead of recovering. Two independent regressions from the 1.49.x/1.50.x RAM + 1.46.x SMP arcs; neither is a `spawn_path`#43 fault (the #43 kernel path was always correct ‚Äî `exec-smoke`'s `ring3: spawnpath OK` passes; agnsh simply never reached a prompt to *issue* `&`). Root-caused on the dev box via QEMU-monitor register/stack reads + `-d int` (RIP stuck in `proc_reap_off_cpu_fence`; the `#PF` was `CR2=0`, `e=0x7`; `xp` showed phys `0x400000` was clean but the kernel's `load64` there returned the agnsh ELF header). Found-only-at-runtime: both reproduce **only with RAM > 256 MB** (‚â§256 MB skips the bitmap migration). [[feedback_dont_dodge_large_items]]

### Fixed
- **PMM bitmap shadowed by a per-process VA ‚Üí `sys_mmap` saw 0 free 2 MB regions (the agnsh banner #PF).** `pmm_migrate_bitmap` (RAM > 256 MB) allocates the RAM-backed bitmap from the **‚â§256 MB pool** (`pmm_alloc_2mb` returned phys `0x400000`) and pointed `pmm_bitmap_ptr` at its **identity VA**. But `0x400000` is the **fixed ring-3 code VA** (`PD[2]`, `ring3.cyr`) ‚Äî every per-process CR3 *overrides* `PD[2]` to map the proc's own code. A syscall runs under the **caller's** CR3, so `sys_mmap`#27's `pmm_count_2mb_free()` read **agnsh's ELF code** (‚âà40 % of bits set in every region) instead of the bitmap ‚Üí "no free 2 MB region" ‚Üí `mmap` returned `0`. **Fix:** after `pmm_setup_directmap`, rebase `pmm_bitmap_ptr` onto the **direct-map** (`DIRECTMAP_BASE + phys`, new `pmm_bitmap_use_directmap()` + `pmm_bitmap_phys`). The direct-map lives in the kernel `PDPT[8+]` window that every per-proc CR3 **mirrors and never overrides**, so the bitmap reads correctly under **every** CR3. The rebase is gated AFTER the `cr3_load(0x1000)` switch (the direct-map is only live under the kernel PML4; boot-time `pmm_alloc`s before the switch keep using the identity VA, unshadowed since no proc runs yet). No-op for RAM ‚â§ 256 MB (bitmap stays the static `.bss` array, VA below all user mappings). Why it worked at 1.44.x: RAM was ‚â§ 256 MB, so the bitmap never migrated.
- **`proc_reap_off_cpu_fence` deadlocked reaping a proc that terminated via `kernel_resume` (the box HANG).** The 1.46.x SMP fence spins until `on_cpu[pid] == -1`. A ring-3 **fault** (`fault_kill_current`) or **exit#0** on the **foreground** exec child returns to the parent via `kernel_resume` **without** going through `do_context_switch`, so `on_cpu` is never cleared and still points at the current CPU. Under `exec_preempt=1` (preemptive agnsh) that child *was* scheduled (its `on_cpu` was set), so `proc_reap` (kybernet's reap of agnsh) spun **forever** (IF=0, single core ‚Äî a hard hang). **Fix:** the fence now clears `on_cpu` and proceeds when **this** CPU owns the dead proc (no foreign in-flight restore to wait out); a **different** CPU's ownership still spins (the real SMP race the fence guards). Single-core: the self-owned case is the only one, so the loop never iterates. Proven load-bearing: with the clause reverted, `agnsh` `exit` hangs after "bye" (no `kybernet: shell exited`); with it, the reap completes.

### Notes
- Validation (QEMU): **`agnsh-bg-test` PASS** (`sleeper &` launches non-blocking via #43, prompt stays live, job reaped with `[1] Done`), **`agnsh-multijob-test` PASS** (two concurrent `&` jobs, out-of-order reap), **`exec-smoke` PASS** incl. `ring3: spawnpath OK` + `EXEC-DISK-OK` √ó2, agnsh interactive (`help`/`version`/`mode`) responds, agnsh `exit` recovers cleanly, **`check.sh` 11/11**. Iron rides the next burn.
- **`exec-smoke` default `QEMU_TIMEOUT` 30 ‚Üí 90** (`scripts/exec-smoke.sh`). Unrelated to the bug: under the `RING3_SELFTEST` profile the `ring3: spawnpath OK` verdict prints only after the scheduler selftest's preemption proc B counts to 2¬≤‚Å¥ while time-sliced ‚Äî ~tens of seconds under TCG ‚Äî so 30 s truncated QEMU mid-selftest and the gate **falsely** read as a spawnpath failure (the symptom in the bug report). The #43 path itself was never broken.
- **Test maintenance:** `agnsh-bg-test` / `agnsh-multijob-test` / `agnsh-bg-smp4-test` matched a hard-coded `agnoshi 1.7.0` banner; agnsh is now 1.8.x, so the liveness probe falsely failed. Relaxed to the version-agnostic `agnoshi 1.` so the marker survives agnsh version bumps.

## [1.50.8] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.8 ‚Äî waitpid#4 ownership gate (1.44.22 multi-threading-cleanup item, un-blocked by 1.50.7).** The sibling of the 1.50.7 kill#16 child-gate, on the same `proc_ppid` infra. This was one of four items the 1.44.22 audit deferred; the others' status: **`sched_next` single-pass already landed at 1.47.8**, and **MADT AP-enumeration + loader DRY-up** were never `proc_get_ppid`-blocked (they stay deferred ‚Äî SMP-boot / iron-validated-loader refactors with risk and no functional gain). Only waitpid#4 was un-blocked by 1.50.7's real `proc_get_ppid`.

### Fixed
- **waitpid#4 ownership gate.** `waitpid(pid)`#4 had **no ownership check** ‚Äî any proc could reap any *dead* proc, stealing its exit code and collapsing the slot out from under the real parent's pending `waitpid`. The gate (`proc_may_reap`) was deferred at the 1.44.22 audit because `proc_get_ppid` stubbed `0`: enforcing parent-ownership then would have meant "only init may reap" and **broken agnsh `&`-job reaping**. Now that 1.50.7 made `proc_get_ppid` real, agnsh **is** the parent of its `&` jobs, so it reaps them and a non-parent cannot. New `proc_may_reap(caller, target)` (proc.cyr, sibling of `proc_may_signal`): **init reaps anyone** (the orphan reaper), a non-init caller reaps **only its own direct child** (no "self" clause ‚Äî waitpid#4 already rejects `target==caller`). The gate is checked **before** the alive/dead test, so a non-child gets `-1` regardless of state. **Guardrail:** child-only, no process-group reach ([[project_agnos_auth_posture]]).

### Added
- `PPID_SELFTEST` extended with the `proc_may_reap` truth table (init / parent / stranger), alongside the existing `proc_may_signal` checks ‚Äî one hermetic block now covers both child-gates (`ppid: child-gate PASS`).

### Notes
- Validation: `check.sh` 11/11, **`ring3-smoke` PASS incl. `ring3: parent spawn+wait OK`** (a ring-3 parent `spawn`#3's a child then `waitpid`#4-reaps it to exit ‚Äî the gate's exact happy path: parent reaps its own child, must pass), extended `ppid: child-gate PASS`, `agnsh-bg-test` / `agnsh-multijob-test` (agnsh reaping its `&` jobs ‚Äî the real consumer). Behavior-preserving for every working reap flow (foreground exec uses the kernel-internal `proc_reap`, not #4). Iron rides the next burn alongside 1.50.7.

## [1.50.7] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.7 ‚Äî process-isolation hardening: bg-job fault teardown + real `proc_get_ppid`.** Two small sovereign items surfaced + adversarially verified by the 2026-06-29 kavach‚Üîagnos compat audit ([`agnosticos/.../kavach-agnos-compat.md`](https://github.com/MacCracken/agnosticos/blob/main/docs/development/planning/kavach-agnos-compat.md)). Both are capability/ownership hardening ‚Äî **no Linux projection**.

### Fixed
- **bg-job fault teardown (a).** A ring-3 (CPL3) fault routes to `fault_kill_current`, which `kernel_resume()`s back to the shell for the **foreground** exec child ‚Äî but a **background** (`&`) proc's `#PF`/`#GP`/`#UD` previously fell through to the IDT stub's canary-bar **halt**, killing the whole box. A server running concurrent bg services couldn't survive one service's ring-3 fault. Now the bg path (already torn down above ‚Äî `proc_set_state(pid,0)` + SIGCHLD to its parent, identical to a clean `exit`) **yields to the scheduler** instead of halting: `sti; hlt` until the next timer tick's `do_context_switch` saves the dead slot (harmless), declines to revive it (state 0), and context-switches to the next ready proc (agnsh / idle) through the timer ISR's own iretq epilogue. Reuses the **proven timer-preemption path** ‚Äî no new switch mechanism. The foreground path is unchanged (`fault-kill-smoke: PASS` ‚Äî `run: exit 142` + `SURVIVED`, no regression).
- **real `proc_get_ppid` ‚Üí kill#16 child-gate (b).** `proc_get_ppid` was a stub returning `0` always, leaving `kill#16`'s "a non-init proc may signal only itself or its children" gate **inert** (every proc's parent read as init). Added a flat **`proc_ppid[16]`** (parallel to `proc_cs[16]`/`proc_ss[16]`), filled at **`proc_create_user`** from the creator pid ‚Äî the single inheritance point every user proc is born through (elf_load / elf_load_from_file / spawn), the same source `vfs_fd_inherit` already uses ‚Äî reset to `0` for a recycled slot in `proc_alloc_slot`, and read back by `proc_get_ppid`. The kill#16 authorization is **extracted** into a testable `proc_may_signal(caller, target)` (init signals anyone; a non-init caller signals only itself or a direct child) called from both ksyscall and the new selftest ‚Äî behavior-preserving refactor of the prior inline gate. **Guardrail:** signal-ownership only ‚Äî deliberately **NOT** grown into pgid/sid / `kill(-pgid,‚Ä¶)` (that would be Unix projection, [[project_agnos_auth_posture]]). Side effect (intended): `exit#0`/`fault` SIGCHLD now reaches the **real** parent (e.g. agnsh for its bg jobs) instead of always pid 0 ‚Äî benign, signals are non-destructive bitmasks with no default-terminate action; bg reaping is poll-driven, not SIGCHLD-driven.

### Added
- **`PPID_SELFTEST`** (`selftests.cyr`, build flag `PPID_SELFTEST=1`) ‚Äî hermetic: the getter's out-of-range guard, a save/restore roundtrip of `proc_get_ppid` through a live in-range slot, and `proc_may_signal` across init / self / own-child / stranger / signal-init callers. `ppid: child-gate PASS`. Proves the getter is no longer the always-0 stub and the kill#16 gate is real.

### Notes
- This **un-blocks** the deferred 1.44.22 *waitpid#4 ownership gate* cleanup (its blocker was "`proc_get_ppid` is a stub"); not implemented here ‚Äî a separate item, and with real ppid agnsh IS the parent of its `&` jobs so it would no longer break `&` reaping.
- Validation: `check.sh` 11/11, `fault-kill-smoke: PASS`, `ppid: child-gate PASS`, `agnsh-smoke: PASS` (boot-to-prompt), `exec-smoke` all-PASS except the pre-existing baseline `spawnpath#43` item (verified identical with these changes stashed). Production binary byte-stable (the selftest + new paths are tiny / DCE-gated). **Bite (a)'s on-iron bg-fault survival rides the next burn** ‚Äî the deterministic bg-fault integration test (scheduler + faulting bg ELF + survival across a context switch) is heavier than a hermetic selftest; the foreground sibling is QEMU-green and the bg code is derived from the proven preemption path.

## [1.50.6] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.6 ‚Äî PF_X-aware ELF mapping + NX user stack (W^X, mmap-arena follow-on #4).** Both ELF loaders (`elf_load_from_file` for exec-from-disk, `elf_load` for in-memory spawn) now read each `PT_LOAD`'s `p_flags` (offset 4) and map the segment **executable only if `PF_X` is set**, else NX (`proc_map_page_nx`); and the **user stack** (`0x3FC00000`, pure data) is now mapped **NX** in both loaders. The stack NX blocks the classic stack-shellcode vector ‚Äî safe because AGNOS delivers signals via kernel-side bitmasks (`proc_signals`), with **no user-stack trampoline** (no `sigreturn`), and cyrius programs don't execute their stack.

**Honest scope ‚Äî segment-level W^X is currently blocked by the ELF format, not the kernel.** cyrius/cyrld emits **one `RWE` PT_LOAD per binary** (code + data + bss packed together ‚Äî confirmed on `agnsh`/`kriya`/`owl`), so that segment legitimately has `PF_X` and stays executable+writable (RWX). The PF_X mechanism is correct and future-proof ‚Äî a data-only (`!PF_X`) segment *would* be NX'd ‚Äî but no cyrius binary has one yet. **True segment W^X (RX code / RW data) needs cyrius to emit separate code/data segments** (a cyrius-side change; `cyrius` is out of this repo's scope). What 1.50.6 delivers today: the **NX stack** (a real hardening) + the correct per-segment mechanism. Verified: `exec-smoke` PASS (programs run with an NX stack + reap cleanly), `ring3-smoke` PASS (in-memory loader, 8 concurrent procs), `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` PASS. The mmap arena (1.50.5) + the user stack (1.50.6) are now both NX; the remaining executable-writable surface is the cyrius single-RWE code+data segment.

## [1.50.5] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.5 ‚Äî W^X: NX on anonymous mmap-arena pages (mmap-arena follow-on #3).** Anonymous `mmap` memory is pure data ‚Äî a program never executes its heap ‚Äî so the arena PDEs are now mapped **non-executable** (NX, bit 63; `0x8000000000000087` = the canonical user data flag from `vmm.cyr`). A heap spray can no longer be jumped into. (EFER.NXE is set at boot ‚Äî `boot_shim.cyr`.) **The split:** `proc_map_page` is the shared user-page mapper and also maps ELF **code** segments, which MUST stay executable ‚Äî so NX is a *separate* mapper, not a blanket change. New `proc_map_page_nx` (NX clone of `proc_map_page`) backs the **low** arena (`sys_mmap`'s `[256 MB, 1 GB)` path); `proc_map_page_hi` now sets NX for the **high** arena. ELF code/stack stay on `proc_map_page` (`0x87`, executable). **Required ripple:** every site that extracts phys from an arena PDE switched from `& 0xFFFFFFFFFFE00000` (which includes bit 63!) to **`& 0x000FFFFFFFE00000`** (bits 21‚Äì51 only) ‚Äî `proc_free_address_space` (low + high sweeps), `sys_munmap` (low), `proc_unmap_2mb_hi`, and `proc_copy_from_user` (reads any user page, e.g. an env pointer into the NX arena). Missing one would `pmm_free`/read a corrupt (NX-bit-set) address. Verified: `mmap-himem: chain PASS` now also **asserts NX bit 63 set** (W^X), and the e2e/himunmap **free-count restorations** prove the bit-63-safe mask frees NX pages correctly. No regression: `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` PASS (its heap is now NX, runs as data), **`exec-smoke` 15/15** (multi exec+exit+reap ‚Üí `proc_free_address_space` low sweep over `0x87` ELF pages + the new mask, e2fsck clean). **Remaining W^X surface (follow-on):** ELF *data*/*stack* segments still map `0x87` (executable) ‚Äî the loader maps segments uniformly; a PF_X-aware loader would NX non-code segments (bigger change). The mmap arena ‚Äî the classic heap-spray target ‚Äî is covered.

## [1.50.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.4 ‚Äî per-process high-arena cursor (mmap-arena follow-on #2).** The high mmap arena's bump cursor was a single global `himmap_next_vaddr`; it is now **per-process** ‚Äî `proc_himmap_next[16]` indexed by pid (mirrors `proc_rsp0`, `0 = uninit ‚Üí 128 GB base`), reserved via a new `himmap_reserve(pid, len)` helper. Each address space already maps its high VAs in its own CR3, so giving each process its own `[128 GB, 512 GB)` cursor means a long-lived multi-process system never globally exhausts the arena the way one shared monotonic cursor would. `sys_mmap` reserves the whole range from the caller's cursor; `sys_munmap`'s LIFO reclaim rewinds the caller's own cursor. `himmap_reserve` guards bad pids (`<0`/`‚â•16`) and the 512 GB ceiling. Verified by `MMAP_HIMEM_PERPROC_SELFTEST` (`mmap-himem-perproc: PASS`): two scratch pids both reserve from 128 GB independently, a near-ceiling reserve returns 0 (exhaustion), bad pid returns 0. The e2e + himunmap selftests still pass (`>1GB map+free PASS`, `mmap-himunmap: PASS`). No regression: `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` PASS.

## [1.50.3] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.3 ‚Äî high-range `munmap` (mmap-arena follow-on #1).** `sys_munmap` now handles the high arena `[128 GB, 512 GB)`, not just the low arena. Where the low arena shares one 0‚Äì1 GB PD (a single walk), each high page may sit in a different per-proc PDPT entry, so the high path unmaps **per-page** via a new `proc_unmap_2mb_hi(cr3, virt)` (walk PML4‚ÜíPDPT[hi]‚ÜíPD; if the PDE is present+user, clear it, flush the TLB, free the 2 MB phys; user-bit guard so a kernel/absent entry is never freed; idempotent). LIFO cursor reclaim rewinds `himmap_next_vaddr` when the freed range was the most-recent map. The PD page is left allocated (it may hold siblings; `proc_free_address_space` reclaims an emptied PD at exit ‚Äî bounded at ‚â§384 √ó 4 KB). Previously a high `munmap` returned -1 (pages reclaimed only at process exit); now it frees eagerly. Verified by `MMAP_HIMUNMAP_SELFTEST` (`mmap-himunmap: PASS`): maps 4 high pages, unmaps the middle 2 (free-count +2, those PDEs cleared, neighbors kept), re-unmap is idempotent, teardown restores the count. No regression: `check.sh` 11/11, `mmap-smoke` 2/2 (low pmm/rounding/munmap), `agnsh-smoke` PASS. The low-arena `munmap` path is byte-preserved (relocated into a branch).

## [1.50.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.2 ‚Äî opens the user mmap-arena full lift (let one process mmap many GB, up to machine RAM).** The anonymous mmap arena is `[256 MB, ~1 GB)` in the per-process 0‚Äì1 GB PD ‚Äî so a single process tops out at ~768 MB of heap regardless of the 62 GB machine. The full lift (user's call) adds a **high-VA arena backed by the direct-map**, placed at PDPT[128..511] (‚â•128 GB, within PML4[0]) ‚Äî chosen ABOVE the ‚â§64 GB direct-map (PDPT[8..71]) and clear of every device BAR + the framebuffer (all PDPT[0..3]), so a present entry there is unambiguously a per-process arena PD (never a shared kernel entry ‚Äî which is what lets teardown free it safely). Plan + bite breakdown: [`docs/development/planning/mmap-arena-full-lift.md`](docs/development/planning/mmap-arena-full-lift.md).

### Added
- **`proc_map_page_hi(cr3, virt, phys)` ‚Äî high user-arena page mapper (bite 1a; `kernel/core/proc.cyr`).** Mirrors `proc_map_page` but for VA ‚â• 128 GB: `proc_map_page` only ever walks PDPT[0]'s 0‚Äì1 GB PD, whereas a high VA needs its own per-process PDPT entry + PD, neither of which exists until first map. On a miss it allocates a zero-filled PD (`pmm_alloc`, 4 KB, ‚â§256 MB identity) and installs it user (`0x07`), then writes the 2 MB user PDE (`0x87`); a sibling map into the same 1 GB reuses the PD. **Dormant** ‚Äî not yet wired into `sys_mmap`, so the live kernel is byte-unaffected. Verified hermetically by `MMAP_HIMEM_SELFTEST` (`mmap-himem: chain PASS` at `-m 2G`): builds a throwaway address space, maps two sibling 128 GB VAs, and walks PML4‚ÜíPDPT‚ÜíPD to assert the on-demand PDPT entry is present+user, the sibling reuses the same PD, and each PDE maps the correct phys with user+2 MB bits. Production (`check.sh` 11/11) unaffected. Iron-validation of the live path is bite 1c (a burn with a >1 GB userland workload).
- **High mmap arena wired live + teardown (bite 1b; `kernel/core/proc.cyr`).** `sys_mmap` no longer rejects `length > 1 GB`: small allocs use the proven low arena `[256 MB, ~1 GB)`; a request that doesn't fit there (big, or the low cursor exhausted) **spills to the high arena** `[128 GB, 512 GB)` via `proc_map_page_hi` + a `himmap_next_vaddr` cursor (zero-filled through the direct-map handle, same as the low path). `proc_free_address_space` now also sweeps per-proc **PDPT[128..511]**, freeing each present+user arena PD's 2 MB pages then the PD page ‚Äî so a process that uses the high arena doesn't leak its pages on exit (the kernel never uses that PDPT range, so a present+user entry there is unambiguously per-process). A single sanity cap (`length ‚â§ 64 GB`) blocks both absurd requests and the round-up overflow. **`MMAP_HIMEM_E2E_SELFTEST` (`mmap-himem-e2e: >1GB map+free PASS` at `-m 4G`):** maps a **1.026 GB contiguous** region (513 √ó 2 MB) spanning PDPT[128]+[129] (on-demand PD across a boundary), asserts the free-count dropped by exactly 513, then tears the AS down and asserts the count is **fully restored** (no leak). No regression: `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` (low path) PASS, `agnsh-hiram-smoke` `-m 8G` (large-RAM low path) PASS. **munmap of a high region returns -1 for now** (the pages are reclaimed at exit by the teardown above); high-range `munmap` + a per-proc cursor are follow-ons.

## [1.50.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-29

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.1 ‚Äî promote the boot-CR3 ‚Üí own-PML4 switch to DEFAULT-ON (iron-validated).** The 1.50.0 switch (gated `BOOTCR3_OWN_PML4`, off by default) passed its iron burn on real 64 GB Zen 2026-06-29: the framebuffer rendered the *entire* post-`dev_init` boot to the agnsh prompt (no freeze ‚Äî the `vmm_remap_uc_2mb` guard fix held on real silicon while NVMe/AHCI/xHCI/net all UC-remapped into the FB's shared PDPT[3] 1 GB entry), the **keyboard worked** (`iam` typed + ran ‚Üí `Memory: 61 GiB`), and ~62 GB of RAM was online (`kybernet: 16177229 free pages`) under the switch, with SMP-4 / DHCP lease / ext2+jbd2+FAT all green. So the kernel now runs boot/idle on its **own** complete PML4 (`0x1000`) by default rather than gnoboot's transient boot CR3.

### Changed
- **Boot-CR3 ‚Üí own-PML4 switch is now DEFAULT-ON.** The gate inverted from opt-in `#ifdef BOOTCR3_OWN_PML4` to opt-**out** `#ifndef BOOTCR3_KEEP_GNOBOOT_CR3` (`kernel/core/main.cyr`); a plain `./scripts/build.sh` now boots on `0x1000`. **Opt-out escape hatch:** `BOOTCR3_KEEP_GNOBOOT_CR3=1 ./scripts/build.sh` reverts to running boot/idle on gnoboot's transient boot CR3, for bringing up a future hardware target that regresses. This realizes the deliverable the 1.49.9 directmap misdiagnosis pointed at: the boot/idle context is now consistent with the per-proc CR3s (which already copy `0x1000`'s `PDPT[1..511]`) and reaches >256 MB phys directly. Validated: default build prints `kernel CR3: own PML4 (0x1000)` and boots to `[ASSIST]` at `-m 8G`; the opt-out build omits the switch and still boots; `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` PASS. **Soak:** a second clean iron burn of the default-on kernel gates the *next* RAM items (user mmap-arena) per the user.

## [1.50.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-28

**‚ñ∂ 1.50.0 ‚Äî opens the RAM full-usage continuation arc.** The 1.49.x full-RAM arc is **iron-validated + CLOSED**: the re-burn of 1.49.12 boots to the agnsh prompt on real 64 GB Zen with >256 MB RAM online (the dynamic RAM-backed PMM bitmap + the >256 MB direct-map, after the 1.49.12 raw-phys fix). That made RAM *allocatable + direct-map-reachable*; this arc makes it *fully usable* end-to-end. Two walls remain, in different layers:
- **User side** ‚Äî a per-process mmap arena bounded to ~1 GB (one PD page, `[0x10000000, 0x40000000)`), so a single process can't use more than ~1 GB of heap regardless of machine RAM.
- **Kernel side** ‚Äî `pmm_alloc` (4 KB: page tables, DMA rings, kernel buffers) capped at the 256 MB identity window, because the kernel still runs on gnoboot's *transient* boot CR3 and can't reach a >256 MB 4 KB page there. Lifting this is gated on the **boot-CR3 ‚Üí own complete PML4** cleanup (the kernel running on its own `0x1000` tables, which also closes the "boot CR3 can't see kernel VM edits" class that caused the 1.49.9 misdiagnosis).
- **Edge** ‚Äî the single 2 MB PMM bitmap caps coverage at 64 GB; >64 GB needs a multi-region contiguous bitmap (the 64 GB dev box fits, so this is deferred until bigger hardware).

Bite plan (user 2026-06-28): **1.50.0 = full-RAM stress (de-risk) ‚Üí boot-CR3 own-PML4 foundation; 1.50.1 = user mmap arena expansion.** Decade-map: `1.50 RAM continuation` ([roadmap](docs/development/roadmap.md)). Tracker ‚Üí agnosticos iron-log `#tracker-150x-cycle`.

### Added
- **Full-RAM stress self-test (`PMM_RAMSTRESS_SELFTEST`, `kernel/core/pmm.cyr` `pmm_ramstress_run`).** Allocates up to 16384 2 MB regions (‚â§32 GB) across the machine RAM, writes a per-region sentinel at the start + end of each through the direct-map (under CR3 0x1000), reads both back + verifies, frees all. Where the 1.49.12 fix proved *one* >256 MB region, this exercises *thousands* across many GB ‚Äî catching per-region mis-mapping, overlap, and reservation bugs at scale. Reports `ramstress: regions=N (~G GB) bad=B firstbad_phys=‚Ä¶`.
- **Boot-CR3 ‚Üí own complete PML4 switch (`BOOTCR3_OWN_PML4`, `kernel/core/main.cyr`).** The kernel now switches off gnoboot's *transient* boot CR3 onto its OWN permanent PML4 (`0x1000`, built by `pt_init` + carrying the direct-map installed by `pmm_setup_directmap`) ‚Äî `cr3_load(0x1000)` placed **late** in boot, right after the post-pmm FB WC remap and before `dev_init`. The late placement is load-bearing: by that point every gnoboot-CR3-relative access is already satisfied ‚Äî the UEFI memmap (near the top of RAM, e.g. 5.4 GB @ -m 8G) was read only by `pmm_probe/extend/migrate/setup_directmap` under gnoboot's full-identity CR3; `boot_info` is already copied into the low `boot_info_copy` (`boot_info_capture_rdi`, mbi.cyr); the framebuffer was just (re)mapped **WC** (the prior early-switch attempt left the FB as a 1 GB **WB** huge page under `0x1000`, so writes batched in cache and never reached the display ‚Äî looked like a "freeze at Page tables: 1024MB mapped" but serial showed the full boot); and `dev_init` runs *after*, so it maps every device BAR into the now-active `0x1000` tree. This makes the boot/idle context consistent with the per-proc CR3s (which already copy `0x1000`'s `PDPT[1..511]`) and closes the "boot CR3 can't see kernel VM edits" class that caused the 1.49.9 directmap misdiagnosis. Gated `BOOTCR3_OWN_PML4` (off by default pending an iron-promotion burn). **QEMU-validated at `-m 8G`** (q35, KVM, `-smp 4`, NVMe): serial prints `kernel CR3: own PML4 (0x1000)`, boots clean to the `[ASSIST]` prompt, and a QMP screendump confirms the framebuffer renders the *entire* post-switch boot (NVMe/AHCI/GPT/VFS/ext2/heap/scheduler/kybernet/agnsh) ‚Äî device init, FS, and the shell all run on `0x1000`. **Depends on the `vmm_remap_uc_2mb` guard fix below** ‚Äî without it the switch freezes the FB on iron (not on QEMU). (The switch itself was first validated against a stale disk image that masked it; re-validated after forcing a fresh kernel stage ‚Äî see the agnosticos iron-log.)
- **PDPT-guard self-test (`PDPT_GUARD_SELFTEST`, `kernel/core/vmm.cyr` `pdpt_guard_selftest`).** Forces the shared-1 GB-PDPT-entry cache-type collision that the iron FB + device BARs hit but QEMU cannot (QEMU's FB is PDPT[2], its lone NVMe PDPT[3]): WC a chunk, then UC its sibling in the same PDPT entry (synthetic PDPT[4], 4‚Äì5 GB, restored after), and assert the WC chunk survived. Pre-fix prints `pdpt-guard FAIL (WC clobbered to WB)`; post-fix `pdpt-guard PASS`. Reproduces the FB-clobber regression on the dev box at zero iron cost.

### Fixed
- **`vmm_remap_uc_2mb` was missing the idempotency guard its WC twin has ‚Üí re-shattering a shared PDPT entry clobbered sibling chunks to WB (FB-freeze on iron under the boot-CR3 switch).** `vmm_remap_wc_2mb` (since the 1.30.11 FB work) reuses an already-shattered PD and edits only the target PDE; `vmm_remap_uc_2mb` (used for device-BAR MMIO) **unconditionally allocated a fresh PD and rebuilt all 512 PDEs as WB**, overriding only its own chunk to UC. On real archaemenid the GOP framebuffer (`~0xd0000000`, PDPT[3]) shares ONE 1 GB PDPT entry with **every** device BAR (NVMe/AHCI/xHCI/r8169/IOMMU, all in `0xC‚Ä¶‚Äì0xF‚Ä¶`), so once the kernel runs on its own PML4 (`BOOTCR3_OWN_PML4`), the first device's UC remap **after** the FB WC remap reverts the FB 2 MB chunk to WB ‚Üí frozen display (the box has no serial, so a frozen FB reads as a hang and a wasted burn), plus a leaked PD per call. QEMU cannot surface it (FB in PDPT[2], lone NVMe in PDPT[3] ‚Äî never the same 1 GB entry). Surfaced by the 1.50.0 pre-burn adversarial review; fix mirrors the WC guard (reuse the shattered PD, edit only the target PDE to `0x9B`). Proven FAIL‚ÜíPASS by `PDPT_GUARD_SELFTEST` at `-m 8G`; production (gate off) unregressed ‚Äî `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` PASS.
- **PMM bitmap migration left memmap GAPS free above 256 MB ‚Üí `pmm_alloc_2mb` handed out unbacked regions (memory-corruption latent).** The 1.49.11 `pmm_migrate_bitmap` reserved non-conventional memmap *entries* but not *gaps* ‚Äî an address range simply ABSENT from the UEFI memmap (e.g. the **2‚Äì4 GB PCI hole**) is not a reserved entry, so it stayed free and `pmm_alloc_2mb` could return a 2 MB region there; the kernel's direct-map access to that region then hit MMIO/unbacked phys. Invisible to the agnsh/exec smokes (their allocations stay low), it surfaced **immediately** under the new full-RAM stress at `-m 32G`: `bad=1770`, `firstbad_phys=0x81000000` (the 2 GB hole). Fix: above 256 MB the migration now **reserves by default and frees only `EfiConventionalMemory` (type 7) RAM** (byte-`memset` the 8-page-aligned middle of each region, bit-clear the edges), so gaps *and* reserved entries are excluded uniformly. Re-stress at `-m 32G`: `regions=16354 (~32 GB) bad=0`. (Known cosmetic follow-on: `free_count` still counts reserved gaps toward `pmm_total`, so the reported free pages over-count by the hole size ‚Äî same approximate accounting as below 256 MB. Perf follow-on: `pmm_alloc_2mb` scans bottom-up with no next-free hint, so bulk allocation is O(n¬≤) region-checks.) No regression: `agnsh-smoke`, `mmap-smoke` 2/2, `ram-probe-smoke`, `check.sh` 11/11; agnsh still launches at `-m 32G`.

## [1.49.12] ‚Äî 2026-06-28

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.12 ‚Äî iron-burn fix: the 1.49.11 >256 MB RAM extension exposed three raw-physical-address access sites that #PF-halted once allocations crossed 256 MB. The 64 GB iron burn locked up launching agnoshi; root-caused + fixed + reproduced/verified in QEMU.**

### Fixed
- **>256 MB raw-physical-address access in `sys_mmap` + `elf_load` (`kernel/core/proc.cyr`, `kernel/core/elf.cyr`) ‚Äî the agnsh-launch lockup on the 64 GB iron burn.** 1.49.9 introduced `pmm_kva_for_access` (identity ‚â§256 MB / direct-map above) and converted `elf_load_from_file`, but **three sibling sites kept accessing freshly-allocated 2 MB pages via their raw physical address as an identity VA**: `sys_mmap`'s zero-fill (`memset(phys, 0, 2 MB)`), and `elf_load`'s segment copy (`memset`/`memcpy(phys, ‚Ä¶)`) + stack `vmm_map(phys, phys)`. While `pmm_alloc_2mb` was capped at 256 MB these were always identity-mapped, so they were latent. **1.49.11 lifted the cap to the full machine RAM** ‚Äî so a >256 MB region now reaches those sites and the raw-phys access #PF's at a multi-GB linear address (identity only covers ‚â§256 MB) ‚Üí the fault handler kpanic-halts (`HLT`, `IF=0`). On the 64 GB iron burn the kernel booted fully (RAM migration ‚úÖ `16177224 free pages`, SMP ‚úÖ `cpus online: 4`, net ‚úÖ, FS ‚úÖ) then locked up right after `kybernet: exec /bin/agnsh` ‚Äî agnsh loads via the already-correct `elf_load_from_file`, enters ring 3, and its **first heap `mmap` hits `sys_mmap`**. Fix: route all three sites through `pmm_kva_for_access` (the user PTE still gets the raw `phys` via `proc_map_page`; only the kernel's transient zero/copy access uses the handle). **Reproduced in QEMU at `-m 8G` (hung, RIP in the halt loop, `CR2` ~6.2 GB, 2 MB op) and verified fixed at `-m 8G` and `-m 32G` (both reach the agnsh prompt); `-m 512M` baseline unaffected.** No regression: `agnsh-smoke`, `mmap-smoke` 2/2, `exec-smoke`, `ram-probe-smoke`, `check.sh` 11/11.

### Fixed (docs-cycle, folded in)
- **Stale `munmap: pmm-reuse` selftest (`kernel/core/selftests.cyr`).** Its out-of-range guard test freed `pmm_free_2mb(0x1000000)` (16 MB) expecting a "‚â• pool" rejection, but the 2 MB allocator's pool grew from 16 MB to 256 MB+ (and now full RAM, 1.49.11) ‚Äî so 16 MB is IN-pool and the call no longer rejects, failing the test. Changed to `0x1000000000` (64 GB), which is above the bitmap's 64 GB cap and always rejects. `mmap-smoke` is green again (2/2). (Investigation also confirmed `exec-smoke`'s apparent failure was a build-flag mistake ‚Äî it needs `EXEC_SELFTEST=1 EXT2_WRITE_SELFTEST=1`; it passes cleanly when built correctly. Neither was a regression.)

### Docs
- **Purged the stale ">256 MB blocked by a cyrius high-VA limit" framing** left over from 1.49.9, now that 1.49.10 root-caused it as a false-positive boot probe and 1.49.11 shipped >256 MB RAM. Updated `kernel/core/pmm.cyr` (the module header + the `pmm_fullram_selftest` comment) and `docs/development/roadmap.md` (the 1.49.x "Full RAM initialization" capability-gap slot + the active-arc and decade-map pointer lines now mark it ‚úÖ shipped 1.49.11). The direct-map works under the kernel / per-proc CR3; cyrius is exonerated.

## [1.49.11] ‚Äî 2026-06-28

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.11 ‚Äî full-RAM extension, bite 3c: >256 MB RAM comes online. Dynamic RAM-backed PMM bitmap + the 2 MB user-page allocator lifted to the full machine RAM, now that 1.49.10 proved the direct-map works.**

### Added
- **Dynamic RAM-backed PMM bitmap (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr` `pmm_migrate_bitmap` + `pmm_bitmap_ptr`/`pmm_bitmap_pages`).** The static 8 KB `pmm_bitmap` is now only a **bootstrap** (256 MB) ‚Äî it breaks the allocator-needs-a-bitmap-to-allocate-the-bitmap chicken-and-egg. After the RAM probe, if RAM > 256 MB, `pmm_migrate_bitmap` allocates one 2 MB region (`pmm_alloc_2mb`, ‚â§256 MB so it stays identity-reachable under every CR3), copies the 0‚Äì256 MB reserved/used state into it, reserves non-conventional memmap regions in (256 MB, ram_top], switches `pmm_bitmap_ptr`, and lifts `pmm_bitmap_pages`/`pmm_total`/`pmm_2mb_top_region` to the full RAM. One 2 MB bitmap covers **64 GB** (RAM beyond that is capped + logged, not silent ‚Äî a multi-region bitmap is the follow-on). This is the storage approach chosen over a static `var[N]` array, which would inflate the kernel binary past its size budget. All bitmap access (set/clear/test, alloc/free, init, extend) was routed through the pointer first (behavior-neutral indirection), then the migration swapped it.
- **`PMM_HIRAM_SELFTEST` (`pmm_hiram_selftest`) + `scripts/`-side QEMU validation.** Allocates 2 MB regions bottom-up until one lands at/above the 256 MB identity ceiling, round-trips it through the direct-map under the kernel CR3 (`dm_roundtrip_kernel_cr3`), then frees all. Verified in QEMU `-m 1024M`: `PMM: 2mb_top_region=511` (was 128), `HIRAM: allocs=120 hi=10000000` (region at phys 256 MB), `HIRAM: rd@hi=f00dcafe` (round-trip through `DIRECTMAP_BASE + 256 MB`), `HIRAM: >256MB alloc+directmap OK`, boots to shell.

### Changed
- **`pmm_alloc_2mb`'s 256 MB cap (`pmm_2mb_top_region`) is lifted to the full RAM after migration.** User 2 MB pages (ELF code / stack / mmap heap) now reach above the 256 MB identity ceiling; the kernel zeroes/loads them via `DIRECTMAP_BASE + phys` (`pmm_kva_for_access`) under the caller's per-proc CR3, which carries the direct-map. The 4 KB allocator (`pmm_alloc`, kernel page tables / DMA) **deliberately stays** in the 256 MB identity window ‚Äî only direct-map-reachable user pages go higher. ‚â§256 MB RAM keeps the bootstrap bitmap unchanged (`directmap: installed`).
- **Regression scope checked:** `ram-probe-smoke` (4/4, incl. the corrected >256 MB round-trip), `pmm-fullram-smoke` (4/4), and `agnsh-smoke` (boot-to-shell) all pass with and without the feature. `exec-smoke` and `mmap-smoke`'s `munmap: pmm-reuse` fail on this box, but they **fail identically on the 1.49.9 baseline** (pre-existing, unrelated to this change).

## [1.49.10] ‚Äî 2026-06-28

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.10 ‚Äî full-RAM extension, bite 3c: the >256 MB direct-map WORKS ‚Äî 1.49.9's "cyrius high-VA limit" was a misdiagnosis. The blocker was a false-positive boot probe running under the wrong page tables. cyrius fully exonerated.**

### Fixed
- **Root-caused the >256 MB direct-map "failure" ‚Äî it was never broken, and never a cyrius bug.** The boot-time probe runs under **gnoboot's transient boot CR3 (`0x3fc01000`), not the kernel's PML4 (`0x1000`)** where the direct-map is installed. gnoboot's `PDPT[8] = 0x2000000e3` is a **1 GB identity huge page ‚Üí phys 8 GB** (unbacked at <8 GB RAM ‚Üí reads 0, drops writes, *no fault* because it's a present mapping) ‚Äî so every >256 MB direct-map access at boot hit that phantom page. Under **CR3 `0x1000`** the identical `store64`/`load64` to `DIRECTMAP_BASE + 320 MB` (`0x214000000`) round-trips perfectly (verified `0xcafebabe` in QEMU `-m 1024M`). The 1.49.9 `directmap: low+hi OK` probe was a **two-zeros false match** (both the direct-map read and the identity read returned 0 under the boot CR3), which is what got mis-attributed to a cyrius load/store truncation. The direct-map is correct and reachable in every runtime context: kernel threads run at CR3 `0x1000`, and every per-proc CR3 copies `PDPT[1..511]` from `0x2000` (`proc.cyr`), inheriting `PDPT[8]`. gnoboot's CR3 is never captured or reloaded after the first context switch, and touches no >256 MB page. Empirical A/B/C/D/E walk + full analysis: `docs/development/issue/2026-06-28-cyrius-high-va-load-store.md`.
- **Replaced the false-positive direct-map boot probe (`kernel/core/main.cyr`).** It now round-trips a true >256 MB VA (phys 320 MB, low bits non-aliasing) **under the kernel CR3 `0x1000`** ‚Äî the context the direct-map's consumers actually run in ‚Äî then restores the live CR3. Gated on `RAM > 320 MB && RSP < 256 MB` so the brief CR3 switch is always stack-safe (the boot stack is identity-mapped <256 MB under `0x1000`); otherwise a structural "`PDPT[8]` installed" check. New boot lines: `directmap: >256MB OK (kernel CR3)` (QEMU `-m 1024M`) / `directmap: installed` (‚â§320 MB). Helpers added in `vmm.cyr`: `dm_read_cr3`, `dm_read_rsp`, `dm_roundtrip_kernel_cr3`.

### Changed
- **The >256 MB RAM extension is unblocked.** It needs only mechanical work, **no VM redesign**: lift `pmm_alloc_2mb`'s 256 MB cap (`pmm_2mb_top_region`) to the real RAM ceiling, and re-grow the 4 KB PMM bitmap past 256 MB. The bitmap re-grow needs a storage decision (the prior 4 GB bitmap was a *static* array that blew the ~1.4 MB kernel-size budget ‚Äî candidates: BSS-backed, on-demand 2-level, or 2 MB-granularity-only tracking above the identity ceiling). `pmm_alloc_2mb` held at 256 MB this bite; the direct-map fix is the prerequisite, now done.

## [1.49.9] ‚Äî 2026-06-28

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.9 ‚Äî full-RAM extension, bite 3b: the >256 MB path is blocked by a cyrius high-VA limit; held at 256 MB with the groundwork in place.**

### Discovered
- **The 1.49.7 kernel direct-map does NOT work above 256 MB ‚Äî bite 2's validation was a false positive.** Building the >256 MB user-page path surfaced it: `pmm_alloc_2mb` reaches >256 MB fine and the direct-map page-table chain is correct (read back live: `PD[160]=0x14000083` ‚Üí phys 320 MB), but a `store64`/`load64` to `DIRECTMAP_BASE (8 GB) + 320 MB` returns 0 **with no fault** ‚Äî the MMU mapping is right, yet the access never reaches the mapped page. Bite 2's `directmap: low+hi OK` probe only tested ‚â§100 MB, where the 8 GB VA's low bits **alias the live identity map**, so it read the identity and passed regardless. The strong inference: **cyrius `load64`/`store64` don't address a ‚â•4 GB virtual address correctly** (they appear to operate on the low bits). Filed: `docs/development/issue/2026-06-28-cyrius-high-va-load-store.md`.

### Changed
- **`pmm_alloc_2mb` held at the 256 MB ceiling** (`pmm_2mb_top_region = 128`) ‚Äî user RAM stays at the working 256 MB identity window (1.49.8 behavior), since the kernel can't touch a >256 MB page during ELF load without the (cyrius-blocked) 8 GB direct-map. Verified: pmm-fullram 4/4 + exec-smoke PASS at 256 MB; `check.sh`/fmt clean.
- **Groundwork retained for when cyrius ‚â•4 GB addressing lands**: `pmm_kva_for_access` (identity ‚â§256 MB / direct-map above) + the `elf.cyr` access-handle split (`phys`/`kva`, `stack_phys`/`stack_kva`). The PMM bitmap stays at **256 MB** ‚Äî cyrius stores the array static (not BSS), so the 4 GB version blew the 1.4 MB kernel-size budget (1.47 MB); re-growing it (`var[16384]` + the validity/free bounds) + lifting the `pmm_2mb_top_region` cap then brings >256 MB online with **no new VM design**.
- **Next**: the cyrius ‚â•4 GB load/store fix (the proper path to the box's full 60 GB), or ‚Äî if it's needed sooner ‚Äî a ‚â§4 GB kmap window (caps at ~3 GB).

## [1.49.8] ‚Äî 2026-06-28

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.8 ‚Äî full-RAM extension, bite 3a: user pages reach the full 256 MB (the user-facing half of 1.49.6).**

### Changed
- **`pmm_alloc_2mb` (the 2 MB user-page allocator) now serves regions 1..127 = 2‚Äì256 MB, not the hardcoded 2‚Äì128 MB (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr`).** 1.49.6 grew the bitmap + per-proc identity to 256 MB, but `pmm_alloc_2mb` was still capped at region 63 (128 MB) ‚Äî so user processes (ELF code / stack / mmap heap, all from this allocator) stayed capped at 128 MB despite the larger bitmap. Lifted to a `pmm_2mb_top_region` (= 128 regions = 256 MB); `pmm_count_2mb_free` (the mmap pre-check) tracks the same ceiling, and `pmm_free_2mb`'s 128 MB bound (`base >= 32768`) ‚Üí 256 MB (`>= 65536`) so the new regions free cleanly. **No direct-map needed here**: 256 MB is identity-mapped (the per-proc PD[8..127] re-assert + `elf.cyr`'s on-demand `vmm_map(phys,phys)`, which covers 0‚Äì1 GB), so the kernel reaches every user page during ELF load via the existing path. Validated: **pmm-fullram-smoke** `PMM 2mb: >128MB OK` (**102 regions allocated, max `0xda00000` ‚âà 218 MB** at `-m 256M`) + boot-to-shell (new smoke gate); **exec-smoke PASS non-PIE AND PIE**; `check.sh` 11/11.
- **Next (1.49.9): bite 3b ‚Äî the >256 MB extension.** Grow the bitmap past 256 MB and route the kernel's user-page access (ELF load, stack, argv) through `DIRECTMAP_BASE + phys` (the 1.49.7 direct-map) instead of the identity map ‚Äî above 256 MB the identity collides with the user mmap arena, which is exactly what the direct-map was built to sidestep.

## [1.49.7] ‚Äî 2026-06-28

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.7 ‚Äî full-RAM extension, bite 2: the kernel direct-map (the access path past the 256 MB identity ceiling).**

### Added
- **A kernel direct-map of physical RAM (`kernel/core/vmm.cyr` `pmm_setup_directmap` + `pmm.cyr` `pmm_memmap_ram_top` + `main.cyr`).** 256 MB (1.49.6) is the per-proc *identity* limit ‚Äî beyond it the kernel's identity VA collides with the user mmap arena (256 MB‚Äì1 GB). The direct-map decouples kernel RAM access from the low-half user VAs: all physical RAM is mapped at **`DIRECTMAP_BASE (8 GB) + phys`**, as 2 MB WB supervisor pages, in the *existing* kernel PDPT @ 0x2000 (PDPT[8..511] = 504 GB of reach). The per-proc CR3 mirrors kernel PDPT[1..511], so the direct-map propagates to **every CR3** ‚Äî the kernel can reach any physical page via `DIRECTMAP_BASE + phys` during any syscall, regardless of the mmap arena. `pmm_memmap_ram_top` (the conventional-RAM top, **not** the MMIO-inflated memmap top, which is TB-range from 64-bit BARs) bounds the mapping.
- **Placement rationale (hard-won; three attempts).** The conventional spot is the canonical HIGH half (`0xFFFF8...`), but that VA has **bit 63 set = a negative i64**, and cyrius's signed `load64`/`store64` faulted on it *despite a verified-correct page-table chain*. A 16 TB low-half base (PML4[32], a **new PML4 entry**) also faulted ‚Äî large-address codegen and/or a new-PML4-entry paging-structure-cache subtlety the CR3 reload doesn't clear. The working placement: **8 GB ‚Äî a GB-range address in the existing PDPT @ 0x2000**, the exact pattern `apic.cyr` uses for the LAPIC (PDPT[3]) and proven to work.
- Validated: **ram-probe-smoke** `directmap: low+hi OK` (phys 0 + 100 MB read through the direct-map == the identity map) + boot-to-shell (new gate (3)); **exec-smoke PASS non-PIE AND PIE** (the direct-map rides the per-proc CR3 through proc creation + exec-from-disk); pmm-fullram 3/3; `check.sh` clean.
- **Next (1.49.8+): switch the PMM/heap to return `DIRECTMAP_BASE + phys` pointers + grow the bitmap to `pmm_memmap_ram_top`.** That's bite 3 ‚Äî where RAM above 256 MB actually comes online (the direct-map is the *access path*; bite 3 makes the allocator hand it out).

## [1.49.6] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.6 ‚Äî full-RAM extension, bite 1: 128 ‚Üí 256 MB (the per-proc identity's full window).**

### Changed
- **The PMM now manages up to 256 MB instead of 128 MB (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr` + `proc.cyr`).** The blocker was never the bitmap ‚Äî it's the per-proc CR3, which reaches PMM pages only via their identity VA during a syscall. `proc_create_address_space` re-asserted the per-proc identity for PD[8..63] (16‚Äì128 MB); it now re-asserts **PD[8..127] (16‚Äì256 MB)** as supervisor identity ‚Äî the full low window before the user mmap arena (which starts at 256 MB), so no VA conflict. The PMM bitmap grows 512‚Üí**1024 u64** (256 MB = 65536 pages), and every size-dependent constant tracks it (validity bound 65536, alloc-cap clamp 65535, `pmm_total`, and the kernel-image reservation now covers the full KASLR range within the bitmap). **Result: ~198 MB of usable 4 KB pages** (50877 free above 16 MB at `-m 256M`, up from ~104 MB). Validated: **pmm-fullram-smoke** alloc_top=65535 (256 MB) + boot-to-shell; **exec-smoke PASS non-PIE AND PIE** (proc creation + exec-from-disk + e2fsck-clean; a KASLR'd kernel at 122 MB *inside* the bitmap was reservation-protected); `check.sh` 11/11. (`exec-smoke`'s sysi gate moved 73‚Üí81 ‚Äî `/bin/sysi` exits `'A' + (pmm_total*4096 >> 24)`, which the 256 MB pool shifts to 0x10 ‚Üí 81; kernel correct, the test just pinned the old size.)
- **Next (1.49.7+): the high-half kernel direct-map.** 256 MB is the ceiling of the identity design ‚Äî above it the kernel's identity VA collides with the user mmap arena (256 MB‚Äì1 GB). Mapping all physical RAM at a high kernel VA, shared across CR3s, with the PMM/heap returning high-half pointers, decouples kernel RAM access from the low-half user VAs ‚Üí the **full available RAM**. The foundational sub-arc that reaches "the available".

## [1.49.5] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.5 ‚Äî capability gap 3, socket-as-VFS-fd bite 2: VFS_SOCK fds are epoll-ready. Gap 3 COMPLETE.**

### Added
- **`epoll_wait` #21 now reports a VFS_SOCK fd as readable when its TCP conn has RX data or is dead (`kernel/core/syscall.cyr` + `net_tcp.cyr`).** A new **`tcp_readable(conn_id)`** probe ‚Äî `net_poll`, then `rx_write - rx_read > 0` (data buffered) OR conn-dead (EOF) ‚Äî backs an added VFS_SOCK arm in `epoll_wait`'s per-watch readiness loop, so a socket fd in an epoll set wakes `epoll_wait` with EPOLLIN exactly when a `read#5` would return data. Additive: the signalfd/timerfd readiness arms + the watch-fd bounds checks are byte-identical. This **completes the socket-as-VFS-fd keystone** ‚Äî an accepted TCP connection is now a uniform, **epoll-able** fd (`sock_accept`‚Üífd; `read`/`write`/`close`; and epoll), the event-loop primitive a real network service / container host needs. Validated: **loopback-smoke 5/5** ‚Äî over `lo`, the readiness flips **0‚Üí1 across the client send** (`rdy 0->1`), i.e. it reports not-ready until data arrives then ready, byte-exact read-back unchanged; `check.sh` 11/11. (`tcp_readable` ‚Äî the exact function the epoll arm calls ‚Äî is probed directly in the selftest; the full ring-3 `epoll_wait`-on-socket path needs a user events buffer unavailable at the boot selftest.) Gap 3 of the 1.49.x capability-gaps arc is done. **Still owed in 1.49.x: the >128 MB RAM extension** (bigger/relocated PMM bitmap + per-proc CR3 identity past 256 MB).

## [1.49.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.4 ‚Äî capability gap 3, socket-as-VFS-fd bite 1: accepted TCP connections are now fds.**

### Changed
- **`sock_accept` #57 now returns an epoll-able VFS_SOCK fd instead of a raw conn_id, and `read#5` / `write` / `close#6` dispatch uniformly to the TCP layer (`kernel/core/vfs.cyr` + `syscall.cyr`).** A new **`VFS_SOCK` (tag 10)** fd carries a TCP `conn_id` in `payload[0]`; `vfs_create_sock` wraps an accepted conn, and `vfs_read`/`vfs_write`/`vfs_close` route it to **`tcp_recv`/`tcp_send`/`tcp_close`**. So an accepted connection is a normal VFS handle ‚Äî the same `read`/`write`/`close` a file or pipe uses (and, next bite, epoll), the prerequisite for AGNOS to be a network service / container host. The legacy conn_id-based `sock_send`#48 / `sock_recv`#49 / `sock_close`#50 stay for raw-conn_id code. Validated: **loopback-smoke 4/4** ‚Äî over `lo`, `sock_accept` returns an fd, a client send is read back **through the fd** (`read#5` ‚Üí `tcp_recv`, 6 bytes byte-exact), and `close` tears the conn down; **exec-smoke PASS** (the file/pipe read/write/close arms unregressed); `check.sh` 11/11. **Bite 2 (next): epoll** ‚Äî make a VFS_SOCK fd readiness-reportable so `epoll_wait` fires when a socket is readable/writable (the keystone for an event-loop server).

## [1.49.3] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.3 ‚Äî full RAM init, bite 3: enable the PMM extension for PIE/KASLR kernels.**

### Changed
- **Removed the conservative 1.49.2 PIE auto-disable ‚Äî the 4 KB-allocator extension now applies to BOTH non-PIE and KASLR'd (PIE) kernels (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr`).** The 1.49.2 deferral was a **false alarm**: the apparent PIE boot failure was the full-RAM smoke's FAT-only ESP (no ext2 root, so kybernet can't `exec /bin/agnsh` ‚Äî non-PIE falls to the emergency shell, PIE doesn't), NOT the extension. With a proper ext2 root, a PIE kernel slid **inside** the bitmap (kernel_base=0x3a00000 = 58 MB) + the extension (alloc_top=32767) + the kernel-image reservation boots-to-shell and execs from disk **e2fsck-clean** ‚Äî top-down `pmm_alloc` correctly skips the reserved 16 MB kernel-image window. Validated: **exec-smoke full pass** under `CYRIUS_PIE=1` (58 MB base, in-bitmap ‚Üí reservation load-bearing); **kaslr-smoke PASS** with the extension live (bases 132/252 MB, above-bitmap ‚Üí slide varies + boots). So every KASLR'd kernel ‚Äî image inside OR above the 128 MB bitmap ‚Äî now gets the full ~104 MB extension safely. **>128 MB RAM** (a bigger/relocated bitmap) remains the one open follow-up.

## [1.49.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.2 ‚Äî full RAM init, bite 2: open the 4 KB allocator to the discovered RAM (non-PIE).**

### Changed
- **The PMM's 4 KB allocator now serves up to the full 128 MB bitmap instead of capping at 16 MB (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr`).** `pmm_alloc` scanned a hardcoded page-4095 (16 MB) top; it now scans `pmm_alloc_top`, which **`pmm_extend_to_memmap`** raises to the discovered memmap top, clamped to the 128 MB bitmap (page 32767). The extend is **additive** ‚Äî `pmm_init`'s proven 0‚Äì18 MB reservations are byte-identical; it only opens the new 18‚Äì128 MB window, reserving non-EfiConventionalMemory memmap regions (firmware/ACPI/MMIO holes) there first. **Result: ~104 MB of newly-usable 4 KB pages** (the selftest counts 26752 free pages above 16 MB at `-m 256M`). `pmm_alloc_2mb` (user 2 MB allocs) is unchanged ‚Äî still the low 2‚Äì16 MB. Validated: **pmm-fullram-smoke** (new) ‚Äî `alloc_top=32767`, two allocs land > 16 MB distinct + freed, boot-to-shell; **exec-smoke** full pass (boot-to-shell + exec-from-disk + envp + framebuffer + clock + **e2fsck-clean**) with the extension live; `check.sh` 11/11.
- **Auto-disabled for a KASLR'd (PIE) kernel (`kbase ‚â• 32 MB`), conservatively.** PIE boot-to-shell couldn't be cleanly confirmed *with* the extension ‚Äî the only full-RAM smoke uses a FAT-only ESP that fails PIE boot-to-shell even with the extension **disabled** (pre-existing; non-PIE falls to the emergency shell there, PIE doesn't). Production is non-PIE (`CYRIUS_PIE` is opt-in, only kaslr-smoke); the auto-disable makes the PIE path **byte-identical to before** ‚Äî **kaslr-smoke PASS** (slide live, unregressed). Enabling the extension for PIE (a proper PIE + ext2-root validation) and **>128 MB RAM** (a bigger/relocated bitmap) are follow-up bites.

## [1.49.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.1 ‚Äî full RAM initialization, bite 1: UEFI memory-map discovery.**

### Added
- **`pmm_probe_memmap` reads the UEFI memory map gnoboot passes in boot_info and reports the machine's actual RAM (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr` + an always-on boot log in `main.cyr`).** gnoboot already hands the kernel `memmap_phys`/`count`/`entsize` (boot_info `0x28`/`0x30`/`0x34`); the probe walks the `EFI_MEMORY_DESCRIPTOR` entries (type@0, phys_start@8, num_pages@24), summing **EfiConventionalMemory (type 7)** pages = free RAM and tracking the top-of-RAM phys address, then logs `RAM: usable=<N>MB top=<addr>`. **Discovery only ‚Äî no allocation change**: the PMM still allocates from its 4‚Äì16 MB pool (`pmm_alloc` scans pages 1024‚Äì4095 of the fixed 128 MB `pmm_bitmap`). The discovered values are stored (`pmm_memmap_usable` / `pmm_memmap_top`) for **bite 2**, which sizes the PMM to actual RAM. Validated: **ram-probe-smoke** (new) boots the same kernel at `-m 256M` ‚Üí **205 MB** and `-m 1024M` ‚Üí **973 MB**, asserting the reported RAM exceeds the old hardcoded 128 MB and scales with `-m`; `check.sh` 11/11. (`bi` is passed in, not read via `&boot_info_ptr`, to avoid the cross-file global-address parse-order trap.)

## [1.49.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.49.0 ‚Äî opens the kernel-capability-gaps arc.** The 1.47.x perf series and the 1.48.x FAT/exFAT review are closed; this arc turns to the real capability gaps surfaced 2026-06-27 ‚Äî the items that only became visible because the storage/net/FS foundation is now solid enough to expose them. Staged across 1.49.x‚Äì1.50.x ("plenty of numbers to use"):

- **(1) Full RAM initialization** ‚Äî `pmm_init` hardcodes `pmm_total=32768` and `pmm_alloc` caps at page 4095 (**16 MB**), ignoring the boot_info memmap; size the PMM to the machine's actual RAM (parse `memmap_phys`/`count`/`entsize`) + lift the alloc cap. Unblocks server/desktop workloads + the self-hosting "build agnos on agnos" goal. *(Foundational + self-contained ‚Äî the recommended first gap.)*
- **(2) Loopback interface** ‚Äî no `lo` / 127.0.0.1 today (the net stack is physical-NIC-only); add a loopback so a server + a local client on one box (and socket-IPC) work without the wire.
- **(3) Socket-as-VFS-fd bridge** ‚Äî accepted connections are `conn_id`s (the `sock_accept`#57 return), not VFS fds, so they aren't epoll-able; bridge them into the per-process fd table (a `VFS_SOCK` tag) so `epoll`/`read`/`write`/`close` work uniformly. Pairs with broader VFS-fd unification (may share this block or take its own minor).

**Staggering confirmed (user, 2026-06-27): loopback first.**

### Added
- **Loopback interface (`lo`) ‚Äî bite 1: the core mechanism + UDP (`kernel/core/net.cyr` + `net_ingress.cyr`).** Frames destined to **127.0.0.0/8 or our own `net_ip`** now loop back internally instead of hitting the wire: a new **`net_tx`** TX-chokepoint wrapper detects a loopback IP frame and **queues** it (an 8-slot ring) rather than calling `nic_send`; **`net_lo_drain`** (wired into `net_poll`, and runnable with **no physical NIC up**) drains the queue through **`net_demux_frame`** ‚Äî the ethertype dispatch extracted from `net_rx_drain` so a looped frame takes the *same* path as the NIC ring. Queuing (not synchronous inject) means a future TCP handshake's replies re-enter `net_tx` without recursion. `udp_send` is routed through `net_tx` (its `nic_ready` gate relaxed for loopback dsts). Validated: **loopback-smoke PASS** in QEMU ‚Äî a UDP datagram to 127.0.0.1 round-trips through the queue + demux into `net_udp_buf` with *no wire peer* (QEMU SLIRP never returns a 127.0.0.1 datagram, so the populated buffer proves the internal loop); **dns-smoke 3/3** (DHCP + DNS unregressed ‚Äî the demux refactor + `net_tx` are transparent to the NIC path); `check.sh` 11/11. New: `scripts/loopback-smoke.sh`, `LOOPBACK_SELFTEST` build gate.
- **Loopback interface (`lo`) ‚Äî bite 2: TCP + ICMP (`kernel/core/net_tcp.cyr` + `net_icmp.cyr`).** `tcp_send_pkt` (all TCP TX) and the ICMP echo-request/echo-reply paths now route through `net_tx`, so TCP and ICMP frames to 127.x / `net_ip` loop back too (`icmp_ping`'s `nic_ready` gate relaxed for loopback dsts). Targeting our own `net_ip` (self-loop) makes a reply's dst = the request's src = `net_ip`, which `net_is_loopback` also catches ‚Äî so **no source-address selection is needed** for replies to loop. Validated (extended loopback-smoke, **3/3**): a **full TCP handshake over `lo`** ‚Äî `tcp_connect(net_ip, 7777)` against `tcp_listen(7777)` completes SYN/SYN-ACK/ACK entirely through the loopback queue (confirming the no-recursion drain handles a multi-RTT handshake), plus an **ICMP self-ping** (echo request + reply both via the queue). No wire regression ‚Äî dns-smoke 3/3, icmp-smoke pass, and the TCP-listen kernel path accepted a real wire connection (`tcp_accept: conn_id=1`). *(Pre-existing, unrelated: `tcp-listen-smoke.sh` greps a stale string `"no connection within timeout"` ‚Äî the selftest emits `"tcp_listen smoke: done"` ‚Äî and its accept gate needs `nc` installed; neither is touched by this work.)*

## [1.48.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.48.2 ‚Äî other-FS perf review (3rd item, closes the set): FAT/exFAT multi-cluster read coalescing.** (The 4th planned item ‚Äî cluster-chain re-read reuse ‚Äî was found ALREADY DONE.)

### Changed
- **`exfat_read` + `fatfs_read` now coalesce a physically-contiguous cluster RUN into one multi-sector read, on top of 1.48.0's per-cluster collapse (`kernel/core/exfat.cyr` + `kernel/core/fatfs.cyr`).** Each scans forward while the chain stays contiguous (`next == cur+1`; exFAT `no_fat_chain` runs are contiguous by definition, no FAT read), **capped so the run stays ‚â§4 KB** ‚Äî the read is then one bounce command and there is **never a >4 KB per-sector regression**. For small clusters (spc=1) this folds up to **8 contiguous clusters into 1 NVMe command** (vs 8 per-cluster reads at 1.48.0); spc‚â•8 (‚â•4 KB cluster) stays one cluster (== 1.48.0). The scan's FAT reads hit the **already-cached FAT block** (`fatfs_fat_buf`/`exfat_fat_buf`); the run advances `clus` en bloc. The cluster-level analog of ext2's 1.47.6 multi-block read coalescing. Validated: **exfat-smoke PASS** (multi-cluster FAT-chain read, upcase TableChecksum reproduced), **fat-smoke PASS** (multi-cluster chain read byte-exact past the first cluster), `check.sh` 11/11. *(A self-caught `break`-vs-`run=cap` bug ‚Äî which would have read non-contiguous clusters as one run ‚Äî was fixed before build.)*
- **The 4th planned 1.48.x item (cluster-chain re-read reuse) was found already implemented** ‚Äî both drivers cache the current FAT block (`fatfs_fat_buf`/`exfat_fat_buf`) and re-read only on a FAT-block change, so consecutive clusters in the same block already hit the cache (the FAT/exFAT analog of 1.47.5's indirect-block reuse). **The 1.48.x FAT/exFAT perf review is now complete**: read-amp (1.48.0) + write-amp (1.48.1) + multi-cluster coalescing (1.48.2) + FAT-block reuse (pre-existing).

## [1.48.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.48.1 ‚Äî other-FS perf review (2nd item): FAT/exFAT cluster-WRITE amplification collapse (the write mirror of 1.48.0).**

### Changed
- **`fatfs_write_file` + `exfat_write_file` now write each cluster's full data sectors in ONE multi-sector write straight from `src` instead of sector-by-sector (`kernel/core/fatfs.cyr` + `kernel/core/exfat.cyr`).** Both filled each 512-byte sector with a byte-at-a-time copy then wrote it per-sector. They now write the full data sectors in one **`fat_blk_write_sectors`/`exfat_blk_write_sectors`** call (the new multi-sector write helpers, the write mirrors of the 1.48.0 read helpers) **directly from `src`** ‚Äî collapsing the per-sector writes AND eliminating the byte-copy; on NVMe a ‚â§4 KB cluster's data goes out as **1 multi-LBA command vs `sectors_per_cluster`** (the FAT/exFAT analog of ext2's `block_write_amp` 1.47.1). The partial tail data sector is filled (data + zero-pad) via `memcpy`/`memset` + a single-sector write. exFAT's clusters are contiguous (`exfat_alloc_contiguous`), so the whole extent's data writes in one call spanning all clusters (the cluster slack is zero-padded as before). Larger clusters (>4 KB) fall to the per-sector bounce, **no regression**. Validated: **fat-write-smoke PASS** (syscall write + read-back over FAT + whole-file + subdir ops), **exfat-write-smoke PASS** (write + read-back + **fsck.exfat -n clean** ‚Äî structure + SetChecksum/NameHash valid), `check.sh` 11/11.

## [1.48.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.48.0 ‚Äî opens the other-FS perf review arc (FAT/exFAT): cluster-read amplification collapse.**

The 1.47.x ext2 I/O perf wins now extend to the non-ext2 FS drivers (FAT16/FAT32 + exFAT), which read **per-sector** ‚Äî the same amplification ext2 had pre-1.42.8.

### Changed
- **`exfat_read` + `fatfs_read` now read each cluster in ONE multi-sector backend call instead of sector-by-sector (`kernel/core/exfat.cyr` + `kernel/core/fatfs.cyr`).** Both walked a cluster with an inner `for s < sectors_per_cluster` loop ‚Äî one `blk_read_on` per 512-byte sector + a byte-at-a-time copy into the caller's buffer. They now read the cluster's full sectors in one **`exfat_blk_read_sectors`/`fat_blk_read_sectors`** call (the new multi-sector helpers, mirroring the single-sector ones) **straight into `buf`** ‚Äî eliminating both the per-sector round-trips AND the byte-copy; on NVMe a ‚â§4 KB cluster goes out as **1 multi-LBA command vs `sectors_per_cluster`** (the FAT/exFAT analog of 1.42.8's `block_read_amp`, already proven). The partial tail sector (when the read ends mid-sector) still bounces through the 512-byte scratch. Larger clusters (>4 KB) fall to the per-sector path with **no regression**. Validated: **exfat-smoke PASS** (mount + multi-cluster FAT-chain read with the upcase TableChecksum reproduced + shell ls), **fat-smoke PASS** (mount + multi-cluster chain read byte-exact + cat + ls), `check.sh` 11/11. First item of the **1.48.x other-FS perf review** ‚Äî multi-cluster coalescing, FAT-write-path copy elimination, and FAT/cluster-chain re-read reuse follow as 1.48.x patches.

## [1.47.8] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.8 ‚Äî perf patch (6th of the 1.47.x perf series): scheduler single-pass round-robin.**

### Changed
- **`sched_next` collapses its two-pass idle-deprioritization scan into a single pass (`kernel/core/sched.cyr`).** The scheduler ran TWO full round-robin scans over the proc table per tick: pass 1 (skip the idle kthread, so real work is never starved by a hlt-only proc), then pass 2 (the same scan WITH idle included, so idle still runs when it's the only ready proc). The single pass scans once: the first ready+pickable **non-idle** proc wins immediately; the first ready+pickable **idle** is remembered and returned only if no non-idle was ready ‚Äî **byte-identical selection** to pass-1-then-pass-2 (when pass 1 found nothing, pass 2's first ready proc could only be an idle, since no non-idle was ready), at **half the per-tick iterations in the idle-only case** (the common boot-era kmain‚Üîidle rotation). All SMP fences unchanged (`state==1`, `on_cpu==-1` foreign-CPU skip, `sched_idx_pickable` foreign-idle + STEP-1-AP skips). The behavior-preserving cleanup flagged in the 1.44.22 audit. Validated: **ring3-smoke** (spawn/exec/wait/preempt/stress all OK), **thread-smoke** PASS (kthread time-slicing + preempt gate), **agnsh-smoke** PASS (boot-to-shell), `check.sh` 11/11.

## [1.47.7] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.7 ‚Äî perf patch (5th of the 1.47.x perf series): ext2 multi-block write coalescing (the write mirror of 1.47.6).**

### Changed
- **`ext2_write_at` now coalesces physically-contiguous full-block writes into ONE direct multi-LBA DMA, and the non-coalesced bounce copy is now word-wide (`kernel/core/ext2.cyr` + `kernel/core/block.cyr`).** The prior path wrote one NVMe command per FS block AND copied each block src‚Üí`ext2_block_buf` with a **byte-at-a-time 4 KB loop** before the bounce. Now, for a run of full, block-aligned writes from a DMA-safe src (page-aligned + in the 0‚Äì256 MB per-proc-CR3 identity window, IOMMU inactive), `ext2_write_at` allocates the run and ‚Äî if the blocks are physically contiguous ‚Äî writes the whole run **straight from src in one multi-LBA NVMe command** via the new **`blk_write_sectors_direct`**, skipping both the per-block copy and the bounce. The non-coalesced fallback (partial/unaligned/high/extent writes) now uses **word-wide `memcpy`/`memset`** instead of the byte loops. Gated identically to the 1.47.6 read coalescing; shares the **`ext2_read_coalesce_max`** kill-switch. Validated: new **`block_coalesce_write_amp: multi=1 perblk=16`** bench proof + **`ext2w: write-coalesce 32KB round-trip OK`** (data read back byte-identical ‚Äî e2fsck checks metadata, this checks content); ext2-write-smoke e2fsck-clean, exec-smoke 15/15, `check.sh` 11/11. **Iron-DMA validation rides the 1.47.x burn** alongside 1.47.6 (same NVMe >2-page PRP-list path; kill-switch is the fallback).

## [1.47.6] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.6 ‚Äî perf patch (4th of the 1.47.x perf series): ext2 multi-block read coalescing.**

### Changed
- **`ext2_read_at` now coalesces physically-contiguous block runs into ONE direct multi-LBA DMA (`kernel/core/ext2.cyr` + `kernel/core/block.cyr`).** The prior path issued one NVMe command per FS block (1.42.8's `block_read_amp` collapsed the 8 sectors *within* a block, but not across blocks), so a contiguous 589 KB exec-load was ~147 commands. `ext2_read_at` now scans forward for a run of contiguous physical blocks and reads up to **16 blocks (64 KB) in one multi-LBA NVMe command** via the new **`blk_read_sectors_direct`** ‚Äî DMA'd straight into the caller's buffer with **no bounce scratch and no PMM cost**. It is gated for safety: the dest must be **page-aligned and inside the 0‚Äì256 MB per-proc-CR3 identity window** (phys==virt ‚Üí physically contiguous = DMA-safe), and the IOMMU must be inactive (`iommu_active == 0` ‚Äî AMD Zen disables AMD-Vi + QEMU has no IOMMU; **Intel VT-d falls back to the per-block bounce path**). A sparse/partial block or a high/unaligned dest (e.g. a >256 MB user buffer) also falls through to the single-block path. **`ext2_read_coalesce_max`** is the kill-switch (set to 1 to force single-block). Cross-block mirror of `block_read_amp`. Validated: new **`block_coalesce_amp: multi=1 perblk=16`** bench proof; **exec-smoke 15/15** (binaries read through the coalesced path), ext2-write-smoke **e2fsck-clean**, `check.sh` 11/11. **Iron-DMA validation rides the 1.47.x burn** ‚Äî this is the first ext2-read use of the NVMe >2-page PRP-list path, which QEMU can't validate against real NVMe; the kill-switch is the fallback.

## [1.47.5] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.5 ‚Äî perf patch (3rd of the 1.47.x perf series): ext2 single-indirect read reuse.**

### Changed
- **`ext2_read_at` no longer re-reads the single-indirect block (`l1`) on every block of a sequential file read (`kernel/core/ext2.cyr`).** A file in the 48 KB‚Äì4 MB range keeps its data-block pointers in one single-indirect block (`i_block[12]` ‚Üí `l1`); the old `ext2_logical_to_physical` re-read `l1` from disk for **every** logical block, so an exec-load of a 589 KB binary (doom / kriya) re-read `l1` ~135√ó. A new **`ext2_logical_to_physical_cached`** keeps `l1` resident in `ext2_indirect_buf_l1` across one `ext2_read_at` call via a **per-call local cache** (`cached_l1`) and skips the read on a hit ‚Äî so a sequential single-indirect read drops from **N `l1` reads to 1**. Direct / double / triple-indirect / extent blocks defer to the original walker (which clobbers the buffer), invalidating the cache first ‚Äî so there is **no global state and no shared-buffer staleness risk**. Read-path analog of 1.47.2's dir-lookup inode reuse + 1.42.8/1.47.1's block read/write-amplification collapse. Validated: new **`ext2w: indirect reuse=1 reread=2`** proof (`EXT2_WRITE_SELFTEST`); **exec-smoke 15/15** (binaries read through the cached path), ext2-write-smoke **e2fsck-clean** (the new indirect write+read exercises a previously-untested selftest path), `check.sh` 11/11. Iron rides the 1.47.x burn.

## [1.47.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.4 ‚Äî full-binary KASLR (Option A): the kernel binary's physical load base is randomized per
boot.** Closes the last ~20% of KASLR value beyond the data-only scope shipped at 1.28.0 ‚Äî ROP/JOP
gadgets can no longer be pre-computed against a fixed kernel binary. Pairs with **gnoboot 0.6.0** (the
slid PIE-kernel loader). Built on the 1.47.3 foundation (the PIE kernel boots at a fixed base).

### Added
- **The kernel is now buildable as a position-independent ET_DYN image** via `CYRIUS_PIE=1
  ./scripts/build.sh` (cyrius `EMITELF64_KERNEL` ET_DYN + RIP-relative codegen ‚Äî **relocation-free**, no
  `.rela` table to walk). gnoboot loads it at an RDRAND-chosen, 2 MB-aligned base in **[32 MB, 254 MB)**
  and jumps to `base + e_entry`; the RIP-relative code runs correctly at the random base. The default
  (non-PIE ET_EXEC) build is byte-identical and unaffected ‚Äî KASLR is opt-in at the kernel-build level and
  driven entirely by which image gnoboot is handed.
- **Why no kernel-side memory rework was needed:** the slide window sits *above* the kernel's fixed
  0‚Äì18 MB boot scratch (page tables `0x1000`, boot stack `0x380000`, syscall + per-proc kstack pools) and
  the **16 MB PMM ceiling** (`pmm_alloc` only ever touches pages 1024‚Äì4095), and *below* the **256 MB
  per-proc-CR3 identity window** (`proc.cyr` copies PD[0..127]). So the PMM never allocates over the slid
  image, and every CR3 ‚Äî UEFI's 4 GB boot map and each per-proc CR3 ‚Äî maps it. Only the RIP-relative
  code/data slide; the kernel's absolute scratch constants stay fixed and correct.
- **`KASLR: kernel_base=<hex>` boot probe** (`core/main.cyr`, after the banner): prints the actual load
  base gnoboot passed at `boot_info+0x70` (copied CR3-independently by `boot_info_capture_rdi`) ‚Äî the
  per-boot slide proof + the base for crash symbolization.
- **`scripts/kaslr-smoke.sh`** ‚Äî boots the PIE kernel twice (a 3rd time only on a rare RDRAND tie) and
  asserts the bases are in-window, 2 MB-aligned, and **distinct** (the slide is live).

### Validated (QEMU; iron rides the 1.47.x burn)
- `kaslr-smoke` PASS ‚Äî distinct per-boot bases (observed 0x2C00000 / 0x5200000 / 0x9400000 / 0xD000000).
- `exec-smoke` 15/15 on a slid kernel (ring-3 exec + path lookups under the per-proc CR3 that maps the
