  slid base); fault-kill (`exit 142` + `SURVIVED`), `dirlk`, and banner all correct at the random base;
  `check.sh` 11/11.
- The non-PIE ET_EXEC kernel still loads at fixed `0x100000` (`KASLR: kernel_base=100000`) and boots
  unchanged.

## [1.47.3] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**Full-binary KASLR foundation (bite 1).**

### Added
- The AGNOS kernel can be built as a position-independent **ET_DYN** image (`CYRIUS_PIE=1`) and boots
  correctly at a *fixed* base via gnoboot ‚Äî proving cyrius's PIE codegen (RIP-relative, relocation-free)
  is correct on the real kernel. gnoboot loads an ET_DYN kernel (program-header `p_paddr=0`) at the fixed
  `0x100000` and jumps to `base + e_entry`. The per-boot randomization lands in 1.47.4.

### Changed
- **Cyrius toolchain pin ‚Üí 6.2.44** (kernel; gnoboot to match): the PIE / `EMITELF64_KERNEL` ET_DYN
  codegen path the KASLR work relies on.

## [1.47.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.2 ‚Äî perf patch (2 of the deferred 1.47.x perf series): ext2 directory-lookup inode reuse.**

### Changed
- **`ext2_path_lookup` no longer re-fetches each directory inode per path component (`kernel/core/ext2.cyr`).** Walking `/a/b/c` read every intermediate inode TWICE: once for the symlink check (`ext2_get_inode(next_inode)`), then again at the next component's `ext2_dir_lookup` (which re-fetched the same inode). `ext2_dir_lookup` is split into **`ext2_dir_lookup_in(inode_buf, name, len)`** (the name-scan given an already-resident dir inode ‚Äî the scan never clobbers `inode_buf`, since `ext2_logical_to_physical` uses the indirect/extent bufs and the dirent scan uses `ext2_dir_buf`) + a thin `ext2_dir_lookup` wrapper that fetches then calls it. `ext2_path_lookup` now keeps the current dir's inode **resident in `ext2_symres_inode`** (pre-read root once; the symlink-check read leaves `next_inode` ‚Äî the next current ‚Äî in it; a symlink follow re-syncs it) and calls `ext2_dir_lookup_in` per component ‚Äî so a **K-component path drops from ~2K to ~K+1 inode reads** (each = 1 BGDT + 1 inode-table block). Read-only optimization ‚Äî no caching, no write-invalidation risk (path lookup never writes). Validated: new **`ext2w: dirlk reuse=0 reread=1`** proof in `EXT2_WRITE_SELFTEST` (the per-component inode read is elided ‚Äî the analog of `block_write_amp`); **ext2-write-smoke PASS** (all path-lookup + symlink cases correct ‚Äî fast/relative/slow symlinks, ELOOP, cross-parent dir move ‚Äî + e2fsck clean); exec-smoke 15/15; check.sh 11/11. Iron rides the 1.47.x burn.

## [1.47.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.1 ‚Äî perf patch (1 of the deferred 1.47.x perf series): ext2 block-write batching.** First of the per-item performance patches deferred from the 1.47.0 cut.

### Changed
- **ext2 block writes now issue ONE multi-LBA NVMe command per 4 KB block instead of 8 single-sector submits (`kernel/core/ext2.cyr`, `block.cyr`, `nvme.cyr`).** The write-side mirror of the 1.42.8 read fast-path: `ext2_write_block` looped `ext2_sectors_per_block` (8) single-sector `blk_write_on` calls (8 NVMe submit/poll round-trips per block) while the read path already batched. New **`nvme_blk_write_sectors`** (mirror of `nvme_blk_read_sectors` ‚Äî copies the caller's possibly-non-DMA-safe buffer into the DMA-safe `nvme_read_scratch` under `nvme_lock`, then one multi-LBA write; the read bounce reuses the same scratch, the lock serializes them) + **`blk_write_sectors_on`** dispatch (NVMe ‚Üí the fast path; other backends keep the per-sector loop ‚Äî AHCI/USB already issue ~1 command/sector, no amplification to collapse). `ext2_write_block` now calls `blk_write_sectors_on` ‚Üí **~8√ó fewer NVMe round-trips on every block write** (file create, directory update, jbd2 journal commits, the self-hosting build's write load). Validated: new **`block_write_amp` benchmark = `multi=1 single=8`** (the structural proof, the analog of `block_read_amp`); **ext2-write-smoke PASS** (all 19 write ops produce correct on-disk data + clean superblock ‚Äî the batched DMA-bounce write is correct); `check.sh` 11/11. Behavior-identical on non-NVMe backends (the fallback loop). Iron-burn rides the 1.47.x burn.

## [1.47.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

**‚ñ∂ 1.47.0 ‚Äî fault-resilience + per-process fd tables.** Cut on the current work: the two systemic-robustness bites the just-closed **1.46.x SMP arc** (STEP-2 iron-validated on archaemenid 2026-06-26) surfaced ‚Äî **(1) proc-teardown-on-fault** and **(2) per-process fd tables** (2a seam ‚Üí 2b storage+inheritance ‚Üí 2c pipe refcount) ‚Äî both QEMU-validated; iron burn pending. The **perf tail + full-binary KASLR Option A are DEFERRED to 1.47.x PATCHES** (per user 2026-06-27: cut 1.47.0 on the current work, performance lands per-patch); any iron-burn fixes also land as 1.47.x patches.

### Added
- **1.47.x arc framing + bite ladder.** Headline first bites:
  1. **Proc-teardown-on-fault** ‚Äî a faulting **ring-3** process must be *terminated* with control returned to the agnsh prompt, instead of the current behavior where an unhandled ring-3 `#PF`/`#GP` halts the box behind the 1.45.16 fault-canary bar. Surfaced by the cyrius-doom sustained-play `#PF` (CPL3, `err` U/S=1) on the 1.46.8 burn: a userland segfault should not take the system down, but there is **no signal-delivery / proc-teardown-on-fault path** yet ([[project_doom_on_agnos_blockers]]).
  2. **Per-process fd tables** ‚Äî filed by the 1.46.11 pipe work (`docs/development/issues/2026-06-27-agnos-pipes-per-proc-fd-streaming.md`): today's pipes are single-foreground store-and-forward over a global fd table; per-proc fd tables unlock **streaming / SMP-safe / N-stage** pipes.

  **Deferred to 1.47.x patches** (NOT in the 1.47.0 cut): the **perf-tuning remainder** ‚Äî scheduler/context-switch, syscall entry/exit, block-IO batching, ext2/4 hot loops (the 1.42.x Track-A tail; heap-zeroing already landed at 1.42.5) ‚Äî and **full-binary KASLR Option A** (un-gated by cyrius userland PIE v6.1.41). Tracker ‚Üí agnosticos iron-log `#tracker-147x-cycle`. 1.46.x SMP arc marked iron-complete.

- **proc-teardown-on-fault ‚Äî a ring-3 (CPL3) fault kills the proc + returns to the agnsh prompt instead of halting the box (`kernel/arch/x86_64/idt.cyr`, `kernel/core/syscall.cyr`, `kernel/core/main.cyr`).** The first bite, landed under 1.47.0. The `#PF`/`#GP`/`#UD` exception stubs gained a **privilege gate**: a CPL3 fault (saved-frame CS RPL==3) `call`s a new `fault_kill_current(vector)` which mirrors `exit#0`'s teardown ‚Äî exit code `128+vector`, `flock_release_pid`, mark the proc dead, SIGCHLD the parent ‚Äî then `kernel_resume()`s back into the foreground `exec_and_wait` (agnsh) EXACTLY as a clean exit would, reusing the proven exit path. A **CPL0 (kernel) fault still falls through to the 1.45.16 canary-bar halt, UNCHANGED** ‚Äî a real kernel bug halts loudly (the gate's `jne` skips the kill-call only when CPL==3). The stub‚Üíhandler call is INDIRECT through a new `exc_ring3_kill_fn` global: idt.cyr is parsed (agnos.cyr:14) before syscall.cyr (:107), so a direct `&fault_kill_current` can't resolve ‚Äî main.cyr (parsed last) points the hook at the handler during boot init. The stub 16-aligns rsp before the call (the exception frame is abandoned ‚Äî teardown either `kernel_resume`s away or returns to halt). **QEMU-validated** (`scripts/fault-kill-smoke.sh` + the `FAULT_SELFTEST` kernel selftest, a ring-3 ELF reading an unmapped 5 GB addr): `run: exit 142` (128+14) + `fault: SURVIVED back in kernel` ‚Äî no halt; `exec-smoke` 16/16 + `check.sh` 11/11 (no regression ‚Äî the shared exec / `kernel_resume` / execwait#37 path intact). **Scope:** the foreground exec child (the cyrius-doom OOB-#PF case). A faulted `&` background job still halts ‚Äî bg-proc fault teardown needs a scheduler-yield-from-fault path (follow-on, same arc). **Iron-burn pending** (the agnsh‚Üítool #37 path rides the same `kernel_resume` exec-smoke just re-proved). `build/agnos` 1,312,000 B.

- **per-process fd tables (bite 2: 2a seam ‚Üí 2b storage+inheritance ‚Üí 2c pipe refcount) ‚Äî `kernel/core/vfs.cyr`, `proc.cyr`, `syscall.cyr`.** Replaces the single global `vfs_table` (which the pipe redirect swapped) with a PER-PROCESS table ‚Äî the prerequisite for streaming / SMP-safe / N-stage pipes. **2a** routed every `vfs_table` access through a `vfs_fd_table()` accessor (byte-behavior-identical seam, ~30 sites). **2b** gave each proc its own kmalloc'd 32-entry table (`proc_fd_base[16]`, like `proc_rsp0[16]`; proc 0 keeps the static global), INHERITING a copy of the creator's table at `proc_create_user` (the single point covering elf_load / elf_load_from_file / spawn) and freeing it at reap; the execwait#37 redirect now targets the CHILD's table (`ew37_cpid`), not the parent's ‚Äî so a child's `close()` or a pipe redirect can no longer race/clobber the parent's fds. `proc_fd_base[pid]==0` falls back to the global (kthreads / pre-init / kmalloc-fail) ‚Äî a safe degradation, not a break. **2c** added the `VFS_PIPE` arm to `vfs_close` with an OWNER-keyed refcount (`pipe_rc_*`): each pipe buffer is owned by its creator, only the owner's two closes decrement, freed at 0 ‚Äî killing the prior 4 KB-per-pipeline leak (a child closing an inherited copy is a no-op; the owner's reap also frees). Store-and-forward correctness holds because the pipe write head lives in the SHARED buffer (only the read tail is per-entry, so an inherited reader still drains to the shared head). Validated: `check.sh` 11/11 ¬∑ **pipe-smoke PASS** (inheritance + redirect-to-child + shared-buffer drain) ¬∑ exec-smoke 15/15 + fault-kill-smoke (no regression) ¬∑ a new **`PIPE_RC_SELFTEST`** proves the buffer frees on the owner's LAST close (heap_frees witness, not the first) + double-close-safe. Iron-burn pending. `build/agnos` 1,313,200 B.

## [1.46.11] ‚Äî 2026-06-27

### Fixed
- **`read#5` now honors a redirected fd 0 ‚Äî the stdin side of shell pipes (`kernel/core/syscall.cyr`).** `read(0)` hardcoded the keyboard path (`kbd_read_blocking`/`kbd_read_nonblock`), so even after `exec_redirect#62` copied a pipe read-end's entry over `vfs_table[0]`, a child's `read(0)` still read the keyboard ‚Äî the `cmd2` side of `cmd1 | cmd2` could never consume its piped stdin. Now the keyboard path is taken **only while fd 0 is still the console device** (`ktag(&vfs_table) == VFS_DEVICE`); a redirected fd 0 (tag `VFS_PIPE`) falls through to `vfs_read ‚Üí pipe_read`. The interactive keyboard is unchanged (fd 0 is `VFS_DEVICE` at the prompt). This completes the kernel half of two-stage pipes ‚Äî the stdout‚Üípipe direction (`write#1` tag-dispatch + `exec_redirect#62`/`execwait#37` apply-restore) was already proven. Validated: `exec-redirect-smoke` now asserts `redir: stdin-pipe OK` (a redirected fd 0 reads the pipe, not the keyboard ‚Äî added to the `EXEC_REDIRECT_SELFTEST`), keyboard no-regression (`agnsh-type-test`), `check.sh` 11/11, and a new end-to-end `scripts/pipe-smoke.py` (`owl -p /hello.txt | anuenue` ‚Üí owl's `OWLPROOF` reaches anuenue's stdin and is truecolor-rainbow'd on the console). Pipe semantics (store-and-forward, 4088-byte buffer, single-foreground) + the two follow-ons (pipe-buffer refcount free-on-close; per-process fd tables for streaming/SMP-safe pipes) are tracked in `docs/development/issues/2026-06-27-agnos-pipe-buffer-leak-refcount.md` + `‚Ä¶-pipes-per-proc-fd-streaming.md`. agnoshi's `sh_run_pipeline` driver is the consumer.

## [1.46.10] ‚Äî 2026-06-26

### Changed
- **Raised the exec argv cap 8 ‚Üí 16 tokens (`kernel/core/elf.cyr`).** `elf_load_from_file` builds the SysV init stack from the command tail and broke the tokenize loop at `argc >= 8`, so `<tool> a b c d e f g h ‚Ä¶` silently dropped every token past the 8th ‚Äî e.g. `anuenue something longer to test the length of project` (9 tokens) lost the last word ("cuts off after a few words"). agnos has no working shell pipes yet (an agnoshi follow-up), so positional argv is the only way to pass text to a tool, and 8 was too low for a sentence. The argv pointer array (`0x3008..0x3100`) plus the 2-entry envp hold ~27 ptr slots, so 16 is safe headroom; the existing `str_o < 0x4000` string-region cap still bounds total command length. Validated: `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-delegation` PASS, `exec-smoke` (EXEC_SELFTEST) PASS; `build/agnos` byte-size-identical (1-byte immediate). Surfaced by the anuenue rainbow "cuts off after a few words" report.

## [1.46.9] ‚Äî 2026-06-26

### Added
- **`input_lock` ‚Äî the kb_buf SMP/ISR serialization lock (1.46.x SMP arc, sub-bite 5 completion).** `kb_buf` is a ring with **multiple producers advancing `kb_head`** ‚Äî `kb_isr` (the IRQ1 PS/2 hand-asm ISR) and `hid_kb_push` (the xHCI USB path) ‚Äî plus one consumer advancing `kb_tail` (`kb_read_scancode`). The `(idx+1)&0xFF` index update is a non-atomic load-modify-store, so a producer interleaving another producer/consumer (an IRQ1 mid-RMW same-CPU, or a foreign CPU once `smp_sched_aps=1` schedules real procs on APs) loses or corrupts a keystroke. `input_lock` (`kernel/arch/x86_64/keyboard.cyr`) serializes the `kb_buf` critical sections: **cli first** (mask the same-CPU IRQ1 producer so the spinlock can't self-deadlock against `kb_isr`) **then an xchg spinlock** (the cross-CPU guard), via new `input_spin_lock`/`input_spin_unlock` (byte-identical to the proven no-param `pmm_spin_lock` ‚Äî exact `[rbp-N]` frame offsets, objdump-verified) and a sole-local `kbd_irq_save`/`kbd_irq_restore` irqsave pair (re-enables IF only if it was set on entry, so the critical section is correct even from a future IF=0 caller ‚Äî e.g. `hid_poll` serviced from the timer ISR). Wired into the two production C-path RMWs (`hid_kb_push`, `kb_read_scancode`). `kb_isr` is left UNMODIFIED: it runs at IF=0, so a C-path holding `input_lock` with cli excludes it same-CPU, and IRQ1 is PIC/IOAPIC-routed to the BSP while the `kb_buf` consumers run BSP-pinned (foreground) ‚Äî so it never contends cross-CPU under the current single-IRQ1 routing (a follow-on if IRQ1 is ever redirected to an AP). Slots into the plan's global lock order (`‚Ä¶console_lock < input_lock < nvme_lock‚Ä¶`) as a pure leaf (the critical sections do no allocation, so it never nests). Closes the last strictly-SMP deferred lock from sub-bite 5; the net-family cli/sti locks remain the separate net-revisit scope. Validated (QEMU): `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-type-test` (multi-command typing, USB path), `agnsh-irq1-repro` PASS (the PS/2 `kb_isr` path coexists with the locked C-paths ‚Äî bug not reproduced), `smp-smoke` `cpus online: 4`, `agnsh-bg-smp4` PASS (keyboard input survives under live concurrent AP scheduling). `build/agnos` 1,309,616 B. Near-zero iron value on archaemenid (IRQ1 is dead there ‚Äî keystrokes are xHCI `hid_poll`); landed for SMP-correctness completeness + QEMU PS/2 correctness + future-proofing the hid-poll-from-ISR path.

## [1.46.8] ‚Äî 2026-06-26

**‚òÖ 1.46.8 ‚Äî SMP STEP-2 pre-burn review fixes (independent adversarial review of 1.46.7).**
A second independent review (6 dimension reviewers + refute-by-default verification) of the 1.46.7
hardening, run at the user's request before staging the iron burn, raised **6 findings ‚Äî all 6
confirmed, 0 refuted, 0 burn-blockers** (5 distinct: #1==#4). Each is a real SMP-correctness gap the
`smp_sched_aps=1` flip makes reachable; this cut closes the four code ones + one doc clarification.

### Fixed
- **`exec_env_src` / `exec_env_len` left as bare globals ‚Äî the missed per-CPU conversion (`kernel/core/elf.cyr`, `kernel/core/syscall.cyr`, `kernel/user/init.cyr`).** 1.46.7 made the env *buffers* (`ew37_env`/`spawn_env_buf`) per-CPU but not the two pointer globals that hand them to the loader. They are set in the #37/#43 handlers + `kybernet` right before `elf_load_from_file` and consumed-at-entry there; under `smp_sched_aps=1` two CPUs in #37/#43 concurrently clobber the shared pair ‚Üí CPU A's child gets CPU B's env (a cross-process env leak) or B's child loses its env. Converted to `pcpu_exec_env_src[4]`/`pcpu_exec_env_len[4]` + `_get`/`_set` folding `pcpu_cpu()*8`. (No fault ‚Äî the clobbered value is always a valid kernel buffer or 0 ‚Äî but it is silent env corruption.)
- **agnsh-launch preemptible unpinned gap ‚Äî `proc_current_set` before the `exec_and_wait` pin (`kernel/user/init.cyr`).** `kybernet` (proc 0) runs IF=1-preemptibly; it sets `proc_current_get()` = agnsh at the kernel launch *before* `exec_and_wait` pins it (`idle_owner`). A BSP timer tick in that gap runs `do_context_switch` with `old==agnsh` (state==3, unpinned) ‚Üí `sched.cyr:348` flips agnsh `state 3‚Üí1` + `on_cpu=-1`, and a woken AP's `sched_next` then steals the half-set-up agnsh (`proc_cs` still ring-0 `0x08`) and runs `kybernet`'s gap continuation on the AP. The #2 (state=3) fix narrows this but does not close it because `do_context_switch` readies `old` unconditionally. **Fix:** `cli` the gap (one byte) so it runs IF=0 ‚Äî exactly how the #37 path is already immune (its whole handler is IF=0); `enter_ring3`'s iretq restores IF=1 (`rflags_start=0x202`) so agnsh still runs preemptible. Applied to both the agnsh and `NET_SELFTEST` launches. (The review's suggested pin-only / `preempt_disable` fixes were both unsound ‚Äî pin loses the gap's continuation when the BSP switches away; `preempt_disable` would never re-enable ‚Üí non-preemptible agnsh.)
- **`elf_hdr_buf` shared scratch parsed unlocked (`kernel/core/elf.cyr`).** The ELF header+phdr buffer is filled under `fs_lock` by `vfs_read_file_at` but **parsed** (validation + the PT_LOAD segment-map loop) with no lock held ‚Äî the "single-consumer FS scratch" invariant the flip breaks. Two concurrent `elf_load_from_file` (#37 on the BSP + #43 on an AP) let CPU B's header read overwrite the phdrs CPU A is still walking (TOCTOU on the segment bounds ‚Üí a corrupt/mis-mapped child). Made per-CPU: `pcpu_elf_hdr_buf[2048]` + `elf_hdr_buf_base()` folding `pcpu_cpu()*4096`.
- **`vfs_file_size` walks ext2 metadata scratch with no `fs_lock` (`kernel/core/vfs.cyr`) ‚Äî the highest-severity finding.** `vfs_file_size` (`ext2_path_lookup` + `ext2_get_inode` ‚Üí the shared `ext2_inode_buf`/`block_buf`/`bgdt_buf`/`dir_buf`) is called from `elf_load_from_file`, which does **not** hold `fs_lock` ‚Äî so it raced a concurrent locked `vfs_read_file_at`/FS-mutation on another CPU and corrupted an in-flight file read. Wrapped in `fs_lock` (outer-wrapper + `vfs_file_size_inner`, single return point, mirroring `vfs_read_file_at`). Audited every other ext2-touching `vfs.cyr` entry: the `_inner` functions are covered by their `vfs_*_on` wrapper, and `vfs_ext2_parent` by the FS-mutation **syscall handlers'** own `fs_lock` (so it must *not* self-lock ‚Äî re-acquire would deadlock); `vfs_file_size` was the lone unlocked ext2 entry on a concurrent path.

### Changed
- **Net residual scope note widened (`kernel/arch/x86_64/smp.cyr`, doc-only ‚Äî review #3, low).** The `net_rx_lock` deferred-residual comment now also notes the RX *consumer-read* path: `net_recv_udp`/`tcp_recv` read `net_udp_buf`/`tcp_conns` **after** `net_poll()` releases `net_rx_lock`, so a concurrent drain could overwrite the buffer between drain and read. Same AP-only reachability as the TX residual; not a foreground-model burn issue. No code change.



**‚òÖ 1.46.7 ‚Äî SMP STEP-2 part 2: full hardening (the last 3 concurrency bugs ‚Üí burn-ready).**
Completes the STEP-2 hardening the 6-lens adversarial review surfaced. Where 1.46.6 landed the
always-reachable double-run + per-CPU `ksyscall_a4`, this cut closes the three bugs that need a woken
AP running a *real* proc to reach ‚Äî (#3) the per-CPU `exec_and_wait`/#37 resume + staging context,
(#2) the rare proc-creation mid-load readiness window, and (#5) net-stack RX cross-CPU locking. After
this, the `-smp 4` `#43`/`#37`/net paths are clean (functional + `-d int` exception census); **STEP-2
is burn-ready.** `smp_sched_aps=0` remains the kill-switch (full revert to the STEP-1 MVP).

### Fixed
- **#3 ‚Äî per-CPU `exec_and_wait`/#37 resume + staging context (`kernel/arch/x86_64/ring3.cyr`, `kernel/arch/x86_64/syscall_hw.cyr`, `kernel/core/syscall.cyr`, `kernel/core/main.cyr`).** The whole exec/program-launch state was a single-CPU invariant. Under `smp_sched_aps=1` a background proc on an AP that calls `execwait#37`/`spawn` clobbers `agnsh`'s in-flight resume frame on the BSP (and vice versa). Converted to per-CPU, resolving the kernel's own deferred note (`syscall.cyr`: *"make `ew37_*` per-CPU then [at the flip]"*): the **resume context** (`exec_ctx` setjmp, `exec_entry_g`/`rsp_g`/`cr3_g`, `exec_resume_pid`, `kernel_return_rsp`/`rip` ‚Äî `exec_ctx` IS a kernel-stack continuation, so per-CPU is also *correctness*), the **`ew37_*` nested-save block** (9 scalars + `ew37_ctx`/`ew37_path`/`ew37_env`), the **spawn staging** (`spawn_elf_buf`/`spawn_path_buf`/`spawn_env_buf`), and the **one-shot fd-redirect** (`redir_pending`/`src_fd`/`dst_fd`/`redir_backup`). Each scalar ‚Üí `pcpu_X[4]` + `_get`/`_set` folding `pcpu_cpu()*8`; each array ‚Üí `pcpu_X[N*4]` + a `_base()` helper folding `pcpu_cpu()*stride` (so the hand-asm copy loops read VERBATIM into this CPU's block). `pcpu_cpu()`'s `cpu_count<2` fast path = byte-identical single-core. Validated: `run37-smp4` (two foreground `#37` render + `agnsh` resumes intact), `agnsh-bg-smp4` (`#43`), `exec-smoke`, `exec-redirect-smoke`.
- **#2 ‚Äî the proc-creation mid-load readiness window (`kernel/core/proc.cyr`, `kernel/core/elf.cyr`, `kernel/arch/x86_64/ring3.cyr`, `kernel/core/syscall.cyr`).** `proc_create_full` set `state=1` (schedulable) *before* its user-proc callers wired the per-proc CR3 + ring-3 selectors. Under `smp_sched_aps=1` a woken AP's `sched_next` could pick the half-built proc (CR3=`0x1000`, ring-0 `CS=0x08`) and `iretq` a user RIP under ring 0 ‚Üí fault. **Fix:** new `proc_create_user` = `proc_create_full` but leaves the slot at the existing `state=3` **claiming** sentinel (invisible to both `sched_next`, which picks only `==1`, and `proc_alloc_slot`'s reuse scan, which reuses only `==0`). `elf_load`/`spawn_user_proc` flip it ready (`proc_set_state(pid,1)`) only *after* full wiring; `elf_load_from_file` returns it **not-ready** and the `#43` handler readies it after `proc_set_ring3`; the `#37` child **stays `state=3`** for its whole run ‚Äî entered out-of-band via `exec_and_wait`/`enter_ring3`, never via `sched`, which also blocks a woken AP from double-running it. `proc_create_full` (kthread/idle/boot ‚Äî valid-on-return) is untouched. Validated: `exec-smoke` (from-disk `#37`), `agnsh-bg-smp4` (`#43` ready‚Üírun‚Üíreap), `run37-smp4`.
- **#5 ‚Äî net-stack RX-drain cross-CPU lock (`kernel/arch/x86_64/smp.cyr`, `kernel/core/net_ingress.cyr`, `kernel/arch/x86_64/pic.cyr`).** `net_rx_drain` (the single consumer of the one shared r8169 RX ring + dispatch tables) was guarded by the **non-atomic** `net_poll_busy` flag. The timer-ISR drain is already BSP-gated (`pic.cyr`, `pcpu_cpu()==0`), but under `smp_sched_aps=1` a background net program on an AP can drive `net_poll()`‚Üí`net_rx_drain` *concurrently* with the BSP ISR drain ‚Äî the non-atomic check-then-set races cross-CPU ‚Üí both drain the one ring ‚Üí `net_rx_pkt`/`r8169_rx_idx` corruption. Replaced with `net_rx_lock`, an **atomic `xchg` TRY-lock** (`net_rx_trylock` returns `0`=acquired / `1`=busy‚Üískip): a non-blocking try, so it is ISR-safe + re-entry-proof **without** `cli`/`sti` (a same-CPU timer landing mid-drain just fails the trylock ‚Üí no-op, exactly as the old flag intended ‚Äî a spinlock here would self-deadlock). LEAF lock (the RX dispatch path touches only static arrays ‚Äî no `kmalloc`/`pmm`). Validated: `whirl-smoke` (HTTP + HTTPS fetched example.com over the sovereign stack).

### Changed
- **`smp_sched_aps` STEP-2 hardening complete ‚Äî burn-ready.** With #2/#3/#5 closed (joining 1.46.6's double-run + `ksyscall_a4`), all five concurrency bugs the adversarial review surfaced are fixed. `-smp 4 -d int` test harness added (`scripts/run37-smp4-test.py`, `RUN37_DINT=1`) for the foreground-`#37` exception census.

### Notes
- **Deferred residual (net TX/conn side ‚Äî NOT reachable in the foreground-net model).** #5 locks the RX-drain consumer; the TX/conn-table mutators (`nic_send`/`tcp_send`/`tcp_connect` touching `tcp_conns`/`arp_cache` from a net syscall) are **not** yet under a cross-CPU lock. Closing it needs short-held fine-grained conn-table locking that never spans the blocking ~8 s `tcp_connect` poll (holding the lock there would starve the BSP RX drain) and never self-deadlocks the RX-dispatch‚ÜíACK-send path ‚Äî a follow-on. Only reachable once a net program runs on an **AP** (a background net tool); the current net stack runs foreground on the BSP (all net state BSP-serial).

## [1.46.6] ‚Äî 2026-06-26

**‚òÖ 1.46.6 ‚Äî SMP STEP-2 part 1: the flip + dominant double-run hardening (clean `-smp 4` boot).**
Flips `smp_sched_aps=1` so woken APs pull real ring-3 procs from `sched_next` ‚Äî and fixes the bugs the
flip exposes. The flip is **not** a one-liner: a 6-lens adversarial review (16 agents) + a QEMU
`-smp 4 -d int` repro (single-core = 0 exceptions; `-smp 4` reproduced torn frames + `#UD`/`#DF`)
surfaced **5 concurrency bugs**. This cut lands the always-reachable ones ‚Äî the dominant *double-run*
class that broke the boot, plus per-CPU `ksyscall_a4` ‚Äî and reaches a **deterministic clean boot to the
`[ASSIST] >` prompt under `-smp 4` (3/3 runs, 0 exceptions)**, matching single-core. The remaining two
(per-CPU exec-resume context, net cross-CPU locking) + the rare mid-load window land at **1.46.7**;
**do not burn until then.** `smp_sched_aps=0` is the kill-switch (full revert to the STEP-1 MVP).

### Fixed
- **The double-run class: out-of-band, BSP-bound, preemptible procs ran *unpinned* ‚Üí a woken AP stole them (`kernel/core/main.cyr`, `kernel/arch/x86_64/ring3.cyr`, `kernel/core/proc.cyr`).** A proc entered via `enter_ring3` (not `do_context_switch`) runs at `state==1`/`on_cpu==-1`; under `smp_sched_aps=1` an AP's `sched_next` saw it as a ready unowned proc and ran it concurrently on the same CR3/stack ‚Äî the QEMU `-smp 4` faults (torn ring-3 frame `IP=SP=0x1caa49`; an AP stealing `kybernet` ‚Üí `kputc` overran the framebuffer ‚Üí `#PF`/`#DF`; `kmain` re-run ‚Üí `#UD` at `0x1000`). **Fix:** pin every out-of-band BSP-bound proc to its launch CPU via `idle_owner` (the per-proc CPU-pin `sched_idx_pickable` already honors ‚Äî idles were its first users): the **BSP idle** and **proc 0/kmain** at boot (`main.cyr`, after `proc_on_cpu_init` which zeroes `idle_owner`), and every **foreground (`exec_and_wait`) proc** to its launch CPU (`ring3.cyr`, before `enter_ring3`). `proc_alloc_slot` now resets `idle_owner` on slot reuse so a recycled slot starts unpinned (`proc.cyr`). Foreground procs carry a launch-CPU-bound kernel resume context (`exec_ctx`), so pinning is also *correctness* ‚Äî they must never migrate. Inert single-core (`sched_idx_pickable`'s `io==pcpu_cpu()` branch keeps the BSP picking its own pinned procs); validated by 3/3 clean `-smp 4 -d int` boots.
- **Per-CPU `ksyscall_a4` ‚Äî concurrent 4-arg-syscall race (`kernel/arch/x86_64/syscall_hw.cyr`, `kernel/core/syscall.cyr`, `kernel/core/main.cyr`).** The single global 4th-syscall-arg scratch (set at `syscall_handler` entry, read deep in `ksyscall` by rename/link/blit/execwait/spawn/usercopy/kbd) would be clobbered between set and read by a second CPU's syscall ‚Üí e.g. `rename` with the wrong `newlen` ‚Üí FS corruption. Converted to `pcpu_ksyscall_a4[4]` + `ksyscall_a4_get`/`_set` folding `pcpu_cpu()*8`, mirroring `pcpu_sc_entry_regs`/`sc_scr_base`; `pcpu_cpu()`'s `cpu_count<2` fast path keeps it byte-identical single-core.

### Changed
- **`smp_sched_aps=1` (`kernel/arch/x86_64/smp.cyr`) ‚Äî woken APs now schedule real ring-3 procs** (SMP sub-bite-7 STEP-2). Rides the 1.46.5 SYSRET fix (`ap_entry` runs `syscall_msr_init` so each AP bakes `STAR=0x13`). Kill-switch: revert to `0` for STEP-1 (wake + AP-idle-only).

## [1.46.5] ‚Äî 2026-06-26

**‚òÖ 1.46.5 ‚Äî IF=1 PREEMPTIVE AGNSH ON IRON (the ~2-week SYSRET SS-RPL blocker, fixed in one byte).**
The `#GP` that froze every IF=1-preemptive `agnsh` boot on real Zen since 1.44.14 ‚Äî the single blocker
gating both preemptive `agnsh` and SMP STEP-2 ‚Äî is **root-caused and fixed**. archaemenid (Zen) now
boots with `exec_preempt=1`: kybernet execs `/bin/agnsh`, the shell reaches its `[ASSIST] >` prompt
**preemptible (IF=1)**, and multi-command interaction (coreutils/`kriya`, `bnrmr`, `owl`, builtins)
survives ‚Äî **confirmed on iron** (user burn, 2026-06-26). This also confirms 1.46.4's working-coreutils
(region-8 PMM) fix on hardware. SMP wake stays on (STEP-1, `cpus online: 4`). The torn-live-frame
`(a)/(b)` ambiguity that dominated 1.46.1‚Äì1.46.4 is **superseded** ‚Äî the real cause was never a frame
tear (see below). Next: SMP sub-bite-7 **STEP-2** (`smp_sched_aps=1`), which rides this same fix.

### Fixed
- **The IF=1 first-preempt `agnsh` `#GP` = the AMD SYSRET SS-RPL bug (`kernel/arch/x86_64/syscall_hw.cyr`, one byte).** `syscall_msr_init` programmed `STAR[63:48] = 0x10` (the user CS/SS base). On 64-bit `SYSRET`, AMD (APM Vol 3 ¬ßSYSRET, verbatim: *"the CPL is forced to 3 ‚Ä¶ the SS selector ‚Ä¶ RPL is NOT forced to 3"*) forces `CS.RPL=3` (‚Üí `CS=0x23` ‚úì) but loads **SS verbatim** = base+8 = **`0x18` (RPL 0)**. So after `agnsh`'s first syscall returned, it ran ring-3 with `SS=0x18` while `CPL=3`; the next timer-ISR `iretq` returning to CPL3 then `#GP(0x18)`'d (an `iretq` to an outer ring requires `SS.RPL == CS.RPL`). **Intel SDM and QEMU TCG both OR 3 into SS on SYSRET** (QEMU `helper_sysret`, post bug #1428352), so it was invisible in every QEMU run (245k+ int-lines clean) ‚Äî iron-only for ~2 weeks. **Fix:** `STAR[63:48] = 0x13` (= `0x10 | 3`, RPL-3 baked into the base) ‚Üí SYSRET yields `CS=0x13+16=0x23`, `SS=0x13+8=0x1B`, both RPL 3 (the +8/+16 don't carry into the low 2 RPL bits). This is exactly Linux's `MSR_STAR` layout. The fault frame decoded on iron (`idt.cyr` decoder below) was the gun: **LOADED `SS=0x18`** (RPL stripped to 0), LOADED `CS=0x23` (correct ring-3 ‚Äî falsifying the long-chased "torn `CS=0x08`" theory, which had conflated the *faulting-instruction* CS inside the ring-0 ISR with the *loaded-frame* CS), GDT[3] access `0xF3` (= `0xF2 | Accessed` = a valid descriptor, not corruption). `build/agnos` 1,235,040 B (byte-size-identical ‚Äî an immediate-byte change).
- **Confirms on iron: 1.46.4 working-coreutils (`pmm.cyr` region-8 reservation).** The same burn that proved the SYSRET fix ran `ls`/`kriya`/`bnrmr`/`owl` from the shell with no `argv`-empty `#PF` ‚Äî 1.46.4's region-8 fix (previously QEMU-only) is now hardware-confirmed.

### Added
- **IF=1 `#GP` live-frame diagnostic ‚Äî the `idt.cyr` `#GP` decoder + `sched.cyr` switch heartbeat (`kernel/arch/x86_64/idt.cyr`, `boot_data.cyr`, `kernel/core/sched.cyr`).** The pure-diagnostic burn that cracked the bug: the `#GP` handler (vector 13) dereferences the faulting `iretq`'s saved frame and relays the **LOADED** `SS`/`CS`/`RFLAGS`/`RSP` + GDT[3] access byte (live `sgdt`) + the last `do_context_switch` transition (`g_switch_log` heartbeat: old/new pid + switch counter + magic `0x5C`) to virgin low-bank CMOS, decoded by `agnosticos read-boot-log.cyr`. This is what isolated the LOADED `SS=0x18` as the gun. Kept in the binary (zero-perturbation: the decoder runs only on a `#GP`, the heartbeat is a single global store that never touches the `iretq` frame) so it is available for the STEP-2 burn; QEMU byte-identical, `agnsh-smoke` PASS.

### Changed
- **`exec_preempt=1` ‚Äî IF=1 preemptive `agnsh` re-enabled and now iron-confirmed (`kernel/user/init.cyr`).** kybernet launches `/bin/agnsh` preemptible, so the prompt time-slices against `&` background jobs. The 1.46.2‚Üí1.46.4 IF=0 cooperative ship was a stopgap (never the destination); with the SYSRET fix the IF=1 path is the iron-validated one. The defensive `sched_fix_live_frame` guard (added 1.46.3) is retained but is **not** the fix ‚Äî its CMOS magic never stamped because the `#GP` fired on an *early-return* timer tick upstream of it; the one-byte STAR change is the actual cure. `sched_fix_torn_selector` is likewise retained as belt-and-suspenders.

## [1.46.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-25

**‚òÖ 1.46.4 ‚Äî WORKING COREUTILS (the 1.46.3 boot-to-shell `ls`/argv `#PF` fixed).** The 1.46.3 burn
reached the interactive shell but the first external-tool exec faulted; this cut makes `ls`/`kriya`/
`bnrmr`/`owl` run. Single one-line kernel fix (`pmm.cyr` reserves region 8) for an empty-argv
regression introduced by the 1.46.1 region-7 kstack reservation; root-caused and validated entirely
burn-free in QEMU. Iron re-burn pending. Still pending in the SMP arc: sub-bite-7 **STEP-2**
(`smp_sched_aps=1`, real procs on the woken APs).

### Fixed
- **execwait argv corruption ‚Äî `ls`/coreutils `#PF` after boot-to-shell (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr`).** The 1.46.3 iron burn reached the shell but the first external-tool exec (`ls`‚Üí`kriya`) took a `#PF` (`CR2=0`, CPL3 read, user RIP `0x00400171`). Root-caused **entirely burn-free** in QEMU (the `agnsh-delegation`/`kriya-crash-probe` harness reproduced it single-core): `bnrmr hi` printed its help instead of rendering and `kriya` faulted ‚Äî both got **empty argv** (`argc=0`). Traced via page-table walk: the **1.46.1 region-7 reservation** (per-proc kstack pool, `0xE00000‚Äì0x1000000`) pushed `pmm_alloc_2mb`'s next 2 MB-aligned user-stack allocation to **phys `0x1000000` (region 8)** ‚Äî which the 1.46.1 comment *claimed* to reserve but the code did not. The **boot CR3's PD[8] is not identity** (it resolves VA `0x1000000`‚Üíphys `0xC00000`; `pt_init` maps it identity but an early-boot writer clobbers it ‚Äî boot PD[8]=`0xC00000|0x83` while PD[6/7/9..]=correct identity, and `proc_create_address_space` re-asserts the *per-proc* PD[8..63] identity but not the boot PD). So `elf_load_from_file`'s `store64(stack_phys+off, ‚Ä¶)` SysV-block write (run under boot CR3) landed at phys `0xC03000`, while the child read its real stack page (raw phys `0x1003000` via its own PD[510]) ‚Üí `argv(0)`=null ‚Üí `path_basename_ptr(null)`‚Üí`strlen(null)`‚Üí`#PF`. **Fix:** reserve region 8 in `pmm_init` (the documented-but-missing 1.46.1 intent) so user stacks land at region 9 (phys `0x1200000`, correct identity in BOTH the boot PD and the per-proc PD) ‚Äî the kernel's identity-write and the child's stack mapping agree again. (The stale boot-PD[8] writer is a separate latent bug; region 8 is now unused, so it is inert.) **Validated burn-free:** `agnsh-delegation` PASS (kriya `mkdir`/`cp`/`ls` + owl `cat` + `bnrmr hi` now *renders*), `exec-smoke` argv tests PASS (argv[1] deref + execwait#37 argv-carry), `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke`/`ring3-smoke`/`thread-smoke`/`smp-smoke` (`cpus online: 4`) all green. `build/agnos` 1,225,808 B. Iron re-burn pending.

## [1.46.3] ‚Äî 2026-06-25

**‚òÖ 1.46.3 ‚Äî BOOT-TO-SHELL ON IRON.** The IF=0 cooperative-agnsh ship (`exec_preempt=0`) reached an
interactive shell on real Zen (archaemenid, burn `1461`): past a live DHCP lease, kybernet execs
`/bin/agnsh`, **agnsh 1.7.0 reaches its `[ASSIST] >` prompt and runs `help` + `version`** ‚Äî with SMP
wake left **on** (`smp_wake_enabled=1`, STEP-1 `cpus online: 4`). This is the milestone the whole
1.46.x arc was grinding toward: the IF=1 first-preempt `#GP` (1.46.0‚Äì1.46.2) is *side-stepped*, not yet
solved ‚Äî agnsh resumes via `sysret` (register-only), dodging the inter-privilege ISR `iretq` that the
torn-live-frame ambiguity poisons. **The first external-tool exec faults:** `ls` (‚Üí `kriya`) takes a
**`#PF`** ‚Äî `CR2=0x0`, err `0x05` (protection / read / **CPL3** / data), faulting user RIP `0x00400171`
(= the user code base `0x400000` + `0x171`; a null-pointer read in the freshly-exec'd tool, *not* a
kernel address despite the boot-log decoder's `<16 MB` heuristic). Tracked as the next issue (userland
exec / argv path). **Still pending in the SMP arc:** sub-bite-7 **STEP-2** (`smp_sched_aps=1` ‚Äî real
procs scheduled onto the woken APs); STEP-1 (wake + AP-idle-only) is what this burn validated.

### Changed
- **SHIP boot-to-shell via IF=0 cooperative agnsh (`kernel/user/init.cyr`).** The 1.46.2 torn-selector re-burn FALSIFIED the array-guard hypothesis: the #GP recurred (errcode `0x18`/RIP `0x1bbfbd`/CS `0x08`) with **no `0x59=0x7C` array stamp** ‚Äî so `sched_fix_torn_selector` correctly saw `proc_cs[]`/`proc_ss[]` consistent, yet the timer-ISR `iretq` still faulted. Root cause refined (two diagnosis workflows + a prior-art workflow): the iretq validates the **LIVE on-stack frame** (`isr_rsp+128`/`+152`), which `do_context_switch` writes under the OLD CR3 (`sched.cyr:304`) and the iretq reads under the NEW CR3 after `cr3_load` (`sched.cyr:316` ‚Üí `pic.cyr:147`) ‚Äî a cross-CR3 produce-then-consume the array-guard is blind to. To unblock the boot-to-shell milestone, `exec_preempt = 0` launches agnsh **IF=0 cooperative** (the iron-proven 14115/1439 model; resumes go via sysret, register-only, dodging the inter-privilege ISR iretq). SMP wake is orthogonal and stays on (`cpus online: 4`). `agnsh-smoke` PASS. Flip back to `1` for an IF=1 diagnostic burn.

### Added
- **IF=1 hardening diagnostic: `sched_fix_live_frame` (`kernel/core/sched.cyr`).** Runs as the last statement of `do_context_switch` (the GPR pops touch only `isr_rsp+0..+112`, so `+128`/`+152` are exactly what the iretq loads). Reads the ACTUAL live frame CS/SS; no-ops on a consistent pair (QEMU byte-identical AND inert on the shipped IF=0 path, which only resumes CPL0‚ÜíCPL0 kernel frames); on a torn frame it STAMPS the values to virgin low-bank CMOS `0x63`(CS)/`0x64`(SS)/`0x65`(magic `0x3C`, written last) and REPAIRS the pair from the frame's RIP privilege. The next IF=1 burn (flip `exec_preempt=1`) disambiguates the standing ambiguity ‚Äî magic set + boot-to-shell ‚üπ a cross-CR3 frame tear (repairable; adopt the `cr3_load`-before-restore reorder); no magic + still #GP ‚üπ HW rejects a valid frame at the first inter-privilege ISR iretq; magic set + still #GP ‚üπ escalate to a pre-iretq hand-asm stamp. `agnosticos read-boot-log.cyr` decodes it (gated `0x65==0x3C` + selector-sanity + `fvec!=14`). `agnsh-smoke` PASS (inert). `build/agnos` 1,225,728 B.

## [1.46.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-25

### Fixed
- **SMP sub-bite-7 STEP-1: the IF=1 first-preempt agnsh `#GP` root-caused + fixed (iron diagnostic-burn decoded).** The 1.46.1 re-burn halted right after the agnsh banner with `#GP` errcode `0x18` (selector idx 3 = user-SS), faulting RIP `0x1bbd2d`, CS `0x08`. The RIP maps **into `.bss`** ‚Äî it is the **timer ISR's own final `iretq`** (AGNOS emits ISRs into `.bss` code buffers; `boot_data.cyr var timer_isr[64]` / `pic.cyr:147`) faulting on a **restored frame whose `(CS,SS)` was a torn privilege pair** (`proc_cs[]`/`proc_ss[]` are separate `.bss` arrays, `sched.cyr` save 170-171 / restore 204,208). Surfaced only by the **1.46.x-new combination** ‚Äî woken APs + the BSP's first IF=1-preemptive context switch ‚Äî and is iron-only (QEMU clean across 245k int-lines). **Fix:** `sched_fix_torn_selector` (`kernel/core/sched.cyr`) normalizes the proc's `(CS,SS)` to the consistent pair its RIP privilege implies before the lock-free restore, so the iretq frame is always internally valid; zero-perturbation on a consistent pair (QEMU byte-identical). A desync is stamped to virgin low-bank CMOS `0x56-0x5B` (magic `0x7C`) for next-burn confirmation; `agnosticos read-boot-log.cyr` decodes it. QEMU regression green: `check.sh` 11/11, `ring3-smoke` 8/8, `thread-smoke` 2/2, `smp-smoke` 3/3 (`cpus online: 4`), `agnsh-smoke` PASS. Re-flash + re-burn pending. `build/agnos` 1,225,280 B.

## [1.46.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-25

**‚òÖ 1.46.1 ‚Äî SMP sub-bite-7 STEP-1 #GP root-caused + fixed + QEMU-validated burn-free.** The 1.46.0
STEP-1 iron burn (`smp_wake_enabled=1`, `smp_sched_aps=0`) woke all 4 CPUs (`cpus online: 4` ‚úÖ) but
`agnsh` took a #GP right after its banner (the blue FB fault-canary bar; CMOS `0x54=0x0D`). Three real
STEP-1 bugs were found and fixed ‚Äî and, contrary to the "SMP is iron-only" assumption, all three were
**reproduced and fixed entirely in QEMU `-smp 4`** (a `usb-kbd` + `-d int` harness gave the exact
errcode + faulting `CS:RIP` for free; only the LVT keyboard-death + real-Zen LAPIC timing stay iron-only).

### Fixed
- **AP trampoline never reloaded CS ‚Äî the actual #GP** (`smp.cyr`). The `jmp far 0x18:..` enters 64-bit
  via the *trampoline* GDT (where `0x18` = code64), then `lgdt [kernel GDT]` does **not** reload the cached
  CS ‚Äî so `ap_entry` kept running with `CS=0x18`, which in the kernel GDT is **user DS**. Every AP timer
  frame then pushed `CS=0x18`, `proc_save_context` persisted it into the idle's `proc_cs`, and the resume
  `iretq` reloaded `CS=0x18` against the kernel GDT ‚Üí **#GP (errcode `0x18`)** on each AP. **Fix:** after
  RSP is valid, a far return (`lretq`) installs the kernel code64 descriptor (`CS=0x08`) so each AP runs
  ring-0 correctly. (Reproduced 3/3 under QEMU `-smp 4`; iretq frame showed `CS=0x18`, GDT pristine.)
- **An idle-only AP fell back to proc 0 (kmain)** (`sched.cyr`) ‚Äî exposed once the CS fix let the APs run
  far enough to schedule. `sched_next`'s final `return 0` ran *kmain* on two CPUs at once (shared proc-0
  state + the BSP's kernel stack) ‚Üí a `#PF`/`#GP`/`#UD` cascade during kybernet init. **Fix:** a non-BSP
  CPU with nothing else ready returns **its own idle** (`pcpu_cpu()!=0 ‚Üí sched_idle_pid_get()`), so
  `do_context_switch` sees `new==old` and early-outs ‚Äî the AP simply keeps idling. The BSP keeps the
  proc-0 fallback (the Attempt-14 sub-case-3b anti-starvation rule). Correct for STEP-2 too.
- **AP-bring-up vs proc-table-bootstrap boot-order race** (`main.cyr`/`smp.cyr`). `smp_start_aps` returned
  on `ap_online_mask` (set early in `ap_entry`) while APs were still registering their idle kthreads, then
  the `proc_count = 0` + `proc_on_cpu_init()` bootstrap (~800 lines later) **wiped** those slots and reset
  `on_cpu`. **Fix:** wake the APs **after** the proc-table bootstrap (relocated the `smp_start_aps()` call
  to just before `sched_active = 1`), and make `smp_start_aps` wait on `pcpu_ap_ready` (set last in
  `ap_entry`) so the scheduler arms only once every AP is fully built.
- **Validation (burn-free):** QEMU `-smp 4` interactive `agnsh` ‚Äî prompt reached, a `&` background job
  runs to completion while the prompt stays live, repeated `version` answered under preemption, **zero
  guest faults** (the `#GP(0x18)` and the `#PF/#GP/#UD` cascade both gone). `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke`,
  `ring3-smoke` (5/5), `thread-smoke`, `smp-smoke` (`-smp 4`) all green; all three fixes are inert
  single-core. `build/agnos` 1,221,072 B. Still iron-gated at the flip: the AP LVT LINT0/LINT1
  keyboard-death efficacy, the real-Zen LAPIC tick rate, and the idle double-run guard ‚Äî the next burn.

### Added
- **SMP arc sub-bite 6 ‚Äî AP runs the scheduler** (`smp.cyr`/`sched.cyr`/`proc.cyr`/`main.cyr`/`pic.cyr`).
  The scheduler is now SMP-safe (gated off ‚Äî `smp_wake_enabled=0` ‚Äî so APs stay parked and single-core
  is byte-identical; the wake flip is sub-bite 7, iron-gated). Designed by a 10-agent adversarial workflow,
  implemented + 3-lens-reviewed (all SOUND), re-verified on main.
  - **6a (sched_lock + on_cpu ‚Äî single-core validated):** a fixed-global `sched_lock` serializes the
    scheduler. The hard part: `do_context_switch` returns normally but the *new* proc is entered by the
    timer ISR's `iretq` one frame up (pic.cyr) ‚Äî so the lock is **released before the restore**, with a
    per-proc `on_cpu` fence (`on_cpu_set(new, my_cpu)` is the last mutation under the lock; `sched_next`
    skips any proc with `on_cpu != -1`) preventing two CPUs from running the same proc across the
    released-lock window. Every post-acquire exit unlocks (2 in `do_context_switch`, 3 in `sys_sched_yield`)
    ‚Äî a dropped unlock deadlocks the 2nd context switch, which the smoke battery proves it doesn't. The
    `#44` yield path got the same treatment, and its global `yield_frame` is now per-CPU `pcpu_yield_frame`.
  - **6b (AP `ap_entry` loop ‚Äî inert):** `smp_sched_aps` STEP-1/STEP-2 kill-switch, `pcpu_ap_ready`, a
    persistent `idle_owner` array (a descheduled AP idle stays excluded from foreign CPUs even after its
    `on_cpu` clears), `sched_idx_pickable` (foreign-idle + STEP-1-AP skips, both no-ops single-core), the
    rewritten `ap_entry` (AP idle kthread created ABOVE `sched_lock` per the lock order, AP LVT LINT0/LINT1
    masked, LAPIC timer armed with a real-Zen calibration TODO, idle-yield loop), and `timer_handler`
    BSP-gating (`timer_ticks`/`net_rx_drain` only on the BSP; `do_context_switch` runs per-CPU).
  - Validated on main: agnsh-smoke, check.sh 11/11, ring3-smoke (#44 yield Y=12 = baseline), thread-smoke,
    exec-smoke, smp-smoke (APs parked) ‚Äî all green. The idle double-run guard, the GAP-C reap-vs-restore
    ordering, the AP timer calibration, and the LVT-mask keyboard-death efficacy are all **iron-gated**
    (validated at the sub-bite-7 flip). `build/agnos` 1,220,272 B.
  - **Only sub-bite 7 remains in the SMP arc** ‚Äî the iron-gated wake flip (`smp_wake_enabled=1` +
    `smp_sched_aps`-staged burns on archaemenid). (Also still open in sub-bite 5: `input_lock` (blocked on
    the input-path conflict) + the net cli/sti family (net-revisit scope).)
- **SMP arc sub-bite 7 prep ‚Äî the two pre-STEP-2 code gates landed** (`proc.cyr`/`apic.cyr`/`smp.cyr`).
  The flip itself is still iron-gated, but the two code items it depended on are now in the binary +
  smoke-validated, so the next burn is build-ready:
  - **GAP-C reap-vs-restore fence** (`proc.cyr`). A new `proc_reap_off_cpu_fence(pid)` ‚Äî called by BOTH
    `proc_reap` and `proc_reap_child` AFTER `proc_set_state(pid,0)` and BEFORE `proc_free_address_space`
    ‚Äî spins until the proc's `on_cpu` clears, so a reaper can never free an address space a foreign CPU
    is mid `proc_restore_context` on (the STEP-2 race: child exits on an AP, parent reaps on the BSP
    before the AP descheduled). **Inert single-core**: the reaped proc is always descheduled (`on_cpu==-1`)
    before any reap path runs (foreground reaps run cooperatively + never schedule the proc;
    `proc_reap_child` refuses the running proc, so a context switch ‚Äî which clears `on_cpu` ‚Äî already
    happened), so the loop never iterates. ring3-smoke's spawn#3‚Üíwaitpid#4‚Üíreap + 8-proc + non-LIFO
    slot-reuse paths run straight through it without deadlock.
  - **AP LAPIC timer count inherits the BSP's configured reload** (`apic.cyr`/`smp.cyr`). `apic_timer_init`
    now records its `count` in a `lapic_timer_count` global; `ap_entry` arms its timer with THAT instead of
    a separately-hardcoded `10000000`, so every CPU's scheduler tick runs at the BSP's exact, iron-proven
    rate (no AP/BSP skew). **Why not measure the LAPIC frequency:** the BSP timebase *is* the LAPIC timer
    (pic.cyr masks the legacy PIT IRQ0 once it owns the timebase), so `timer_ticks` is driven by it ‚Äî any
    reprogram-to-measure freezes `timer_ticks` and deadlocks; a PIT-channel-2 port-0x61 readback variant
    additionally hung under QEMU q35 (that emulation doesn't drive the ch2 OUT bit ‚Äî caught by a `-smp 4`
    QEMU boot before the burn). Inheriting the BSP's working count sidesteps both. (True Hz-calibration, if
    ever wanted, is a separate enhancement to the BSP timebase, not AP-side.)
  - Validated: production build (`smp_wake_enabled=0`) `build/agnos` 1,220,416 B, check.sh 11/11,
    agnsh-smoke, ring3-smoke (8/8), thread-smoke, smp-smoke (APs parked, boot continuity) ‚Äî all green; the
    GAP-C fence is exercised by ring3's spawn/waitpid/reap. The STEP-1 burn artifact (`smp_wake_enabled=1`,
    `smp_sched_aps=0`) boots clean under QEMU `-smp 4` to the agnsh prompt (`cpus online: 4`, APs parked).
    Still iron-gated at the flip: the LVT-mask keyboard-death efficacy + the idle double-run guard.

## [1.46.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-24

**‚òÖ 1.46.0 ‚Äî the SMP locking foundation.** The first five sub-bites of the SMP / multi-threading arc:
per-CPU scheduler identity (`pcpu_*` APIC-indexed) + the **per-CPU SYSCALL stub** (per-CPU kernel stack,
user-RSP save, KPTI CR3 pair, #44 capture block, #37 guard ‚Äî the arc's hardest, adversarially designed +
reviewed + objdump-verified) + the **subsystem lock set** (xchg spinlocks: heap/pmm/vfs/proctab/fs/nvme/
ahci/console) with the `proc_alloc_slot` claiming sentinel and the FS/NVMe race closure. Everything is
**single-core byte-identical** (a `cpu<4` cap lands the BSP on slot 0) and the AP wake stays **gated off**
(`smp_wake_enabled=0`) ‚Äî the AP-runs-scheduler flip (sub-bites 6‚Äì7) is iron-gated and continues after this
cut. Remaining in sub-bite 5: `input_lock` (kb_buf, blocked on an input-path memory‚Üîcode conflict) + the
net cli/sti family (net-revisit scope). Root-caused along the way: a broken parameterized `spin_lock_at`
that xchg'd address 1 (QEMU-monitor), and the timer ISR's hidden TX path.

### Added
- **1.46.x cycle opened ‚Äî per-process kernel stacks ‚Üí true preemptive multitasking on iron
  (the SMP / multi-threading revisit).** The 1.44.x multi-threading arc's deferred namesake,
  promoted to its own cycle. Each process gets its own kernel stack instead of the shared serial
  `syscall_kstack_top` / TSS `RSP0`, which unblocks together: **IF=1 preemptive `agnsh` on iron**
  (re-enable the `exec_preempt` gate the 1.44.x iron path runs cooperatively), **blocking `waitpid`**
  (yield mid-wait without clobbering a suspended kernel frame), and the rejected Option-A
  blocking-read-yield. Folds in the **SMP-AP wake re-enable + iron-validate** (gated off at 1.44.25,
  `smp_wake_enabled=0`) and the four 1.44.22 audit-deferred cleanups (loader DRY-up, `sched_next`
  single-pass, the `waitpid`#4 ownership gate via real ppid tracking, MADT AP-enum).
- The arc is intentionally open-ended ‚Äî the **1.46.x minor grows to fit the work**. Plan:
  `docs/development/roadmap.md` ¬ß 1.46.x; iron hypotheses + falsification: agnosticos
  `iron-nuc-zen-log.md` `#tracker-146x-cycle`.
- **Bite 1 ‚Äî per-process TSS.RSP0 kernel stacks (QEMU-validated; iron-pending).** Each proc slot
  gets its own kernel interrupt stack (`proc_rsp0[16]`, `proc_rsp0_top`), assigned in
  `proc_alloc_slot` from a dedicated pool ‚Äî **region 7 (phys 0xE00000‚Äì0x1000000 = 14‚Äì16 MB)**,
  reserved in `pmm_init`. `do_context_switch`, `sys_sched_yield`, and `exec_and_wait` now point
  `TSS.RSP0` at the switched-to proc's own stack (after the `cr3_load`, for the APIC-read rule),
  so a preempted ring-3 proc takes its CPL3‚ÜíCPL0 timer tick on **its own** stack instead of the
  shared `0x3C0000`. **Scoped to RSP0 only** ‚Äî the SYSCALL-entry stack stays shared this bite
  (per-proc syscall stack is deferred to the blocking-`waitpid` bite, where the global SYSRET
  cells move per-proc as one coherent unit; an adversarial design review showed splitting them
  would break the execwait#37 second-stack invariant and clobber `r10`/`&kernel_rsp_save` in the
  SYSCALL stub). **Pool placement:** region 7 sits at the top of the 0‚Äì16 MB boot identity map
  (PD[7]), so it is supervisor-mapped under the boot CR3 *and* every per-proc CR3 (which copies
  PD[0..127]); region 8 (16 MB) is unusable because the boot `vmm_alloc_at(0x1000000)` probe
  remaps PD[8]. **Behavior-preserving on single-core / IF=0** (RSP0 is consumed only on a CPL
  change; the deep FS path stays on the unchanged syscall kstack), so this is inert until
  `exec_preempt=1` (a later bite) makes agnsh preemptive. Validated: `ring3-smoke` 8/8 (IF=1 procs
  take real preempts onto distinct region-7 stacks ‚Äî a misplaced stack would #DF the preempt),
  `agnsh-smoke` PASS, `check.sh` 11/11, `thread-smoke` PASS. `build/agnos` 1,210,368 B.
- **Bite 2 ‚Äî IF=1 preemptive `agnsh` re-enabled (`exec_preempt=1`, `kernel/user/init.cyr`).** agnsh
  again launches preemptible (IF=1) so the 100 Hz timer time-slices its prompt against `&` background
  jobs (`enter_ring3` consumes the flag, so only agnsh's launch is preemptive; foreground
  execwait#37 / `run /bin/X` stay IF=0). This is the milestone 1.44.23 reverted ‚Äî the first
  CPL3‚ÜíCPL0 timer tick took its ISR frame on the shared `RSP0=0x3C0000` which sat in live kernel
  `.bss`, freezing archaemenid at the banner. Bite 1's per-proc region-7 RSP0 is the hypothesized
  fix. Validated in QEMU: `agnsh-bg-test` (sleeper & ‚Äî prompt stays live, reaps `[1] Done`),
  `agnsh-multijob-test` (2 concurrent jobs, out-of-order reap), `agnsh-smoke`, `check.sh` 11/11.
  **Iron-pending** ‚Äî QEMU can't reproduce the Zen-specific freeze, so the next burn is the real test.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 1 ‚Äî restored the xchg spinlock primitive** (`kernel/core/pmm.cyr`). `pmm_spin_lock`/
  `pmm_spin_unlock` were no-op'd at 1.42.7 for perf (~+58% on `pmm_alloc_free`) "until the SMP arc" ‚Äî that
  time is now. Restored to the iron-proven xchg test-and-set on `&pmm_lock`, structured identically to
  smp.cyr's AP-checkin `spin_lock` (same locals, no parameter ‚Üí the hardcoded `[rbp-N]` frame offsets are
  guaranteed correct; verified byte-identical in the binary). The ~14 PMM call sites were already intact, so
  this is the one-place restoration the 1.42.7 no-op was designed for. Single-core behavior-preserving (the
  xchg always wins uncontended), APs still parked. This is the locking-primitive foundation for the rest of
  the SMP arc ‚Äî the full 7-sub-bite decomposition (per-CPU scheduler identity ‚Üí lock shared structures ‚Üí
  per-CPU syscall stub ‚Üí lock FS/devices ‚Üí AP-runs-scheduler ‚Üí flip) + the design review's mandatory
  corrections are recorded in `docs/development/smp-arc-plan.md`. `check.sh` 11/11, agnsh-smoke PASS.
  `build/agnos` 1,211,424 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 2 ‚Äî per-CPU scheduler identity** (`proc.cyr`/`sched.cyr` + ~70 call sites). The single
  globals `proc_current`, `preempt_count`, and `sched_idle_pid` became per-CPU arrays (`pcpu_curproc[4]`/
  `pcpu_preempt[4]`/`pcpu_idle[4]`) indexed by APIC id, with `*_get()`/`*_set()` accessors. **Single-core fast
  path:** while `cpu_count < 2` (every state up to the sub-bite-7 wake flip) the accessors hit slot 0 with NO
  APIC read ‚Üí byte-for-byte the old globals. The old global *names were removed* so any unconverted read fails
  to COMPILE (the design review's forcing-function ‚Äî it caught the full ~70-site set; build is clean). `kpti_*`
  deliberately stay global until sub-bite 4 (the syscall stub reads them via baked imm64 ‚Äî they can't be arrays
  until it's regenerated). APs still parked. Validated: `agnsh-smoke` PASS, `check.sh` 11/11, `ring3-smoke` 8/8
  (multi-proc preemption exercises `proc_current` through every context switch + the per-CPU preempt gate +
  idle deprioritization). `build/agnos` 1,212,336 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 3 ‚Äî `heap_lock` + `vfs_lock` (fixed-global xchg spinlocks)** (`smp.cyr`/`heap.cyr`/`vfs.cyr`).
  `heap_lock` now serializes the slab free-list RMW in `kmalloc` + `kfree_sized` (and `slab_grow`, which
  holds heap then takes `pmm_lock` ‚Üí heap<pmm leaf-last; `slab_grow` calls only `pmm_alloc`/`vmm_map`, so
  no allocator re-entry). `vfs_lock` replaces `vfs_sec_wfile_alloc_slot`'s `preempt_disable` (a SAME-CPU
  reentrancy gate with ZERO cross-CPU exclusion) with a real spinlock.
  - **Fixed a real deadlock bug.** The first wiring used a *parameterized* `spin_lock_at(addr)`. cyrius
    spills the param to the stack frame, which shifts the `old`/`addr` locals off the asm's hardcoded
    `[rbp-N]` offsets ‚Äî so it `xchg`'d **address 1** (a bogus low address) instead of the lock variable.
    It locked nothing real: it "passed" for `vfs_lock` only because `[1]` happened to be 0, and it
    DEADLOCKED in `kmalloc` once `[1]` became 1 (boot hung right after the FAT mount). Root-caused with
    the QEMU monitor (`info registers` at the hang: RIP inside the spin loop, RAX=1 = the xchg target).
    **Fix:** one FIXED-GLOBAL lock-fn pair per lock (`heap_spin_lock`/`heap_spin_unlock`,
    `vfs_spin_lock`/`vfs_spin_unlock`), each structurally identical to the proven no-parameter
    `pmm_spin_lock` so the frame offsets are correct. The parameterized `spin_lock_at`/`spin_unlock_at`
    were removed.
  - **Validated:** agnsh-smoke PASS, check.sh 11/11, ring3-smoke PASS, fat-write-smoke PASS. APs still
    parked (the flip is sub-bite 7). `build/agnos` 1,212,736 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 3 ‚Äî `proctab_lock` + a claiming sentinel** (`smp.cyr`/`proc.cyr`). `proc_alloc_slot`'s
  scan-for-free + claim + `proc_count`-bump now run under `proctab_lock` (fixed-global), and the chosen
  slot is marked with a **CLAIMING sentinel (state=3)** before the lock releases. A bare lock fixes the
  count-bump race but NOT the dead-slot-reuse race ‚Äî `proc_alloc_slot` returned an *unclaimed* slot (state
  still 0), so a second CPU's scan (which reuses only state==0) would re-grab the same index before the
  caller (`proc_create`/`proc_create_full`) set state=1; the sentinel makes the slot invisible to both the
  scan and `sched_next` (which selects only state==1) until it's initialized. State machine: 0=dead/free ¬∑
  1=ready ¬∑ 2=running ¬∑ 3=claiming. The reap-path `proc_count` decrements (`proc_reap`/`proc_reap_child`)
  are also under the lock. Inert single-core (the sentinel is overwritten before any scheduler runs);
  validated as no-regression by ring3-smoke (non-LIFO slot reuse + ‚â•8-proc stress) + thread-smoke +
  check.sh 11/11 + agnsh-smoke. Completes sub-bite 3's global-structure locks (heap/vfs/proctab);
  `sched_lock` folds into sub-bite 6/7. `build/agnos` 1,213,024 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 4a ‚Äî split `syscall_init`** (`kernel/arch/x86_64/syscall_hw.cyr`). The SYSCALL setup
  is now `syscall_msr_init()` (per-CPU: STAR/LSTAR/SFMASK/EFER ‚Äî an AP will run this directly at bring-up
  without rebuilding the shared stub) + `syscall_stub_build()` (BSP-once: the raw-byte stub emission) +
  `syscall_init()` (BSP orchestrator: kstack-reserve ‚Üí msr-init ‚Üí IBRS-detect ‚Üí stub-build). Pure refactor,
  **stub bytes unchanged**. The one frame-offset hazard ‚Äî the IBRS check's `mov [rbp-0x08],rdx` cpuid
  capture ‚Äî is kept correct by leaving `cpuid_edx` as `syscall_init`'s SOLE local (the proven `spin_unlock`
  sole-local pattern); **objdump-verified**: the asm writes `%rdx,-0x8(%rbp)` and the C reads
  `-0x8(%rbp),%rax` ‚Äî offsets match, IBRS detection intact. agnsh-smoke / check.sh 11/11 / ring3-smoke (#44
  yield) green. First step of the adversarially-reviewed sub-bite 4 (per-CPU SYSCALL stub); the dangerous
  per-CPU stub-byte conversion (4b-4d) follows, gated by objdump-after-each-step. `build/agnos` 1,211,472 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 4b ‚Äî per-CPU SYSCALL kernel stack + user-RSP save** (`syscall_hw.cyr`/`sched.cyr`/`syscall.cyr`).
  The two values the SYSCALL entry stub baked as single globals ‚Äî `kernel_rsp_save` (the user-RSP save slot)
  and `syscall_kstack_top` (the kernel stack) ‚Äî are now **per-CPU arrays** indexed by the (capped) APIC cpu
  id the stub RE-READS at each consumer (`r8*8`/`rax*8 + &array`), so two CPUs in the stub at once no longer
  clobber one save slot or grow down one stack. Per-CPU kstacks live in region 7 (`0xF10000`/`0xF90000 +
  cpu*0x10000`, pmm-reserved, supervisor-mapped via the PD[0..127] copy). The chosen design re-reads the APIC
  in three windows (entry‚Üír8, off-100‚Üírax, exit‚Üír8) rather than carrying a base across `call syscall_handler`,
  which **dissolves the old r10-must-survive hazard** entirely. The ew37 #37 nested run swaps THIS cpu's slot
  to its second kstack top instead of rebuilding the shared stub (rebuild removed). All five C readers/writers
  (`sched.cyr:348` #44-yield, `syscall.cyr` ew37 save/restore/swap) moved in lockstep via a capped `pcpu_cpu()`.
  - **Process:** implemented in an isolated worktree from a 10-agent adversarially-reviewed spec, then
    re-reviewed through four lenses (byte-decode, register-survival, C-site-lockstep, memory-residency) ‚Äî all
    SOUND, zero fatal bugs. **Runtime-buffer objdump** confirmed every `jb` lands exactly past its `xor` and
    every disp32 emits with `store32(+4)` (the buffer-corrupting `store64(+8)` trap avoided). Re-verified on
    main: agnsh-smoke + check.sh 11/11 + ring3-smoke (#44 yield reads the per-CPU slot) + exec-smoke (#37
    nested swap-back) all green. SMP isolation under contention stays iron-gated (TCG can't race; cpu<4 cap
    makes single-core byte-identical-behavior). `build/agnos` 1,215,232 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 4c ‚Äî per-CPU KPTI CR3 pair + SYSRET-capture block** (`syscall_hw.cyr`/`sched.cyr`/`ring3.cyr`/`syscall.cyr`/`proc.cyr`).
  The last three SYSCALL-path globals go per-CPU: `kpti_kernel_cr3`/`kpti_user_cr3` (the CR3 pair the stub
  installs on entry/exit) ‚Üí `pcpu_kpti_*[4]`, and `sc_entry_regs` (the 8-cell rcx/r11/rbx/rbp/r12-r15 #44
  capture block) ‚Üí `pcpu_sc_entry_regs[32]` (8 cells √ó 4 CPU, cpu c at byte c*64). Stub points 1/6 become
  `mov r10,[r8*8+disp32]; mov cr3,r10`; point 2 becomes `mov r10,r8; shl r10,6` (√ó64 stride) then eight
  `[r10+disp32]` capture stores. The C readers reuse the existing `+0..+56` offsets verbatim via a
  `sc_scr_base()` helper that folds in `pcpu_cpu()*64` (the load-bearing per-CPU byte-bias). All readers/
  writers (`sched.cyr` context-switch + #44 yield, `ring3.cyr` exec + cr3_load, syscall.cyr ew37 restore)
  were converted in lockstep via the **delete-the-global compile-fail forcing function** ‚Äî grep confirms
  zero bare `kpti_*`/`sc_entry_regs` refs remain. **This completes the per-CPU SYSCALL-stub conversion
  (4a split + 4b kstack/RSP + 4c CR3/capture).**
  - **Process:** worktree-implemented from the verified spec, four-lens adversarial review (byte-decode,
    register-survival/r8-at-point6, c-site-lockstep/sc-bias, residency/kpti-collapse) ‚Äî all SOUND, zero
    fatal bugs. Both failure modes are **TCG-visible** (a wrong `+cpu*64` bias reads CR3 as RIP/RFLAGS; a
    `store64(+8)` disp shifts the stream), so ring3-smoke #44 yield (8/8) is decisive. Runtime-buffer
    objdump confirmed the encodings (`4E 8B 14 C5`, `shl r10,6`, `41 0F 22 DA` not `44‚Ä¶`). Re-verified on
    main: agnsh + check.sh 11/11 + ring3 + exec all green. `build/agnos` 1,215,408 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 4d ‚Äî per-CPU execwait#37 guard** (`syscall.cyr`/`sched.cyr`). The single `ew37_busy`
  re-entrancy guard (1 while a #37 nested exec is in flight) becomes `pcpu_ew37_busy[4]` with capped
  `pcpu_ew37_busy_get()`/`_set()` accessors, so each CPU tracks its own nested-exec state. All 6 sites
  (`syscall.cyr` √ó5 + `sched.cyr:333` #44-yield gate) converted via the delete-the-global forcing function
  (grep-clean). The ew37 `kernel_rsp_save`/`kpti_*` snapshot/restore already uses `pcpu_*[cpu]` (4b/4c);
  the remaining `ew37_*` scratch (cr3/cpid/code/path) stays global ‚Äî deferred-conservative, since only a
  post-flip simultaneous nest collides (inert single-core). C-only, stub bytes byte-identical to 4c.
  agnsh + check.sh 11/11 + ring3 (#44) + exec (#37) green. `build/agnos` 1,215,584 B.
  **‚òÖ This completes sub-bite 4 ‚Äî the per-CPU SYSCALL stub (4a split + 4b kstack/RSP + 4c CR3/capture + 4d
  guard), the SMP arc's hardest piece, is fully done. Remaining: sub-bite 5 (lock FS/devices/console),
  6 (AP runs the scheduler), 7 (the iron-gated wake flip).**
- **SMP arc sub-bite 5 PHASE 1 ‚Äî coarse non-ISR subsystem locks** (`smp.cyr`/`vfs.cyr`/`syscall.cyr`/`nvme.cyr`/`ahci.cyr`/`kprint.cyr`/`net_icmp.cyr`).
  A 4-subsystem locking-map workflow classified every shared mutable structure on the FS/block/net/console
  paths by ISR-exposure and produced a verified plan (global lock order: `fs < tcp < vfs < proctab <
  console < input < nvme < ahci < heap < pmm`). PHASE 1 wires the four **pure non-ISR plain spinlocks**
  (grep-proved no timer/`net_rx_drain`/xHCI/fault handler touches them ‚Üí no cli/sti, INERT single-core):
  **`fs_lock`** (one coarse lock at the VFS-dispatch boundary covering all ext2/fat/exfat scratch buffers +
  jbd2 + caches, held across blocking device polls ‚Äî safe precisely because no ISR touches FS), **`nvme_lock`**
  + **`ahci_lock`** (whole-controller, polling-only), **`console_lock`** (at `kprint`/`kputc`/`kprintln`).
  The fs_lock re-entry safety rests on a call-graph proof (the ext2/fat/exfat backends are leaves w.r.t. the
  VFS boundary; the lone `vfs_close‚Üíflush‚Üívfs_write_on` nest is single-acquire because `vfs_write_on` stays a
  helper) and the console_lock on the "no ISR calls kprint" invariant ‚Äî which was actually **restored** by
  routing two ISR-reachable `net_handle_icmp` diagnostics to `serial_println` (a real latent issue the map
  surfaced). Lock fns are byte-copies of the proven no-param `pmm_spin_lock`.
  - **Process:** worktree-implemented incrementally (build + agnsh-smoke after each lock so a deadlock
    isolates), three-lens adversarial review (re-entry/nesting, order/missed-unlock, console-invariant/
    inertness) ‚Äî all SOUND, zero fatal bugs. Re-verified on main: agnsh + check.sh 11/11 + exec (FS write
    through fs_lock + device locks) + fat-write (the vfs flush nest) + ring3 all green. The map flagged the
    **timer ISR actually transmits** (so several net structures need cli/sti ‚Äî net-revisit scope) and a few
    future-SMP races (link#32, nvme fast path, lseek#58) to fix before the wake flip; both inert now.
    **Remaining sub-bite 5: the ISR-touched `input_lock` (kb_buf, cli/sti) + net cli/sti family.**
    `build/agnos` 1,218,464 B.
- **SMP arc sub-bite 5 ‚Äî close the FS/NVMe future-SMP races** (`syscall.cyr`/`nvme.cyr`). The PHASE-1 review
  flagged three shared-scratch races that were inert single-core but corrupt under the wake flip; closed
  now while the locks are fresh: (1) **link#32 + stat#33** ran `ext2_path_lookup`/`ext2_link`/`ext2_fill_stat`
  on the shared ext2 scratch with no `fs_lock` ‚Äî now wrapped (the validation above touches no scratch, the
  backends are leaves ‚Üí single-acquire). (2) **`nvme_blk_read_sectors` fast path** read into the shared
  bounce page via `nvme_read_sectors` then memcpy'd OUTSIDE that lock (another CPU could clobber the page
  between) ‚Äî now one atomic `nvme_lock` section using the unlocked `nvme_rw_internal` primitive (mirrors
  `nvme_blk_read`, no re-entry). (3) **`nvme_write_sectors`** was unlocked despite submitting to the shared
  IO queue ‚Äî now under `nvme_lock` (unlock before the error kprint, console < nvme). All inert single-core;
  agnsh (nvme fast-path) + check.sh 11/11 + exec (FS write/link/stat) green. `lseek#58` left as a noted
  lower-risk per-FD (not shared-scratch) race. `build/agnos` 1,218,576 B.

### Added (from the kernel-gap issue backlog)
- **`exec_redirect`#62 ‚Äî fd-redirect for output capture** (`kernel/core/syscall.cyr`). Arms a
  ONE-SHOT redirect consumed by the next `execwait`#37: the child's writes to `src_fd` (e.g.
  1=stdout) route to `dst_fd`'s backend (an open writable file/pipe), so a parent can run a tool
  and **capture its stdout**, then read it back after #37 returns. Implemented as a save/swap/
  restore of the global `vfs_table` entry around the run-to-completion child run (`exec_redirect_apply`/
  `exec_redirect_restore` bracket the `exec_and_wait` call) ‚Äî no per-proc fd layer needed, and
  byte-identical to the prior #37 when nothing is armed. Resolves the **"high-value one"** in
  `docs/development/issues/2026-06-15-cyrius-stdlib-missing-syscalls.md` group 1 (the `dup2`/fd-redirect
  gap that blocked cyrius's regression test-runner capture + shakti session logging). Not applied to
  the non-blocking `spawn`#3 (the child runs later ‚Üí a per-proc redirect is the follow-on). Validated:
  new `EXEC_REDIRECT_SELFTEST` + `scripts/exec-redirect-smoke.sh` boot-prove the capture end-to-end via
  a pipe (`redir: capture OK` ‚Äî write to a redirected fd, read it back from the pipe); `agnsh-smoke`
  PASS (the #37 `run` path unchanged), `check.sh` 11/11. `build/agnos` 1,211,392 B.

## [1.45.17] ‚Äî 2026-06-23

### Fixed
- **NIC RX ring is now drained from the timer ISR ‚Äî fixes real-hardware packet loss**
  (`kernel/arch/x86_64/pic.cyr` `timer_handler` + `kernel/core/net_ingress.cyr`). The RX ring was
  serviced **only** while a ring-3 tool sat in its own `net_poll()` loop. During `sleep_ms` ping
  pacing, between commands, and at the idle prompt the r8169 ring went unserviced, so on a live LAN
  broadcast/multicast chatter overran the 64-deep ring (`missed`=FIFO-overflow) and the NIC dropped
  frames ‚Äî **including the tools' DNS/ICMP/TCP replies**. Iron signature (1.45.16 burn `14516_*`):
  `yo google.com` ‚Üí 2/4, **50% loss**; `dig google.com` ‚Üí "no servers could be reached"; `whirl
  https://google.com` ‚Üí "connection failed". The bug was **invisible in QEMU** because SLIRP is
  point-to-point and chatter-free, so the ring never filled ‚Äî the "passes in QEMU, drops on iron" gap.
- **Fix:** the 100 Hz `timer_handler` now calls a new `net_rx_drain()` every tick, so the ring stays
  drained regardless of userland activity. `net_poll()` keeps its exact prior contract (TX retransmits
  + RX drain) for all existing tool/recv-loop callers; `net_rx_drain()` is the RX-only inner loop,
  factored out so the ISR issues **no TX** from interrupt context. A `net_poll_busy` re-entrancy guard
  makes a timer tick that lands mid-drain (tool poll loops run IF=1 in the `sleep_ms` sti-window) a
  no-op rather than re-enter and corrupt the shared `net_rx_pkt` / `r8169_rx_idx`; single-core + a
  synchronous ISR ‚áí the flag fully serializes the two callers. Drains before `do_context_switch`
  (kernel context established at ISR entry, pre-CR3-swap) and is `nic_ready()`-gated (no-op until the
  NIC is up ‚Üí early-boot ticks safe). **Validation:** QEMU regression-clean ‚Äî boot-to-shell, `yo`/`dig`
  0% loss on virtio, both DNS paths NOERROR, `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` PASS. The 50%‚Üí0% on real
  hardware confirms on the next archaemenid burn (kernel-only change ‚Äî rides `install-usb.sh --update`,
  no agnos-fs mount-copy).

## [1.45.16] ‚Äî 2026-06-23

### Added
- **`net_config(field)` syscall (#61)** (`kernel/core/syscall.cyr`) ‚Äî a non-blocking, buffer-less
  getter (same shape as `uptime_ms`#40 / `winsize`#60) returning a kernel network-config datum in
  `rax`: field `0`=`net_ip`, `1`=`net_netmask`, `2`=`net_gateway`, `3`=`net_dns_server` (the
  DHCP option-6 resolver, else the leased gateway). Each is a packed IPv4 in `[0, 0xFFFFFFFF]`
  (`0`=unconfigured, `-1`=unknown field); IF=0-safe pure read of the `net.cyr` globals populated by
  `net_dhcp.cyr` on ACK. **Why:** lets a ring-3 resolver target the DHCP-leased **on-subnet**
  resolver instead of a hardcoded **off-subnet** public IP (1.1.1.1 / 8.8.8.8) ‚Äî the off-subnet
  fallback needs working gateway-MAC egress + a reachable public resolver, the QEMU-passed /
  iron-failed gap that froze `yo google.com` / `whirl https://google.com` on real hardware (QEMU's
  `dig` smoke used an explicit on-subnet `@10.0.2.3`, never exercising the fallback). A cyrius
  `sys_net_config` wrapper is requested (`cyrius/docs/development/issues/2026-06-23-agnos-net-config-syscall-wrapper.md`)
  so consumers drop the interim raw `syscall(61, ‚Ä¶)`.
- **Net-path fault canary** (diagnostic, FB-over-CMOS ‚Äî strip once the net path is iron-clean):
  - `fb_dbg_beacon(code)` (`kernel/arch/x86_64/fb_console.cyr`) paints a 32√ó32 px color-coded square
    in the top-right FB corner at each low-frequency net-syscall stage ‚Äî `net_config`#61 (green),
    `udp_send`#52 / DNS tx (yellow), `sock_connect`#47 (orange), `sock_send`#48 / TLS+HTTP (cyan),
    `icmp_echo`#55 / ping (magenta) ‚Äî so the **last net stage a frozen tool reached is visible on the
    burn photo**. Normal kernel context (FB mapped under the per-process CR3), not the recv poll loops.
  - The 7 CPU-exception stubs (`kernel/arch/x86_64/idt.cyr`) now paint a wide FB bar (`rep stosd`, one
    microcoded instruction) **after** their CMOS vector stamp, before `cli; hlt` ‚Äî a **caught fault is
    now visible on the framebuffer** (bar across the top), distinguishing a **FAULT** from a **SPIN**
    (only the corner beacon shows). The CMOS stamp is written first, so the paint can only add a
    signal, never lose the vector/CR2/RIP; a null-guard (`fb_dbg_phys`) skips the paint pre-console.

### Changed
- **`taar` (whirl) + `yo` DNS resolver discovery prefers the kernel-leased resolver on AGNOS.**
  `taar`'s `_taar_resolv_discover` (`src/socket.cyr` `_taar_plat_dns_server` + `src/dns.cyr`) and
  `yo`'s `_dns_choose_nameserver` (`src/platform_agnos.cyr` `platform_dns_server` + `src/dns.cyr`) now
  call `net_config(3)` first and use it when `> 0`, falling back to `/etc/resolv.conf` then the public
  IP only when the kernel reports no lease. Linux backends return `0`, so the resolv.conf path is
  unchanged there. (Requires the new tools staged onto the agnos-fs `/bin` alongside the #61 kernel.)

## [1.45.15] ‚Äî 2026-06-23

### Fixed
- **ext2/ext4 multi-chunk BGDT walk ‚Äî inodes & blocks beyond the first block-group-descriptor-table
  chunk are now reachable** (`kernel/core/ext2.cyr`). The Phase-1 driver only ever read/wrote the
  **first** BGDT block, which holds `blocksize/desc_size` group descriptors = **64 groups** for a
  default `mkfs.ext4` (4 KB block, 64-byte 64BIT descriptor) = the first **~8 GiB**. The real
  agnos-fs root is **25 GiB (~200 groups)**, so any inode in group ‚â• 64 was unreachable:
  `ext2_get_inode` printed `ext2: block_group beyond first BGDT chunk (Phase 1 limit)` and returned
  `-1`. On the 1.45.x close-out iron burn this surfaced as **`yo google.com` and `whirl https://google.com`
  both failing exec-from-disk** ‚Äî Linux's allocator had placed `/bin/yo` and `/bin/whirl` in high
  block groups during `cp -a` staging, so the kernel couldn't load their inodes (the binaries never
  ran). The whole single-chunk assumption is lifted:
  - The 4 KB `ext2_bgdt_buf` now holds **one BGDT chunk at a time**, tracked by an
    `ext2_bgdt_loaded_chunk` marker; `ext2_load_bgdt_chunk(chunk)` / `ext2_bgdt_load_for_group(group)`
    swap chunks in on demand (short-circuiting if already resident, invalidating the marker on a
    failed read so a partial buffer can't be reused). `ext2_grp_off` is now **chunk-relative**
    (`group ‚àí loaded_chunk¬∑groups_per_block`) ‚Äî byte-identical for chunk 0, so all single-group paths
    are unchanged.
  - `ext2_get_inode` / `ext2_put_inode` load the target group's chunk instead of refusing; the stale
    `(blocksize/32)¬∑inodes_per_group` cap is replaced with a true `s_inodes_count` bound (new
    `ext2_sb_inodes_count` accessor, s_inodes_count @ sb+0).
  - `ext2_write_bgdt` persists the **resident** chunk and `ext2_bgdt_recsum` recomputes bg_checksums
    for that chunk's **absolute** group range ‚Äî correct `crc32c` seed per group on any chunk. The four
    allocators (`ext2_alloc_block` / `ext2_free_block` / `ext2_alloc_inode` / `ext2_free_inode`) load
    each group's chunk per iteration, which also closes a **latent out-of-bounds read**: the alloc
    scan walked `g` to `ngroups` (~200) while `ext2_grp_off(g)` indexed past the 4 KB buffer into
    adjacent BSS for `g ‚â• 64` (masked only because low groups always had free space).
  - META_BG (non-contiguous BGDT, hundreds-of-MB descriptor tables) stays mount-refused by the
    existing incompat mask, so the contiguous `first_data_block+1+chunk` formula is exact for every
    FS that mounts.
- **Validation:** new **`scripts/ext2-multichunk-smoke.sh`** (+ `MULTICHUNK_SELFTEST` build flag)
  boots a genuine **4-chunk image** (197 groups via `-g 256`, default `mkfs.ext4` profile ‚Äî
  metadata_csum + 64bit + extent) and confirms a **high-group inode READ** (inode 1057 in group 66 /
  chunk 1 ‚Üí `mode=0x81a4` regular file ‚Äî the exact iron failure mode), a **negative bound** (inode
  past `s_inodes_count` rejected), a **high-group block alloc/free** (block 16896 in group 66 ‚Üí
  allocator + `write_bgdt` + recsum on chunk 1), and **`e2fsck -fn` clean** post-boot. The existing
  single-chunk smokes are byte-equivalent and stay green: `ext2-smoke` 5/5, `ext2-write-smoke`,
  `ext2-uninit-smoke`, `exec-smoke`, `check.sh` 11/11. Production `build/agnos` **1,207,096 B**.
  Pending the next archaemenid burn to confirm `yo`/`whirl` exec-from-disk on real iron.

## [1.45.14] ‚Äî 2026-06-22

### Changed
- **cyrius toolchain pin `6.2.30` ‚Üí `6.2.36`** (`cyrius.cyml`). Picks up the agnos
  correctness fixes in the 6.2.31‚Äì6.2.36 stdlib: **6.2.36** made `io.cyr`'s file-lock
  helpers (`file_lock` / `file_append_locked`) portable ‚Äî they were `CYRIUS_TARGET_LINUX`-only
  raw `syscall(73)` and thus **`ud2`/SIGILL trap stubs on agnos** (an agnos boot-trap class),
  now routed through agnos `flock`#59; **6.2.35** fixed the agnos mutex + `time`#201 ‚Üí
  `time_unix`#46 and removed the last raw `syscall(SYS_FUTEX)` that "made stale-pinned
  consumers fail `--agnos`". The **kernel binary is byte-identical** (1,204,416 B) ‚Äî cycc
  codegen is byte-identical across 6.2.30‚Üí6.2.36 and the freestanding kernel links no host
  stdlib (`stdlib = []`); the fixes land in the agnos **userland** programs (smokes, bundled
  tools) that link the cyrius stdlib. The pin is now honest (matches the installed/CI toolchain).
  `check.sh` 11/11, `agnsh-smoke` PASS.

## [1.45.13] ‚Äî 2026-06-22

### Added
- **`winsize`#60 ‚Äî ring-3 terminal/console window size** (`kernel/arch/x86_64/fb_console.cyr`
  `fb_winsize()` + `kernel/core/syscall.cyr` dispatch). agnos has no `ioctl(TIOCGWINSZ)`, so a
  ring-3 TUI had no way to learn the console grid and fell back to a hardcoded 80√ó24. `winsize()`
  returns the live framebuffer's character grid packed as `(cols<<16 | rows)` in `rax`
  (`cols = fb_width()/(8¬∑scale)`, `rows = (fb_height()‚àíFB_CONSOLE_Y0)/(16¬∑scale)`, 8√ó16 glyph ‚Äî
  mirroring `fb_putc`'s own cell math), or `-1` if the framebuffer isn't up. No args, no user
  buffer (a pure read of the boot-info FB geometry, like `uptime_ms`#40) ‚Üí no pointer-validation
  or SMAP surface, no preempt/sti window. Consumed by **`darshana`'s `tty_winsize` on agnos**
  (new `#ifdef CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` branch over the raw `syscall(60)`), which unblocks
  **kii / cyim / chakshu** sizing to the real console instead of 80√ó24. Verified by
  `WINSIZE_SELFTEST` (asserts the packed grid matches `fb_putc`'s cell math against the live FB).
- **`lseek`#58 + `flock`#59 ‚Äî ring-3 file seek + advisory locking** (`kernel/core/syscall.cyr`
  + `kernel/core/vfs.cyr`), the shared kernel infra the server-stage apps need (descent's
  libro/patra persistence + agora's `flock`'d shared-world door games ‚Äî see
  `agnosticos/.../planning/server-app-ports.md`).
  - **`lseek(fd, offset, whence)`** ‚Äî `SEEK_SET`/`CUR`/`END` reposition of a `VFS_EXT2_FILE`
    cursor (the fd already carries `pos` at `payload[0]`, `size` at `[1]`, so this just sets
    it); returns the new absolute position or ‚àí1 (bad fd / not seekable / bad whence / negative).
  - **`flock(fd, op)`** ‚Äî BSD advisory whole-file locking (`LOCK_SH`/`EX`/`UN` ¬± `LOCK_NB`),
    keyed by the file's inode (`payload[2]`) in a 16-slot table, holder = `proc_current`.
    EX is exclusive, SH coexist, EX-vs-SH conflicts. NON-BLOCKING at the kernel boundary
    (single-core run-to-completion can't sleep) ‚Üí a contended lock returns ‚àí1 (WOULD_BLOCK) and
    the ring-3 caller poll-spins. **Released in `vfs_close` (close#6) AND on proc exit**
    (`flock_release_pid`, so a fork-per-accept worker that dies mid-turn can't strand the
