- `scripts/agnsh-verb-test.py` **PASS** (`ls /` ‚Üí `lost+found bin vtest`; `cat /vtest` ‚Üí `VERBPROOF`; banner `agnoshi 1.4.2`). `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS** (the larger verbs-enabled binary boots to `[ASSIST] >`). `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (kernel regression ‚Äî code unchanged from 1.42.5). Kernel build **1,074,288 B** (banner-only delta vs 1.42.5), x86_64 multiboot2 OK.

### Notes

- **Deferred (Track-B follow-on, not this cut):** linking `commandress`/`bannermanor`/`mihi`/`iam` as agnsh builtins ‚Äî each needs its own `CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` build, and **mihi/iam sysinfo needs new kernel syscalls** (no `uname`/`sysinfo`/`meminfo` exists today). That's its own kernel+userland bite.


## [1.42.5] ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (**Track A perf: heap allocator zeroing ‚Äî `kmalloc` is now O(1)-zero instead of O(block_size).** First measurement-gated perf bite, on the bench harness 1.42.1 restored. The slab allocator double-zeroed: `kmalloc` re-zeroed the whole block on every alloc, but `kfree_sized` already scrubs the full block on free ‚Äî so the alloc-time re-zero was redundant.)

### Changed

- **`kmalloc` clears only the 8-byte free-list next-pointer instead of re-zeroing the whole block.** `slab_grow` now zeros each fresh 4 KB page once (before chaining), establishing the invariant *every block on a free list is fully zero except its first 8 bytes (the next-pointer)*; `kfree_sized` (unchanged) maintains it by scrubbing the full block on free. `kmalloc` therefore only clears the popped block's next-pointer ‚Äî **O(1) vs the old O(block_size)** (a 4096 B alloc dropped from 512 store64s to 1). It's also a *stronger* guarantee than the old `zero only [0,size)` loop: the **full** block is now zero, so a caller reading `[size, block_size)` can't see stale data. (`kernel/core/heap.cyr`.)
- **`kfree_sized` documents the size-class contract** (now load-bearing): `size` must map to the same class as the originating `kmalloc`, else the scrub under-covers (kmalloc no longer re-zeros to compensate). All current callers pass a matching size ‚Äî **adversarially audited** (two independent auditors + adjudicator: every `&slab_free` writer enumerated, no `kcalloc`/`krealloc`/`kfree`-without-scrub path, invariant holds by induction; verdict **safe**, zero required fixes).

### Performance

- **Heap alloc/free zeroing (KVM `-cpu host`, min-of-N, archaemenid Zen):**

  | class | before | after | Œî |
  |---|---|---|---|
  | `kmalloc/kfree(4096)` | 5719 c/op | **2855** | **‚àí50%** (2.0√ó) |
  | `kmalloc/kfree(256)` | 489 c/op | **313** | ‚àí36% |
  | `kmalloc/kfree(32)` | 132 c/op | **112** | ‚àí15% |

  The win scales with block size (zeroing-dominated); 32 B is fixed-overhead-bound. Measured via the min-of-N estimator because even KVM rdtsc is preemption-sensitive on longer loops.

### Added

- **`bench.sh` gains a KVM accel path** (`-enable-kvm -cpu host` when `/dev/kvm` is present, else `-cpu max` TCG; `BENCH_KVM=0` forces TCG). TCG's host-TSC-based rdtsc swings ~5√ó run-to-run (`pmm_alloc_free` 7782‚Äì40742), which made fine perf deltas unmeasurable; KVM gives real, stable cycles (`pmm_alloc_free` ~800‚Äì1100). `kvm_enabled` is recorded in `bench-history.csv` so KVM and TCG rows stay non-comparable on purpose.

### Notes

- **Deferred Track-A targets (surveyed, with reasons):** `fb_console` scroll is already u64-block-optimized ‚Äî its real win is a RAM-side shadow buffer, an architectural change tied to the parked AMD-Zen-scanout eval, not a 1.42.5 bite. `pmm_spin_lock`'s atomic `xchg` is dead weight on a single core but is deliberate SMP-future-proofing (the coming multithreading arc) ‚Äî not stripped unilaterally. The PMM top-down scan is correctness-load-bearing (the 1.41.12 fragmentation fix); a free-page hint risks reintroducing that bug for a win the bench barely exercises ‚Äî deferred.
- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7**, `agnsh-smoke` PASS, build **1,074,288 B** (‚àí16 B). No iron burn needed (heap-internal); rides the next burn for free.

## [1.42.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (**Hardening (c): reap leftovers ‚Äî the in-memory spawn/elf_load procs are now reaped, and the mmap-arena teardown covers the full arena.** Closes the two process-teardown leaks carried from 1.40.14. Pre-1.42 the `run`/`elf_load_from_file` (from-disk) path reaped on exit, but the `spawn`/`elf_load` (in-memory) path's children leaked their whole address space + proc-table slot for the boot; and the AS-teardown free-loop stopped at PD index 510, one short of the arena's top page at PD 511.)

### Fixed

- **`proc_reap_child` reaps the in-memory-spawned child on `waitpid`.** The in-memory parent/child model (`spawn` ‚Üí scheduler runs the child ‚Üí parent blocks in `waitpid`) never reaped anything ‚Äî every `spawn()`ed child's ELF + stack + any mmap huge pages + proc-table slot leaked for the whole boot. `waitpid` (syscall 4) now reads the exit code first, then calls `proc_reap_child(pid)` to reclaim the child's address space + slot ‚Äî making the in-memory path leak-symmetric with the from-disk path's `sh_cmd_run` ‚Üí `proc_reap`. A **separate** helper (not `proc_reap`) deliberately, because `proc_reap`'s fd-reclaim sweep (close fds 3..31) is correct only under the single-foreground run-to-completion model; in the parent/child model the *parent* is alive and may hold its own fds, so this reap does address-space + slot reclaim only. `waitpid` also gained a self-wait guard (`arg1 == proc_current` ‚Üí `-1`) so a process can't block forever on itself and then free its own running address space. (`core/syscall.cyr`, `core/proc.cyr`; aarch64 stub in `arch/aarch64/stubs.cyr`.)
- **`proc_free_address_space` munmap-on-exit sweep covers the full mmap arena.** The anonymous mmap arena `[0x10000000, 0x40000000)` spans PD indices 128..511; the free-loop ran `1..510`, so a process that exited *without* `munmap` leaked every still-mapped arena page (the heapstress symptom: ~3 interactive `run`s, then `pmm_alloc_2mb` exhaustion). The loop now scans the full user range `1..511`; the `present AND user` predicate still discriminates correctly at every index ‚Äî not-present / never-mapped arena entries are skipped (this is exactly the [1.42.2] "arena not-present until mapped" layout), supervisor kernel-mirror entries are skipped, and the index-511 user-CR3 stash slot (a flagless page-aligned phys) is skipped. Correct under both the pre-[1.42.2] (arena pre-seeded supervisor) and [1.42.2] (arena not-present) PD layouts. (`core/proc.cyr`.)

### Validated

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7**. EXEC_SELFTEST exec-smoke **PASS** with the new reap-validation cycle reporting `reap: 6x mmap-arena page reclaimed, free stable OK` (free-page + 2 MB-region counts stable across 6 map/teardown cycles ‚Äî no leak). Build **1,074,304 B**, x86_64 multiboot2 OK.

## [1.42.3] ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (**Hardening (b): the rare SYSCALL-path RBP smash (`CR2=0x37fed8`) ‚Äî user RBP is now preserved across the kstack switch.** Residual bug #2 from the 1.41.12 `#PF` investigation: ~1/52 boots faulted in agnsh's mmap-wrapper epilogue with the user RBP smashed to ~`0x37ff00` (just under the SYSCALL kstack top `0x3F0000`) while RSP was healthy.)

### Fixed

- **`syscall_init` preserves the user's RBP across the kstack switch.** SYSCALL doesn't save RBP, and the C-ABI `syscall_handler` (+ `ksyscall` callees) frame on RBP (`push rbp; mov rbp,rsp; ‚Ä¶; leave`) ‚Äî so after `call syscall_handler` the RBP register holds a *kernel-stack* frame value (~`0x37ff00`). In ~1/52 boots that leaked-kernel RBP survived back to ring 3 and agnsh's epilogue (`mov -0x28(%rbp),%r15`) `#PF`'d on it. The hand-assembled entry now `push rbp` (`0x55`) immediately after switching RSP to `syscall_kstack_top`, and `pop rbp` (`0x5D`) immediately before the user-RSP restore ‚Äî a balanced pair (the intervening `push rcx`/`push r11` + `pop r11`/`pop rcx` save-for-SYSRET block nets to zero), so the SYSRET stack is intact and the user's RBP is recovered. (`arch/x86_64/syscall_hw.cyr`.)

### Validated

- `scripts/rbp-repro.sh` (boots the agnsh image N=40 under `qemu -d int`, scans for a ring-3 `#PF` with CR2 in the kstack window `0x37fxxx`): **40/40 reached the `[ASSIST] >` prompt, 0 RBP-smash faults, 0 total `v=0e`.** (The bug is ~1/52, so a clean N=40 is strong corroboration of the structural fix, not a proof of absence.) `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (a regression here breaks *every* syscall ‚Äî sweep + agnsh-smoke green). `agnsh-smoke` PASS. Build **1,074,304 B**.

## [1.42.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (**Hardening (a), direction B: the mmap arena is no longer seeded present-supervisor in per-process page tables.** Removes the latent present-supervisor trap behind the 1.41.12 top-down `pmm_alloc` fix (issue `2026-06-04-agnsh-ring3-pf-pmm-fragmentation.md`). Direction A ‚Äî editing page tables under the boot CR3 ‚Äî was tested and rejected in the issue (it replaced the fault with a deterministic `CR2=0x8`); this is direction B, a correctness improvement that pairs with it.)

### Fixed

- **`proc_create_address_space` leaves the mmap-arena PD slots not-present instead of inheriting `pt_init`'s present-supervisor identity entries.** Every per-process PD previously copied kernel PD `[0..510]`, inheriting `pt_init`'s present-SUPERVISOR (`0x83`) 2 MB identity mapping for the *whole* 0‚Äì1 GB range ‚Äî including the mmap arena `[0x10000000, 0x40000000)` (PD indices 128..511). So when `proc_map_page`'s user-PDE write went astray (the wrong-CR3 walk), the stale supervisor PDE "won" and a ring-3 heap store faulted `e=0007` (US-protection on a *present* page) instead of a clean `e=0006` (not-present). The create path now copies only the kernel/identity slots `[0..127]` (`[0, 0x10000000)` ‚Äî kernel text/data/heap + the SYSCALL kstack at `0x3F0000`, which must stay identity-mapped under the per-process CR3) and **blanks the arena slots `[128..510]` to not-present (0)**; `sys_mmap`/`proc_map_page` populate them as user pages (`0x87`) on demand, and an un-mapped arena access now faults cleanly `e=0006` with no stale supervisor PDE to win. Slot 511 remains the user-CR3 stash. (`core/proc.cyr`.)

### Validated

- `scripts/repro-ring3-pf.sh` (boots the real agnsh kernel N=20 under `qemu -d int`, tallies ring-3 `#PF`): **0 ring-3 `#PF` / 20 boots, reached-exec 20/20** (signature `CR2=0x10000000`:0, `CR2=0x8`:0) ‚Äî holds the 0/52 baseline the top-down `pmm_alloc` fix established, now with the supervisor trap removed. `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7**, `agnsh-smoke` PASS. Build **1,074,304 B**.

## [1.42.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (**Track A step 0: `scripts/bench.sh` reconstructed ‚Äî the kernel benchmark harness builds + emits numbers again.** It was double-broken: the 1.37.5 kashi fold-in left its kernel-build invocation kashi-unaware, and the 1.41.9 shell-shrink deleted the `bench`/`bench_report` verbs it drove. `BENCHMARKS.md` had been frozen since 2026-05-11.)

### Added

- **`kernel/core/bench.cyr` ‚Äî a compile-gated 3-tier benchmark entry (`bench_run_all`).** The tier code (core / subsystems / integration; rdtsc cycles/op; the `[tier1]`/`[tier2]`/`[tier3]` markers `bench.sh` parses) was recovered byte-for-byte from the pre-1.41.9 in-kernel `bench`/`bench_report` verbs (git `2a71754^:kernel/user/shell.cyr`) and re-homed as a build-time entry ‚Äî **not** a recovery-shell verb (the shell's shrink to recovery-only is permanent). Included from `kernel/agnos.cyr`; behaviorally inert in the production build (no reachable caller ‚Äî `boot_finish.cyr` still launches `kybernet()`), at the cost of +2,656 B / +2 dead fns, exactly how every other unreachable kernel fn lives in the binary.

### Fixed

- **`scripts/bench.sh` rewired for the post-1.41.9 / post-1.37.5 kernel.** The call-site rewrite is now `kybernet(); arch_halt();` ‚Üí `bench_run_all(); arch_halt();` (the launch site moved to `boot_finish.cyr` at the 1.36.2 split), and the build uses the kashi-aware path (a bare `cyrius build agnos.cyr` fails on `undefined KASHI_FONT_VGA_8X16` since the 1.37.5 fold-in). Added a fail-loud match-guard on the call-site rewrite (so a future launch-site rename can't silently no-op and emit an empty `BENCHMARKS.md`) and removed a dead variable; the existing `test_procs` no-op guard + EXIT/INT/TERM source-restore trap are preserved (sources left byte-identical to HEAD).

### Validated

- `scripts/bench.sh` runs end-to-end (exit 0): builds the production ELF64 kernel with the bench entry wired in, boots gnoboot+OVMF on an NVMe/GPT/ext2 image, parses the full 3-tier table (pmm/heap/memwrite/syscall/vfs/serial), writes a populated 3-section `BENCHMARKS.md`, and appends the 12-column-provenance `bench-history.csv`. Production kernel + `sweep.sh` **7/7** unaffected (the include is inert).

## [1.42.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-06 (**Cycle-open: kernel perf + hardening (Track A) ‚à• userland environment (Track B).** The 1.41.x shell-separation arc is **iron-complete** ‚Äî burn `14115` (2026-06-06) on archaemenid typed `help`/`mode`/`version` at `[ASSIST] >` with correct echo + dispatch, past a real DHCP lease; the userland AI shell is now typeable on hardware. Two non-blocking residuals open the new cycle's backlog. This is the cycle-open marker; the engineering bites ‚Äî `bench.sh` reconstruction + measurement-gated perf tuning, the three carry-forward hardening items, and the Track-B agnsh verb buildout ‚Äî land as 1.42.1+.)

### Notes

- **1.41.x arc iron-close.** Burn `14115` validated the whole typing ladder on real Zen (shell-separation ‚Üí 1.41.12 stdout FB-mirror ‚Üí 1.41.12 PMM top-down ‚Üí 1.41.14 IRQ1/`sti` ‚Üí 1.41.15 line discipline). Tracker: agnosticos `iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-141x-cycle`.
- **Residual 1 ‚Äî stale agnsh banner (`agnoshi 1.4.0`).** NOT a code bug: `agnoshi` source (`src/agnsh.cyr:40`) already reads `1.4.1`. The flashed disk carried a **stale staged binary** ‚Äî `build/rootfs/bin/agnsh` was an `agnoshi 1.4.0` build assembled before the 1.41.15 cut bumped agnoshi to 1.4.1; the agnos-target build + smoke seed both already had 1.4.1. Staged binary refreshed to 1.4.1 (2026-06-06) so the next flash/seed is correct. Design follow-up (Track B): derive `agnoshi`'s `VERSION_STR` from its `VERSION` file so a bump can't desync the banner again.
- **Residual 2 ‚Äî backspace dead on iron.** The kernel `read(fd 0)` line discipline handles erase (`syscall.cyr` `ch==8` ‚Üí `kputc(8);kputc(32);kputc(8)`), the scancode table maps Backspace `0x0E`‚Üí8 (`keyboard.cyr`), and `fb_putc` renders the cursor-back+blank (`fb_console.cyr`) ‚Äî so it works in QEMU (validated over `-serial stdio`). The path is **byte-identical** to the iron-proven in-kernel recovery shell (`shell.cyr:794-797`), so it's a shared keyboard-delivery-layer issue (the Backspace key not arriving as `0x0E` on archaemenid's UEFI USB-legacy emulation), not an agnsh-specific bug. It needs one iron observation to pin down, captured at **no extra burn cost** via the recovery-shell probe (press Backspace at the `agnos>` prompt on the next cycle burn). Tracked ‚Üí 1.42.x hardening backlog.

## [1.41.15] ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (**agnsh typing on iron ‚Äî the `14114` burn read the keyboard but every line collapsed to `Command: D` (a stuck shift + the `d` key) with no echo. Root cause: 1.41.14 routed `read(fd 0)` through IRQ1 correctly, but the read returned after the FIRST char and ring 3 runs with IF=0 between syscalls (`ring3.cyr:108` sets RFLAGS=0x002; SYSRET restores it). In that IF=0 inter-byte gap IRQ1 is masked, so the SHIFT-RELEASE break (`0xAA`) landing there was dropped ‚Üí `kb_shift` latched at 1 ‚Üí scancode `0x20` ('d') resolved through `sc_shifted` to `'D'`=68, and most makes were swallowed too. The proven-on-iron in-kernel recovery shell never hits this: it holds IF=1 across the WHOLE line in one loop. Fix stops diverging from that reference ‚Äî `read(0)` now drains a whole line under one continuous `sti` window, echoes as it goes, and terminates on newline. The matching no-echo half is fixed in the same motion: echo moves kernel-side (the only place it can live once the read is line-buffered). QEMU-green ‚Äî smoke + type-test + sweep 7/7; iron burn pending.)

### Fixed

- **`kbd_read_blocking` is now a continuous-IF, line-disciplined reader ‚Äî mirrors the in-kernel shell that types correctly on archaemenid.** The 1.41.14 per-byte read returned after the first character; combined with `agnsh`'s one-byte-per-`read(0)` loop, every keystroke's scancode stream was split across syscalls, and the ring-3 IF=0 gap between them dropped the shift-release break ‚Äî `kb_shift` stuck at 1, so `d`‚Üí`D` and the line collapsed to that single stuck char (the `14114` "Command: D" symptom). The read now blocks under one `sti` window for the whole line (preemption suspended via `sched_active=0`, exactly as before), processing every make/break in-window so `kb_shift` is always cleared on release, and returns when Enter (`0x1C`‚Üí`0x0A`) is seen or the caller's buffer fills. `kb_shift`/`kb_ctrl` are also reset on read entry as defense-in-depth. (`core/syscall.cyr`.)
- **Kernel-side echo + backspace on `read(fd 0)`.** `agnsh` had no echo at all (the other half of the `14114` symptom: input invisible). Once the read is line-buffered the shell can't echo per-char-as-typed, so echo lives in the kernel ‚Äî printable bytes via `kputc` (serial + GOP framebuffer mirror, the same fb path `write(1)` has used from a ring-3 syscall since 1.41.12), backspace as `BS SP BS`, newline on Enter. This is exactly the in-kernel recovery shell's echo loop (`user/shell.cyr:790-805`). It also fixes the prompt-accumulation cosmetic (a bare Enter now echoes its newline before returning, so empty lines advance cleanly instead of stacking `[ASSIST] >` on one row).

### Changed

- **ABI O1 revised: `read(fd=0)` is canonical-lite (line-buffered + kernel echo), not RAW.** (`docs/development/agnos-userland-abi.md` ¬ß3.1 / ¬ß0-O1.) The original RAW choice assumed the QEMU `hid_poll` model, where polled byte-by-byte reads are IF-independent. On iron, keystrokes arrive only via IRQ1 and ring 3 runs IF=0 between syscalls, so RAW byte-by-byte is *structurally impossible* ‚Äî any scancode arriving while `agnsh` is in userland is lost. Continuous-IF line discipline is the only shape that works under the current single-foreground/IF=0-ring-3 invariant. When the future multithreading arc lets ring 3 run with IF=1 + safe preemption, O1 can return to RAW so a richer `agnsh` line editor (`completion.cyr`) can own editing. Observable syscall numbers/ABI surface unchanged ‚Üí **no cyrius peer change**. `agnsh`'s `read_line` (`agnoshi`) is now a single line read that strips the terminator (agnoshi 1.4.1).

### Validated

- `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS** (boots to `[ASSIST] >`, `agnoshi 1.4.1`). `scripts/agnsh-type-test.py` **PASS** ‚Äî `help`/`version`/`mode` now **echo as typed AND dispatch correctly** (no `Command: D` collapse); the typed line reaches the builtin intact. `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (exec-from-disk + FAT/exFAT/ext2 read+write+subdir, `e2fsck -fn` clean). Build **1,070,960 B**, x86_64 multiboot2 OK.

### Notes

- **Iron burn pending ‚Äî QEMU cannot prove the iron path.** QEMU delivers via `hid_poll` (polled, IF-independent); the IRQ1 + continuous-IF interaction is what `14114` exercised and only a burn confirms. **Confidence is high**: the fix makes `read(0)` byte-for-byte the shape of the in-kernel shell that already types correctly on this exact box. The kernel echo doubles as the on-FB discriminator ‚Äî if typing still shows a fixed char, a deeper producer issue is implicated; expectation is distinct, correctly-cased input. Tracker + falsification in agnosticos `iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-141x-cycle`.

## [1.41.14] ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (**agnsh typing on iron ‚Äî the corrected fix: `read(fd 0)` re-enables interrupts so IRQ1 delivers, instead of polling a source that's dead on real hardware. Supersedes 1.41.13's i8042-poll, which the 2nd identical no-typing iron burn falsified.** The keyboard was never broken, and it was never the keyboard *driver*: the in-kernel recovery shell types fine on archaemenid via IRQ1 ‚Üí `kb_isr`. agnsh's `read(0)` is the only path that fails, because the *frozen ABI itself* specified the read(0) mechanism as "busy-poll `hid_poll()` with interrupts MASKED" ‚Äî correct in QEMU, structurally impossible on iron, where keystrokes arrive only via IRQ1 (which IF-masking blocks). 1.41.13 bet on `kb_poll_i8042`, but AGNOS's own xHCI ownership handoff (`xhci_usblegctlsts_disable_smi`, mirroring Linux `quirk_usb_handoff_xhci`) tears down the firmware's USB-legacy i8042 emulation, so polled 0x60/0x64 reads a dead bus. The fix makes `read(0)` get keystrokes the way the *working* in-kernel shell does. QEMU-green (smoke + type-test + sweep 7/7); iron burn pending.)

### Fixed

- **`kbd_read_blocking` now re-enables interrupts so IRQ1 ‚Üí `kb_isr` fills `kb_buf` ‚Äî the keystroke producer proven on archaemenid by the in-kernel shell.** `read(fd 0)` enters with IF masked (SYSCALL clears IF via SFMASK), which locks out the one keystroke source real hardware has. The fix issues `sti` for the duration of the blocking read; to keep that safe in a non-reentrant syscall it suspends preemption by clearing `sched_active` (the timer ISR still EOIs + advances `timer_ticks`, but `do_context_switch` early-returns, so no context switch fires mid-syscall). `kb_has_key()`'s `hid_poll()` still drains the xHCI HID ring for QEMU. **Why this is the right layer:** the in-kernel recovery shell *already* runs with IF=1 and the same xHCI MSI-X enabled and types fine on iron, so xHCI interrupts were never the hazard the old comment claimed ‚Äî the timer was, and `sched_active=0` neutralizes exactly that. (`core/syscall.cyr`; arch-factored `kbd_irq_enable`/`kbd_irq_disable` in `arch/x86_64/keyboard.cyr` + aarch64 stub.)
- **Removed the dead `kb_poll_i8042` path (1.41.13).** Polling the i8042 data port cannot work on archaemenid: AGNOS claims the xHCI controller from BIOS and disables the legacy SMI (`usb/xhci_port.cyr` `xhci_usblegctlsts_disable_smi`), which is precisely what stops the firmware's SMM-based USB-legacy i8042 emulation ‚Äî so ports 0x60/0x64 read open-bus `0xFF`. It passed in QEMU only because QEMU has a real PS/2 i8042 *and* the poll was a no-op there. Deleted, not kept as a "fallback."

### Changed

- **The garbage "iron-state-as-fact" comments that drove five sessions back into the kernel keyboard driver are gone.** `boot_finish.cyr`, `syscall.cyr` (`kbd_read_blocking`), and `keyboard.cyr` (`kb_has_key`) no longer assert the unverified "firmware emulates an i8042 / the xHCI ring stays empty because firmware owns the device" theory as fact. They now describe the actual, verified mechanism (IRQ1 ‚Üí `kb_isr` ‚Üí `kb_buf`, IF=1 required; `hid_poll` = the QEMU path).

### Validated

- `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS** (boots to `[ASSIST] >`). `scripts/agnsh-type-test.py` **PASS** (exit 0) ‚Äî `help`/`version`/`mode` register cleanly through the xHCI usb-kbd. **This is the exact baseline that FAILED for 1.41.13's discarded `sti` variant**; it passes here because preemption is suspended (`sched_active=0`) rather than left live. `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (exec-from-disk + FAT/exFAT/ext2 read+write+subdir, `e2fsck -fn` clean). Build **1,070,736 B**, x86_64 multiboot2 OK.

### Notes

- **Iron burn pending ‚Äî QEMU cannot prove the iron path.** QEMU delivers keystrokes via its usb-kbd ‚Üí `hid_poll`; the IRQ1 ‚Üí `kb_isr` path is what archaemenid uses and is confirmed only by a burn. **Confidence is high**: the in-kernel shell already proves IRQ1 ‚Üí `kb_isr` works on this exact box, and the fix routes `read(0)` through that same proven mechanism. Falsification + fallback recorded in agnosticos `iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-141x-cycle`.
- **The frozen ABI's read(0) *mechanism* clause was corrected** (`docs/development/agnos-userland-abi.md` ¬ß3.1) ‚Äî observable ABI (RAW, blocks until ‚â•1, returns count) unchanged, so **no cyrius peer change**; the syscall numbers were always a faithful mirror.

## [1.41.13] ‚Äî 2026-06-05 (**agnsh typing on iron ‚Äî `read(fd 0)` ran interrupts-masked; + a codebase-wide comment-accuracy sweep.** The `14112` re-burn (post-PMM-fix) reached the `[ASSIST] >` prompt on the FB ‚Äî A1 now iron-confirmed ‚Äî but typing produced nothing. Root cause was NOT the long-mis-attributed "USB-HID keyboard never worked on iron": the keyboard works (the in-kernel shell types fine on archaemenid), it just arrives via the firmware's USB-legacy i8042 emulation on **IRQ1**, and the ring-3 `read(fd 0)` path runs with interrupts masked so the ISR could never fire. Fixed by polling the i8042 directly, interrupts untouched. Bundled: 21 verified false/stale "iron-state-as-fact" comments corrected across the kernel ‚Äî the same class of comment that misdirected this very diagnosis. **Iron burn pending** (the i8042-poll path can't be exercised under QEMU's real usb-kbd).)

### Fixed

- **Userland agnsh hung at a visible prompt with no keystrokes on iron ‚Üí `kbd_read_blocking` polled only the (empty-on-iron) xHCI ring with interrupts masked.** The keyboard is NOT broken on archaemenid ‚Äî the in-kernel recovery shell types fine there. There is no PS/2 hardware; keystrokes arrive as i8042 scancodes from the firmware's **UEFI USB-legacy emulation**, delivered via **IRQ1** to `kb_isr`. The xHCI USB-HID event ring that `hid_poll()` drains stays empty on this box (firmware owns the device), but that is a red herring, not "keyboard broken." The agnsh path failed because `read(fd 0)` ‚Üí `kbd_read_blocking` runs with **interrupts masked** ‚Äî SYSCALL clears IF via SFMASK (=0x200, `syscall_hw.cyr:98`) ‚Äî so the IRQ1 ISR could never fire and the busy-spin only polled the empty xHCI ring ‚Üí hang. The in-kernel shell typed fine because it runs with IF=1. **Fix:** `kbd_read_blocking` now **polls the i8042 data port directly** via a new `kb_poll_i8042()` (ports 0x60/0x64, bounded ‚â§16, AUXB-skip), interrupt-free, alongside the existing `hid_poll()`. On a given box exactly one source has data, so draining both is race-free and never double-counts. Interrupts stay masked: a discarded first attempt re-enabled them with `sti`, which lets the xHCI raise an interrupt the kernel only polls ‚Äî **a baseline QEMU `agnsh-type-test` proved that regressed QEMU typing**, so the polled approach was taken instead. (`core/syscall.cyr`, `arch/x86_64/keyboard.cyr`, aarch64 stub.)

### Changed

- **Comment-accuracy sweep ‚Äî 21 verified false/stale "iron-state-as-fact" comments corrected across 13 kernel files.** An adversarially-verified pass over all 80 `.cyr` files found comments that bake transient test/iron/runtime state into source as permanent fact, misleading later agents (the exact failure that sent two agents ‚Äî and this one ‚Äî chasing a phantom "USB-HID-on-iron" arc off a stale `boot_finish.cyr` note). Corrected, comment-only (binary unchanged): the xHCI "Attempt 56 proved IE=0 blocks event posting" claim (falsified at Attempt 57; real cause was ERST segment-size=0 ‚Äî `usb/xhci.cyr`); the r8169 "physical-match never delivered a frame on iron" claim (unicast RX solved 1.32.7, RX ring 16‚Üí64 ‚Äî `core/r8169.cyr`); `nvme.cyr` "Iron-test gate (still QEMU-only)" √ó2 (NVMe iron-debuted Attempt 80); `net.cyr` / `selftests.cyr` stale r8169/SLIRP gate notes; `smp.cyr` "GATED FOR ATTEMPT 10 DIAGNOSTIC" + Attempt-9 death narrative ‚Üí parked-by-design; `fb.cyr` boot_shim-canary refs (canary removed v1.30.1); `io.cyr` deleted-consumer reference; `msc.cyr`, `xhci_cmd.cyr`, `xhci_port.cyr`, `ext2.cyr`, `virtio_net.cyr`, `user/init.cyr` stale phase/attempt/version notes. The directly-implicated keyboard comments (`boot_finish.cyr`, `syscall.cyr`, `keyboard.cyr`) were corrected as part of the fix above. (One finding, `test_procs.cyr`, was a code-state mismatch rather than a misleading comment and was left for separate review.)

### Validated

- `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS** (boots to the `[ASSIST] >` prompt). `scripts/agnsh-type-test.py` **PASS** ‚Äî `help`/`version`/`mode` register cleanly through the xHCI usb-kbd, no regression and no doubling (the new i8042 poll is a no-op under QEMU, where `sendkey` targets the usb-kbd). The discarded `sti` variant FAILED this same test (baseline confirmed it was a regression, not a pre-existing failure). Comment-sweep edits are binary-neutral. Build **1,070,976 B**, x86_64 multiboot2 OK.

### Notes

- **Iron burn pending ‚Äî QEMU cannot exercise the fix.** QEMU uses a real `usb-kbd` (‚Üí `hid_poll`), so the QEMU pass only proves no regression; the i8042-poll path is the iron producer and is confirmed only by a burn on archaemenid. Falsification + fallback (selectively unmask IRQ1 at the PIC) are recorded in agnosticos `iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-141x-cycle`.
- **Supersedes the 1.41.12 "USB-HID input status unverified / not this arc's blocker" note.** With the PMM `#PF` fixed, agnsh reached the prompt, the keyboard became the live item, and it is now diagnosed (above) ‚Äî not a separate USB-HID arc.

## [1.41.12] ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (**Iron burn #1 of the 1.41.x arc ‚Üí ring-3 stdout FB-mirror fix.** The `14111_stallout` burn on archaemenid rode clean through storage + a real DHCP lease + kybernet init to `kybernet: exec /bin/agnsh`, then the FB froze ‚Äî no `#UD`, no reset, no emergency-shell fallback. **Not a hang:** agnsh loads (69-block ext2 single-indirect read), enters ring 3, and prints its banner ‚Äî but ring-3 stdout was serial-only, invisible on the serial-less box. Behavioral one-liner; agnsh is now visible on the FB. **Burn #2 (`14112`) then root-caused + fixed a kernel PMM allocation bug that `#PF`'d agnsh before its prompt on ~half of boots ‚Äî `pmm_alloc` now scans top-down (QEMU 0 `#PF`/52 boots, iron-pending).** Tag held until iron confirms both fixes.)

### Fixed

- **Ring-3 stdout/stderr was serial-only ‚Üí the agnsh banner was invisible on iron (the `14111` "stall").** A userland program's `write(1,‚Ä¶)`/`write(2,‚Ä¶)` routes `vfs_write` ‚Üí `dev_write(0)` ‚Üí `serial_dev_write`, which called `serial_print` **only** ‚Äî never `fb_print`. On archaemenid (no serial cable, per [[feedback_no_serial_on_iron]]) the agnsh banner went nowhere, so the boot *looked* hung at the last kernel `kprint` line (`kybernet: exec /bin/agnsh`) even though agnsh was alive in ring 3 (no `#UD`, no reset, no emergency fallback ‚Äî all consistent). A **regression from the 1.41.x write-surface refactor**: at 1.40.9 ring-3 `write(1)` went straight to `kputc`/`fb_putc` and was iron-visible (`EXEC-DISK-OK`, `run: exit 42/90` on the FB); the real-FS-syscall + FAT/exFAT-write bites rerouted stdout through the serial-only console device. `serial_dev_write` now calls `kprint` (serial **+** framebuffer, the same path as every kernel `kprintln`; `fb_print` no-ops until `fb_console_ready`). The QEMU `agnsh-smoke` **serial** log was the dispositive evidence ‚Äî it prints the full `agnoshi 1.3.5` banner after exec, exonerating the ext2 single-indirect read, KPTI page-map, and `mmap` paths. (`core/devs.cyr`.)

- **Userland agnsh `#PF`'d before its prompt on ~50% of boots ‚Üí kernel PMM cross-class fragmentation (the `14112` burn).** `pmm_alloc` (4 KB: page-tables / DMA rings / slab) and `pmm_alloc_2mb` (2 MB: ELF code / user stack / mmap heap) share one 6-region bitmap. `pmm_alloc`'s bottom-up first-fit, seeded from the KASLR-randomized `pmm_next_free`, scattered a single 4 KB page into a random 2 MB region each boot; one set page makes `pmm_alloc_2mb` skip that whole region, so on ~half of boots agnsh couldn't assemble its contiguous code/stack/heap regions and its mmap heap page resolved **supervisor-not-user** (`PD[128]=0x83`) ‚Äî a ring-3 write to it `#PF`'d (`e=0007`) on the first heap store, right after the banner (or `alloc` returned 0 and agnsh chained a NULL ‚Üí `CR2=0x8`). **Proven kernel ‚Äî not cyrius, not agnsh:** a heap-stress program with *zero agnsh code* reproduces it, the same agnsh source passes host smoke 59/59, and a page-table walk shows the supervisor PDE. Fix: `pmm_alloc` now scans **top-down** (page 4095 ‚Üí 1024) so 4 KB allocations cluster in the high 2 MB regions and leave the low regions for `pmm_alloc_2mb`. (`core/pmm.cyr`.)

### Validated

- `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS** ‚Äî banner still reaches serial (`kprint` retains `serial_print`); no emergency-shell fallback.
- **PMM fix: 0 ring-3 `#PF` across 52 fresh QEMU boots (`qemu -d int`, KASLR per boot) vs ~16/20 on the unfixed baseline.** agnsh now boots past its first heap write to its prompt in QEMU. **IRON BURN STILL PENDING** ‚Äî the QEMU memory map differs from Zen, so this is layout-validated only under QEMU. Build **1,070,480 B**. x86_64 multiboot2 OK.

### Notes

- **Correction to a now-deleted false note.** Earlier `[1.41.12]` text claimed the next iron blocker was "the empty USB-HID keyboard ring" and that A1 would show "banner + prompt visible, then no keystrokes." That was **wrong**: agnsh `#PF`'d on its own first heap write (above) **before ever reaching `read(fd 0)`**, so the keyboard never entered the picture and no prompt rendered. USB-HID input status on archaemenid is unverified and is **not** this arc's blocker.
- **Open follow-ons (NOT in this fix).** (1) The top-down PMM change likely *avoids* a deeper page-mapping fragility rather than curing it ‚Äî the direct `proc_map_page`/`vmm_map` page-table fixes were tried and **failed validation**, so a proper page-table fix is still owed. (2) A separate, rare (~1/52) syscall-path RBP frame-smash remains (`arch/x86_64/syscall_hw.cyr`, user RBP not preserved across the kstack switch). (3) The userland `/bin/agnsh` shell has **never reached a prompt on iron**; the native in-kernel recovery shell remains the proven-working typing shell. Full evidence chain ‚Üí agnosticos [`docs/development/issue/2026-06-04-agnsh-ring3-pf-pmm-fragmentation.md`].

## [1.41.11] ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (**Clean + iron-burn prep ‚Äî the 1.41.x shell-separation arc is software-complete.** The residual low finding from the 1.41.10 audit, plus staging the arc's first full hardware validation. agnsh is the interactive shell exec'd from disk in ring 3, the in-kernel shell is a recovery REPL, FAT/exFAT content-write reaches the syscall ABI, and the whole ring-3‚Üíring-0 surface is hardened ‚Äî all QEMU-green. Only the user-driven iron burn on archaemenid remains.)

### Fixed

- **Audit #4 ‚Äî `vfs_close` swallowed the write-fd flush result (low, robustness).** Closing a `VFS_SEC_WFILE` fd always returned 0, so a failed backend write (ENOSPC / write-protected volume) reported **success** to `close()` while the data was silently dropped. `vfs_close` now returns the flush rc. The success path is unchanged (`vfs_write_on` returns 0 on success) and the pool slot is still reclaimed even on a flush failure. (The audit's *primary* proposed fix ‚Äî reject the write-open when `vfs_create_on` fails ‚Äî was **unsound**: `fatfs_create` returns -1 on a name collision, i.e. the normal overwrite case, so that fix would have broken overwriting any existing file. Only the sound secondary fix was taken.) (`core/vfs.cyr`.)

### Added

- **1.41.x arc-burn tracker** (agnosticos [`iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-141x-cycle`](https://github.com/MacCracken/agnosticos/blob/main/docs/development/iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-141x-cycle)). Stages the combined hardware validation with the A1-A4 dispositive rubric: **A1** boot-to-agnsh in ring 3 (no `#UD`, no fallback), **A2** recovery `agnos>` shell reachable when `/bin/agnsh` is absent, **A3** FAT/exFAT write from agnsh survives a power-cycle + host-`fsck` clean, **A4** no exec/storage/net regression vs the iron-validated 1.40.x baseline. Flash the production `build/agnos` via `install-usb.sh --update` with `/bin/agnsh` (`agnoshi/build/agnsh_agnos`) on the ext2 root. User-driven per the iron-burns-block-other-work discipline.

### Validated

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (the `vfs_close` change touches every fd close ‚Äî FAT/exFAT/ext2 write + exec all green), `FS_SYSCALL_SELFTEST` **`fssys: ALL PASS`**, `SYSCALL_HARDEN_SELFTEST` **`shsys: ALL PASS`**, `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS**, `check.sh` 11/11. Build **1,070,720 B**.

### Notes

- **The 1.41.x shell-separation arc is software-complete (1.41.1 ‚Üí 1.41.11).** It is the first userland binary promoted to a system component and locks the permanent kernel‚Üîuserland shell boundary (kernel = recovery only, agnsh = full interactive). Iron burn PENDING (staged above).


## [1.41.10] ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (**Arc-close hardening ‚Äî audit the write path the 1.41.5 sweep predated.** The 1.41.5 security sweep ran *before* 1.41.7 added the `VFS_SEC_WFILE` FAT/exFAT write-fd, so that whole surface ‚Äî open-routing, the `vfs_write`/`vfs_close` arms, the static pool, the flush ‚Äî had never been audited. A 3-dimension multi-agent adversarial audit surfaced **4 real findings** (3 medium / 1 low, 0 refuted); the regression-recheck dimension confirmed the 1.41.5 fixes are all intact after the 1.41.6 dedup/reorder + the 1.41.8/1.41.9 shell work. 3 fixed here; the 1 low rolls to 1.41.11. Every fix behavior-preserving ‚Äî sweep 7/7, `fssys`/`shsys` PASS, agnsh-smoke PASS.)

### Fixed

- **Write-fd pool leak ‚Üí ring-3 DoS of FAT/exFAT writes (medium, security).** The `VFS_SEC_WFILE` write-fd is backed by a finite 4-slot static pool, released only by `vfs_close`. But process exit (`syscall(0)`) and `proc_reap` never walked the fd table ‚Äî so a buggy/malicious ring-3 program could `open(AO_WRONLY)` four FAT/exFAT files and `exit()` without closing, permanently exhausting the pool (every later write-open returns -1 until reboot). `proc_reap` now **sweeps fds 3-31 of the exiting process and `vfs_close`s any still-open** (POSIX `exit()` semantics; `vfs_close` is idempotent so a program that closed its own fds sees a no-op). Safe under the single-foreground run-to-completion model ‚Äî the kernel shell closes its fds synchronously, so at reap any open fd belongs to the just-exited process. (`core/proc.cyr`.)
- **Write-fd returned 0 at the content cap, spinning a conforming writer (medium, hardening).** `vfs_sec_wfile_write` returned `0` once the 4 KB whole-file cap filled ‚Äî a standard ring-3 `while (off < total) off += write(...)` loop would then spin forever the moment a file exceeded 4 KB. It now returns an **error** (`-1`) when the cap is full and bytes remain, so a >4 KB write fails visibly (the 4 KB cap is a known follow-on limit). (`core/vfs.cyr`.)
- **Write-open of a directory corrupted the filesystem (medium, correctness).** A write-open whose path resolved to an existing **directory** flushed through `fatfs_write_file` / `exfat_write_file`, which cleared the directory's dirent and freed its cluster chain ‚Äî on-disk FS corruption. Both backends now **refuse to overwrite a directory** (the `attr & 0x10` directory bit: FAT at the matched dirent +11; exFAT by re-reading the set primary's `FileAttributes` +4, mirroring `exfat_rmdir`). Only fires on the malformed write-to-a-dir case. (`core/fatfs.cyr`, `core/exfat.cyr`.)

### Validated

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** ‚Äî the FAT/exFAT/ext2 write smokes (write-fd + the dir-guards on the normal write path) and exec-from-disk (which exercises the new `proc_reap` fd-sweep on every `run`) all pass. `FS_SYSCALL_SELFTEST` **`fssys: ALL PASS`**, `SYSCALL_HARDEN_SELFTEST` **`shsys: ALL PASS`**, `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS**, `check.sh` 11/11. Build 1,070,288 ‚Üí **1,070,720 B**.

### Notes

- **Rolled to 1.41.11** (the clean cut): the 1 low finding ‚Äî `open(7)` AO_CREAT on FAT/exFAT ignores `vfs_create_on`'s result before opening a write-back fd (the audit's proposed fix was not behavior-sound, so it gets a careful pass) ‚Äî plus final polish + iron-burn staging.


## [1.41.9] ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (**The in-kernel shell shrinks to a recovery REPL ‚Äî the permanent kernel‚Üîuserland shell boundary is locked.** With agnsh (userland) now the interactive shell (1.41.4) and the FS-write test harness decoupled from the shell verbs (1.41.8), the non-recovery verbs in the in-kernel `shell()` are deleted. `kernel/user/shell.cyr` drops **1149 ‚Üí 813 LOC (‚àí336)** and the production kernel **1,084,312 ‚Üí 1,070,288 B (‚àí14 KB)**. The kernel shell is now strictly a recovery fallback: it owns just enough to inspect and repair a broken `/bin/agnsh`. The full verb surface + AI/intent features live in agnsh. This is the kernel-slimming half of the shell-separation arc.)

### Removed

- **Non-recovery verb handlers + dispatch (`kernel/user/shell.cyr`).** Deleted `sh_cmd_touch`, `sh_cmd_mkdir`, `sh_cmd_rmdir`, `sh_cmd_ln`, `sh_cmd_bench` (+ its sole helper `bench_report`), `sh_cmd_ps`, `sh_cmd_free`, `sh_cmd_jbd2`, and `sh_echo_redirect`, plus each one's `sh_exec` dispatch line and its entry in the `help` menu text. The `echo` verb shrinks to a bare print (the `> FILE` redirect path is gone). The dead `sh_write_selftest` (redundant since 1.41.8 migrated the ext2 shell-write test to syscalls) is also removed.
- **Kept (the recovery set):** `help`, `cd`, `pwd`, `ls`, `cat`, `run`, `mv`, `rm`, `sync`, `reboot`, plus the non-write diagnostics/utility verbs (uptime / lspci / cpus / net / parts / date / clear / version / ‚Ä¶). `rm` and `mv` stay because repairing a broken `/bin/agnsh` means deleting it and moving a backup into place. The shared path helpers (`sh_trim` / `sh_abspath` / `sh_split_parent` / `sh_cwd_*` / `sh_is_dir` / `sh_streq` / `sh_parse_ipv4`) stay ‚Äî still used by the kept verbs.

### Validated

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** ‚Äî the FAT/exFAT/ext2 write smokes boot **into** the in-kernel recovery shell (no `/bin/agnsh` seeded), so they directly exercise the shrunk shell + the kept verbs (`cat`/`rm`/`mv`/`sync`); all green. `FS_SYSCALL_SELFTEST` **`fssys: ALL PASS`**, `SYSCALL_HARDEN_SELFTEST` **`shsys: ALL PASS`**, the recovery `agnos>` prompt comes up, `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS** (boot-to-agnsh still launches agnsh, no fallback), `check.sh` 11/11.
- The deletion was performed by a subagent from the 1.41.8 completeness spec, then **independently re-verified**: a grep confirms zero remaining references to any deleted symbol (no dangling dispatch / caller), all 9 recovery verb handlers are present, and the kernel builds clean.

### Notes

- This **locks the permanent boundary**: kernel shell = recovery only; agnsh = full interactive. It does not collapse ‚Äî the emergency shell is the boot fallback whenever no userland shell is reachable. Remaining arc work: **1.41.10** ‚Äî arc-close hardening + the combined iron burn (agnsh interactive on real Zen).


## [1.41.8] ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (**Decouple the FS-write test harness from the in-kernel shell verbs.** The migrate half of the shell-shrink, done first for safety (migrate-then-delete). The FAT/exFAT/ext2 write **selftests** drove the shell's write verbs via `sh_exec("touch /mnt/fat/‚Ä¶")`, so those verbs couldn't be deleted without breaking the smokes. They now drive the write path via **syscalls** ‚Äî the same path agnsh uses ‚Äî so the verbs are undriven by any test and 1.41.9 can delete them with zero coverage loss. The smokes pass **unchanged** (same backend ‚Üí identical on-disk files); sweep 7/7. Planned by a multi-agent map + adversarial completeness spec, which caught ‚Äî and which I then re-derived past ‚Äî an off-by-one path length the spec text itself miscounted.)

### Changed

- **FAT/exFAT/ext2 write selftests ‚Üí syscall-driven (`core/main.cyr`).** New ungated helpers `sc_touch`/`sc_echo`/`sc_mkdir`/`sc_rmdir` issue the real syscalls (`open(AO_CREAT)` / `open(AO_WRONLY|CREAT|TRUNC)`+`write` / `ksyscall(9)` / `ksyscall(10)`) against a self-allocated `pmm_alloc_2mb` user-range scratch, with `strlen`-computed path lengths (no hardcoded-length bugs ‚Äî the failure mode the planning spec warned about and itself tripped on). The FATFS_WRITE / EXFAT_WRITE selftest drives for `touch`/`echo>`/`mkdir`/`rmdir` now call these helpers; `rm`/`mv`/`sync`/`cat` stay as `sh_exec` (they remain recovery-shell verbs). The exFAT `exfat_find` find-back checks + `exfatw:` markers are unchanged (the smoke greps them).
- **`sh_write_selftest` retired (`core/main.cyr` call site).** The ext2 shell-verb write self-test (redundant with `ext2_write_selftest` + `fssys`) is replaced by a syscall-driven block producing the same `/shdir/keep.txt` (content `SHELL-WROTE-IT`) + `/shtmp`-create-then-`rm`, so `ext2-write-smoke`'s `shtest:` marker + disk-state checks stay valid. The `sh_write_selftest` function in `shell.cyr` is now dead (deleted with the verbs in 1.41.9).

### Validated

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** ‚Äî FAT write, exFAT write, and ext2 W1-W5 smokes all pass with the syscall-driven drives (identical on-disk files: SHTOUCH/SHECHO/SHKEEP/SHRMD/SHMV*/SHSUB on FAT, the `exfatw:` find-backs on exFAT, `/shdir/keep.txt` + `/shtmp`-absent on ext2). No smoke-script changes were needed.

### Notes

- The in-kernel shell still has its full verb set this cut ‚Äî **nothing is removed yet**. 1.41.9 deletes the now-undriven non-recovery verbs (`touch`/`echo>`/`mkdir`/`rmdir`/`ln`/`bench`/`ps`/`free`/`jbd2` + `bench_report` + the dead `sh_write_selftest`), shrinking the shell to its recovery set (`help`/`cd`/`pwd`/`ls`/`cat`/`run`/`mv`/`rm`/`sync`/`reboot` + non-write verbs).


## [1.41.7] ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (**FAT/exFAT content-write via the syscall ABI.** Surfaced while planning the shell-shrink (1.41.8): `vfs_write` ‚Äî the `write(fd)` syscall ‚Äî handled only `VFS_EXT2_FILE`/pipe/device, so FAT/exFAT *content*-write was reachable **only** from the in-kernel shell's `echo>` (`vfs_write_secondary`). That meant agnsh couldn't write a data partition, and the FAT/exFAT write tests were shell-coupled ‚Äî which would have stranded the capability when the shell verbs are deleted. So this lands first: a real write path so the shrink is clean. New `VFS_SEC_WFILE` write-fd ‚Äî `open(7)` with write-access on a FAT/exFAT mount returns a fd that buffers content and flushes the whole file to the backend on close. New syscall-driven `syswr:` selftests write+read-back real content on **both** FAT and exFAT disks. Validated: `sweep.sh` 7/7 (FAT + exFAT write smokes now gate `syswr:`), `fssys`/`shsys` PASS, `agnsh-smoke` PASS, `check.sh` 11/11.)

### Added

- **`VFS_SEC_WFILE` ‚Äî FAT/exFAT write-back fd (`core/vfs.cyr`, `core/syscall.cyr`).** `open(7)` with an access mode of `AO_WRONLY`/`AO_RDWR` (the low 2 ABI flag bits) on a path that mount-routes to FAT/exFAT now returns a write-back fd instead of the read-only memfile. `vfs_write` appends into the fd's buffer; `vfs_close` flushes the whole file via `vfs_write_on` (‚Üí `fatfs_write_file`/`exfat_write_file`). `AO_CREAT` still creates first; `AO_TRUNC` is implicit (whole-file replace). This is the first time a userland program can write a FAT/exFAT (non-ext2) filesystem.
- **Static write-fd pool (`sec_wfile_buf` / `sec_wfile_inuse`).** Backing store is 4 reusable 4360-byte blocks **freed on close**, not `kmalloc` ‚Äî the kernel heap is alloc-only (no `kfree`), so a per-fd `kmalloc` both leaks *and* exhausts under repeated writes (this is exactly what the first cut hit: an 8 KB `kmalloc` failing late in the selftest). 4 concurrent write fds suits the single-foreground run-to-completion model.
- **Syscall-driven FAT + exFAT write selftests (`syswr:`, `core/main.cyr`).** Drive `open(AO_WRONLY|CREAT|TRUNC)` ‚Üí `write` ‚Üí `close` ‚Üí read-back through `ksyscall` on the `/mnt/fat` and `/mnt/exfat` mounts; the FAT case lands `SYSCALL-FAT-WR` (14 B) and exFAT `SYSCALL-EXFAT-WR` (16 B) on real disks. Gated in `fat-write-smoke.sh` + `exfat-write-smoke.sh`.

### Notes

- **Limits (follow-ons):** the write fd is whole-file with a **4 KB content cap** per fd; larger files (streaming writes) and `AO_APPEND` (load-existing-then-append) are deferred. A freeing allocator would let the pool grow / the buffer be `kmalloc`'d without leaking.
- This unblocks the shell-shrink (1.41.8): FAT/exFAT write is now syscall-reachable and its tests are syscall-driven, so the in-kernel shell's write verbs can be deleted without losing coverage.


## [1.41.6] ‚Äî 2026-06-04 (**Refactor + exec-hardening ‚Äî the deferred half of the 1.41.5 sweep.** 1.41.5 shipped the hardening/security but punted the *refactoring* and one medium exec-path fix; this cut finishes them as a dedicated, independently-validated pass rather than letting them ride the far-off arc-close. First a **safety net**: a new `SYSCALL_HARDEN_SELFTEST` (`shsys: ALL PASS`) that regression-locks every 1.41.5 security fix AND covers the epoll/timerfd/signalfd region, which had no other smoke ‚Äî so the dispatcher refactors run against a real test. Then the deferred items, each behavior-preserving and verified by `shsys` + `fssys` + `sweep` + `agnsh-smoke`. The risky #23 single-tail canary rewrite stays **rejected** (documented) ‚Äî it's a non-vuln cosmetic that would mean rewriting ~25 early-returns in the security-critical dispatcher.)

### Added

- **`SYSCALL_HARDEN_SELFTEST` (`core/main.cyr`, gated) + `build.sh` flag.** Drives the 1.41.5 security-rejection paths through `ksyscall` with a real `pmm_alloc_2mb` user-range scratch: the `is_user_range`/`is_user_ptr` 1 GB ceiling (valid / ceiling / low), epoll_ctl & epoll_wait type-confusion rejection on a non-epoll fd + the out-of-range watched-fd bound + the valid path, timerfd_settime type-confusion rejection + valid, signalfd, and the ext2 256-byte basename rejection. `shsys: ALL PASS`. This is the first regression coverage for the epoll/timerfd/signalfd syscalls.

### Fixed

- **#11 ‚Äî malformed ELF leaked per-process page tables (medium).** `elf_load` and `elf_load_from_file` validated each PT_LOAD segment's bounds *interleaved with mapping*, **after** `proc_create_address_space` ‚Äî so a bad segment returned `-1` having already leaked the new page tables + any pages mapped so far. Both now run a **validation pre-pass** over all segments **before** allocating the address space; a malformed ELF is rejected with zero allocations to leak. Chosen over adding teardown to every failure path in the iron-validated exec path. (The mapping loop keeps its checks as defensive no-ops for valid input. A rare OOM *mid-mapping* leak is a separate, lower-priority class, not addressed here.)

### Changed

- **#22 ‚Äî FS-syscall dispatch dedup (`core/syscall.cyr`).** New `sc_path_ok(ptr, len)` factors the path-argument preamble (`len >= 1` AND `is_user_range`) shared by `open`/`mkdir`/`rmdir`/`unlink`/`rename`/`link`/`stat` ‚Äî each call site keeps its own canary/return convention exactly, so this is pure factoring. The duplicated identical-body `FS_FAT` / `FS_EXFAT` arms in `mkdir`/`rmdir`/`unlink` collapse to a single `||` branch (both already dispatched to the same `vfs_*_on`). The deeper resolve-+-per-backend dedup is intentionally **not** done ‚Äî the syscalls diverge (AO_* flags, `a4`, ext2-only) and it isn't behavior-provable.
- **#21 ‚Äî `ksyscall` dispatch order (`core/syscall.cyr`).** The dispatcher is organized by *tier* (stubs / signals / epoll), which is intentional; only the trailing 1.41.3 FS group had been appended out of order (`30, 29, 33, 31, 32`). Reordered to numeric (`29, 30, 31, 32, 33`). Each `if (num == K)` block is self-contained, so this is behavior-identical ‚Äî verified by confirming every syscall number 0-33 appears exactly once (no dupes/gaps).
- **#23/#14 ‚Äî stack-canary coverage (documented, not changed).** A rationale comment now records why coverage is deliberately non-uniform: `ksyscall` has no local array buffer for a smash to corrupt, so its canary checks are a *callee*-smash backstop (the leaf functions that own buffers ‚Äî e.g. `elf_load` ‚Äî carry their own). A single-tail collapse was rejected as a risky rewrite of the dispatcher for a non-vuln cosmetic. The audit downgraded both (#14 reach=False, #23 fix_sound=False).

### Validated

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (exec-from-disk + ENOEXEC exercise the #11 pre-pass ‚Äî valid ELF loads, non-ELF refused), `FS_SYSCALL_SELFTEST` **`fssys: ALL PASS`**, `SYSCALL_HARDEN_SELFTEST` **`shsys: ALL PASS`**, `scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh` **PASS** (boot-to-agnsh through the refactored dispatcher), `check.sh` 11/11. Production build 1,063,016 ‚Üí **1,063,944 B**.

## [1.41.5] ‚Äî 2026-06-03 (**Syscall-ingress hardening / security sweep.** The 1.41.x arc put a real userland shell on the far side of the syscall boundary and 1.41.3 added nine FS syscalls that take user pointers ‚Äî so the ring-3‚Üíring-0 ingress is now a genuine attack surface. A 6-dimension multi-agent audit with adversarial verification surfaced 26 findings (10 high / 3 medium / 13 low, 0 refuted; the high cluster cross-converged from two independent dimensions). **15 fixed this cut** ‚Äî all 10 high, 2 medium, 3 low ‚Äî every one behavior-preserving. The headline is **fd type-confusion**: `epoll_ctl`/`epoll_wait`/`timerfd_settime` never checked the fd's `ktag`, so a ring-3 caller could pass any other fd type whose `payload[2]` is a kernel heap pointer (pipe/memfile) or a small integer (an ext2 fd's inode number) and turn the handler's `store64`/`load64` into an **arbitrary kernel write / read** ‚Äî closed with `VFS_*` tag checks mirroring the `getdents` precedent, plus a bound on the watched fd so it can't index `vfs_table` out of range. Validated: `sweep.sh` **7/7**, `FS_SYSCALL_SELFTEST` **`fssys: ALL PASS`**, `agnsh-smoke` **PASS** (real ring-3 agnsh boots to its prompt), `check.sh` 11/11. Full audit + the 8 deferred findings: agnosticos [`kernel-1415-hardening-audit.md`](https://github.com/MacCracken/agnosticos/blob/main/docs/development/prior-art/kernel-1415-hardening-audit.md).)

### Security

- **fd type-confusion ‚Üí arbitrary kernel write/read (high).** `epoll_ctl`(20) and `epoll_wait`(21) now require `ktag(epfd) == VFS_EPOLL` before treating the fd's `payload[2]` as a watch-list pointer; `timerfd_settime`(23) requires `VFS_TIMERFD` before writing the fd's payload. Without these a ring-3 process could `open` a real file (or `pipe`) and pass it as the epoll/timer fd, redirecting the handler's stores/loads to an attacker-influenced kernel address. (`core/syscall.cyr`.)
- **`vfs_table` OOB via unbounded watched fd (high).** `epoll_ctl` now bounds the watched fd (`arg3`) to `[0,32)` at insert, and `epoll_wait` re-checks it before `&vfs_table + wfd*32` ‚Äî previously an unbounded `wfd` gave an arbitrary-offset kernel read.
- **No user-VA ceiling (high).** `is_user_ptr`/`is_user_range` only rejected the low kernel region (< 2 MB); they now also reject `>= 0x40000000` (1 GB). Every legitimate user address is below 1 GB (ELF segments < 34 MB, the per-pid stack < 72 MB, the mmap arena `[0x10000000,0x40000000)`); device MMIO/BARs (NVMe ~0xC0000000) and the 1-4 GB range the per-process CR3 also mirrors sit above it, so without the ceiling a ring-3 caller could aim a kernel `load64`/`store64` at MMIO.
- **ext2 directory-buffer overflow (high).** A ring-3 path with an over-long basename overflowed `ext2_dir_buf` (512 u64 = 4 KB) in the fresh-block dirent append path (`mkdir`/`open`-`O_CREAT`/`rename`-dst/`link`-dst). `vfs_ext2_parent` now caps the basename at ext2's 255-byte max ‚Äî a single ingress covering every FS-mutation syscall.
- **`spawn` ELF buffer unvalidated (high).** `spawn`(3) passed the user `elf_addr`/`elf_size` straight to `elf_load`'s `load8`/`load64`; it now `is_user_range`-validates the whole image first, so a ring-3 caller can't aim the loader at kernel memory.
- **Single-pointer-then-8-byte loads (high/low).** `sigprocmask`(17) and `signalfd`(18) validated a bare pointer then `load64`/`store64` 8 bytes; now validated as a range. `epoll_wait` validates the events buffer for the bytes actually written (`arg3 * 12`) rather than a single ptr.

### Fixed

- **ELF segment can alias the mmap arena (medium).** `elf_load` + `elf_load_from_file` bounded `p_vaddr` low but not high; a crafted `p_vaddr` could land a segment inside `[0x10000000,0x40000000)` and alias the process's own future `mmap`/`munmap`. Now capped at `p_vaddr + p_memsz <= 0x10000000`. (`core/elf.cyr`.)
- **`sys_mmap` length-overflow (medium).** `(length + 0x1FFFFF)` wrapped for a near-u64 length, slipping past the `> 0x40000000` arena guard; a `length > 0x40000000` reject is now applied before the rounding. (`core/proc.cyr`.)
- **`fatfs_read` FAT-chain cycle guard (low).** Added the iteration counter `exfat_read` already has ‚Äî a corrupt FAT chain looping among in-range clusters would otherwise re-read to `maxlen`. (`core/fatfs.cyr`.)
- **`epoll_create` OOM (low).** Allocates the watch list before claiming the fd slot, so an OOM leaks nothing and never hands back an epoll fd with a NULL watch list. (`core/syscall.cyr`.)
- **`dns_qname_encode` length cap (low).** Caps the hostname at 255 before encoding into the 320-byte query buffer. (`core/net_dns.cyr`.)

### Deferred (tracked to 1.41.7 arc-close)

- **#11 ELF-leak teardown (medium)** ‚Äî a malformed on-disk/spawn ELF leaks per-process page tables + already-mapped 2 MB pages; a correct fix needs teardown (or a validation pre-pass) in the iron-validated exec path and deserves its own focused bite, not bundling into a broad sweep.
- **#16 `proc_reap` non-LIFO slot leak (low)** ‚Äî non-triggering under the current run-to-completion single-foreground model.
- **#21/#22/#23 refactors** ‚Äî ksyscall numeric reorder + FS-syscall mount-dispatch dedup + canary single-tail. #22/#23 aren't provably behavior-equivalent and #21 is a large diff in the security-critical dispatcher ‚Äî they belong in a separate pure-refactor cut validated independently, not behind a security diff.

## [1.41.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-03 (**Shell-separation bite 3: first boot-to-agnsh-on-disk.** PID 1 (`kybernet`) now `exec`s the real `/bin/agnsh` ‚Äî the agnos-target build of the agnoshi shell ‚Äî in ring 3 from the ext2 root, instead of dropping into the in-kernel emergency shell. The in-kernel `shell()` becomes a fallback only. This is unblocked by the **cyrius 6.0.56** toolchain leap (pin `6.0.14` ‚Üí `6.0.56`), which landed the `CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` stdlib peer the shell needed: `lib/args_agnos.cyr` + an init-stack capture (6.0.55) closed the `args_init()` `#UD` that had been killing `agnsh` at startup, the `lib/io.cyr` agnos file-op ABI miscompile was fixed, and `lib/process_agnos.cyr` (6.0.56) gave agnos a real `sys_spawn`-based `exec`. Validated end-to-end in QEMU: `agnsh-smoke` boots the production kernel + an ext2 image seeded with `/bin/agnsh`, and `kybernet` reaches the **agnsh prompt** in ring 3 with **no `#UD` and no emergency-shell fallback** ‚Äî PASS √ó3, deterministic.)

### Added

- **`kybernet_exec_agnsh()` (`kernel/user/init.cyr`).** PID 1's init path now loads `/bin/agnsh` off the ext2 root via `elf_load_from_file`, sets it current, `exec_and_wait`s it in ring 3, reaps it (`proc_reap`), and returns its exit code. `kybernet()` calls it first; only if it returns nonzero (load failure / no agnsh on disk) does it fall back to the in-kernel `shell()` ("kybernet: emergency shell"). The emergency shell is now a safety net, not the primary UX.
- **`scripts/agnsh-smoke.sh`** ‚Äî boots a production kernel against a GPT disk whose ext2 partition is seeded with `/bin/agnsh` (the `cyrius build --agnos` agnoshi binary). PASS = `kybernet` reaches `exec /bin/agnsh` **and** does not print "emergency shell" (i.e. agnsh launched in ring 3, no fallback). Prints the boot tail for eyeball.

### Changed

- **Cyrius toolchain pin `6.0.14` ‚Üí `6.0.56`** (`cyrius.cyml`). The kernel is freestanding (vendors `klib/`, no host stdlib), so this is a `cycc` codegen bump only ‚Äî kernel rebuilds clean (multiboot2 ELF64, entry `0x1000a8`, 1,061,944 B). The boot-blocking work was on the **agnoshi** side of the toolchain (the agnos-stdlib `args`/`io`/`process` peer), now present in 6.0.56. No-regression: exec-smoke + FS-syscall sweep green.

 The mount-routed VFS gets a ring-3 syscall face ‚Äî `agnsh`'s filesystem builtins (`ls`/`mkdir`/`rm`/`mv`/`stat`/‚Ä¶) now have kernel calls to reach. Nine syscalls land against the frozen [`agnos-userland-abi.md`](docs/development/agnos-userland-abi.md): `open`(7) re-routed from initrd-only to mount-routed + flags, `mkdir`(9)/`rmdir`(10)/`sync`(12) made real (were stubs), and new `getdents`(29)/`unlink`(30)/`rename`(31)/`link`(32)/`stat`(33). Includes the **`a4=r10` ABI extension** (the syscall ABI grows 3‚Üí4 args for `rename`/`link`, per decision O2). All validated end-to-end in QEMU via a kernel-side selftest that drives every syscall through `ksyscall` with a real user-range scratch + ext2 side-effect verification ‚Äî `fssys: ALL PASS`; sweep 7/7 no-regression.)

### Added

- **`getdents`(29)** ‚Äî new. `open(AO_DIRECTORY)` returns a `VFS_EXT2_DIR` fd; `getdents` walks it from a saved byte-offset and emits **agnos-native dirent records** (¬ß4.2: `reclen`u16/`type`u8/`namelen`u8/`ino`u32/`name`, padded to 8) into the user buffer, resuming across calls. ext2 (the primary FS); FAT/exFAT dir-fd is a follow-on. New `ext2_open_dir` + `ext2_getdents` + `ext2_gd_type` (`core/ext2.cyr`).
- **`stat`(33)** ‚Äî new. Fills the 48-byte agnos stat struct (¬ß4.1: `st_mode`/`nlink`/`size`/`ino`/`blocks`/`mtime`) from the ext2 inode via new `ext2_fill_stat`. FAT/exFAT degrade to -1 for now (O4's `ino=0`+size/type fill is a follow-on).
- **`unlink`(30)** ‚Äî new. Mount-routed: ext2 via a new kernel-layer `vfs_ext2_parent` path-split (the syscall mirror of the shell's `sh_split_parent`, operating on a validated user pointer) ‚Üí `ext2_unlink`; FAT/exFAT ‚Üí `vfs_delete_on`.
- **`rename`(31)** ‚Äî new. Within one filesystem; uses **`a4`** for `newlen`. ext2 (`ext2_rename` with src/dst parent+basename) + FAT/exFAT (`vfs_rename_on`). Cross-FS rename refused.
- **`link`(32)** ‚Äî new. Hard link, **ext2-only** (`ext2_link`); FAT/exFAT return -1 (O4 ‚Äî no inodes/hard links). Uses `a4`.
- **`a4=r10` syscall ABI extension (decision O2).** The entry stub (`syscall_hw.cyr`) preserves the user's `r10` into `r9` as its first instruction (before the CR3-switch scratch clobbers `r10`); `r9` is untouched through the entry path and arrives as `syscall_handler`'s SysV 6th arg, stashed into a new `ksyscall_a4` global that `rename`/`link` read. **`ksyscall`'s 4-arg signature ‚Äî and its many in-kernel callers ‚Äî are unchanged.**
- **`FS_SYSCALL_SELFTEST`** kernel-side selftest (`core/main.cyr`, gated) + `build.sh` flag. Drives all nine syscalls through `ksyscall` with a `pmm_alloc_2mb` user-range scratch and verifies side effects on disk.

### Changed

- **`open`(7) re-routed** from `initrd_open`-only to `vfs_resolve_mount` ‚Üí `ext2_open` / `vfs_open_on` (FAT/exFAT), with `initrd` as the bare-name fallback. Gained the **flags arg** (a3): `AO_CREAT`(0x100) creates, `AO_TRUNC`(0x200) zeroes an existing file, `AO_DIRECTORY`(0x800) returns a dir-fd. No existing consumer depended on the initrd-only behavior (the bare-name fallback preserves it).
- **`mkdir`(9) / `rmdir`(10) / `sync`(12)** ‚Äî were tier-1 stubs returning 0; now real (mount-routed `ext2_mkdir`/`ext2_rmdir`/`vfs_*_on`; `sync` = `ext2_sync` + `blk_flush`).
- New `VFS_EXT2_DIR` fd type (8) in `core/vfs.cyr`.

### ABI / compatibility

The syscall **numbers and struct layouts are unchanged** from the frozen `agnos-userland-abi.md` (these rows were already DECIDED there); this cut implements them and adds the `a4=r10` register. The `a4` change is **additive** ‚Äî 3-arg syscalls are byte-identical in behavior; only `rename`/`link` read the 4th. `r9` is now clobbered by every syscall (it was already the case for `r8`), which is ABI-safe (both are SysV caller-saved). The ABI doc's "lands at 1.41.2" annotations are corrected to **1.41.3** (the boot_info side-bite took 1.41.2).

### Verified

- **`fssys: ALL PASS`** ‚Äî kernel-side selftest: `mkdir`‚Üí`open`(CREAT)‚Üí`stat`‚Üí`rename`‚Üí`getdents`(finds the renamed entry)‚Üí`unlink`‚Üí`rmdir`‚Üí`sync`, each verified against `ext2_path_lookup` on disk.
- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** ‚Äî the `a4=r10` stub change (runs on *every* syscall) and the `open`(7) re-route are non-regressing; the exec-from-disk gate (real ring-3 `write`/`exit`) stays green.
- The a4=r10 **register** delivery (r10‚Üír9‚Üí`sc_arg4`‚Üí`ksyscall_a4`) is exercised in the selftest at the backend (`ksyscall_a4` set directly) and is proven at the register level by analogy to the existing 5th arg already carried in `r8`; its first ring-3 consumer is `agnsh`'s `mv`.

### Notes

- **chdir/getcwd are intentionally NOT syscalls** (ABI ¬ß3.2 ‚Äî CWD is userland-owned; `agnsh` tracks its own and passes absolute paths). The roadmap's earlier "chdir/getcwd" mention for this bite is superseded by the frozen ABI.
- Shell-separation arc bite numbering after the 1.41.2 boot_info side-bite: **1.41.3 (this, FS syscalls) ‚Üí 1.41.4 `kybernet` execs `/bin/agnsh` ‚Üí 1.41.5 shrink in-kernel shell ‚Üí 1.41.6 arc-close + iron burn.** Still gated on the cyrius-side `CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` target before `agnsh` can actually run.

## [1.41.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-03 (**boot_info consumers: canary + ACPI RSDP fallback ‚Äî the agnos half of gnoboot v0.5.0.** A *parallel side-bite*, not shell-separation work: gnoboot v0.5.0 began filling the sovereign `boot_info` fields (`initramfs_phys`/`size`, `cmdline_phys`, `acpi_rsdp_phys`); this cut makes the kernel *read* them ‚Äî a boot-time canary reporting all three (proving the gnoboot‚Üíagnos wire), plus the one cheap real consumer: `acpi_init` falls back to `boot_info`'s RSDP when the legacy BIOS scan finds nothing, which is exactly the UEFI case. Validated end-to-end in QEMU ‚Äî the ACPI fallback genuinely fires under OVMF.)

### Added

- **boot_info canary (`core/main.cyr`, after `dev_init`)** ‚Äî reads `initramfs_phys` (0x10), `initramfs_size` (0x18), `cmdline_phys` (0x20), `acpi_rsdp_phys` (0x38) from the low <4 GB `boot_info_copy` and prints them (dual serial + FB) so gnoboot's pre-EBS fills are observable on QEMU (serial) and iron (FB). Guards `bi==0` + the `'AGNO'` magic, so a non-Path-C boot (bare `-kernel`, no gnoboot) reports `boot_info: absent` / `bad magic` instead of printing garbage.
- **ACPI RSDP boot_info fallback (`acpi_init`, `core/acpi.cyr`)** ‚Äî when `acpi_find_rsdp()`'s legacy EBDA / `0xE0000` ROM scan returns 0 (the UEFI case ‚Äî firmware publishes the RSDP via the EFI Configuration Table, not the legacy BIOS areas), fall back to `boot_info+0x38`, **validated** with the existing `acpi_check_rsdp` signature + checksum before trust. New `acpi_rsdp_via_bootinfo` flag; `main.cyr` appends `(boot_info)` to the `ACPI: RSDP at ‚Ä¶` line when the fallback fired.

### Verified

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (baseline check + FAT/exFAT read+write + ext2 write + exec-from-disk) ‚Äî no regression.
- **End-to-end** in the exec-smoke OVMF boot (booting gnoboot v0.5.0 ‚Üí agnos): canary prints `initramfs=0x0 sz=0x0` / `cmdline=0x0` (no ESP files) / `rsdp=0x1fb7e014` (gnoboot filled it from OVMF's ACPI table), and the **ACPI fallback actually fired** ‚Äî `ACPI: RSDP at ‚Ä¶ (boot_info)` ‚Äî because OVMF/q35 places the RSDP at ~531 MB, above the legacy scan range. Boot reaches `agnos>` cleanly.
- aarch64 untouched ‚Äî `core/main.cyr` + `core/acpi.cyr` are `#ifdef ARCH_X86_64`.

### Notes

- The kernel still does **not** consume `initramfs_phys` / `cmdline_phys` (no INDR-from-`boot_info` mount, no cmdline parser); those remain follow-ons. This cut wires the *one* cheap real consumer (ACPI) plus the canary. The initramfs **format contract** (sovereign INDR, not Linux `cpio.gz`) is still unsettled ‚Äî see the agnosticos roadmap.
- **Parallel to the shell-separation arc.** Taking the next patch slot (1.41.2) for this side-bite shifts the planned shell-separation bites down one: FS syscalls ‚Üí **1.41.3**, `kybernet` execs `/bin/agnsh` ‚Üí **1.41.4**, shrink in-kernel shell ‚Üí **1.41.5**, arc-close + iron burn ‚Üí **1.41.6**.

## [1.41.1] ‚Äî 2026-05-31 (**Shell-separation bite 1: blocking keyboard stdin from ring 3.** `read(fd=0)` now blocks on the keyboard and returns typed characters to a userland process ‚Äî the first *interactive* (blocks-on-input, not run-to-completion) ring-3 syscall, and the agnos-side capability the userland `agnsh` REPL needs. Implements ABI decision **O1 (RAW)**. Pre-built ahead of `CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` so there's no agnos-side blocker when agnoshi builds for the agnos ABI.)

### Added
- **`kbd_read_blocking(buf, count)` (`syscall.cyr`)** ‚Äî `read(fd=0)` routes here (other fds keep the `vfs_read` path). Blocks until at least one real character is typed, drains any further buffered keys (up to `count`), returns the count. **RAW line discipline** (ABI O1): no kernel echo, no line buffering ‚Äî the userland shell (agnsh's `completion.cyr`/`history.cyr`) owns echo + editing. `scancode_to_ascii` returns 0 for modifier/release codes; those are consumed but never counted, so a blocked read never returns 0-length.
- **Cooperative HID drain ‚Äî no interrupt/preemption dependency.** The loop busy-polls `kb_has_key()`, which calls `hid_poll()` (an MMIO poll of the xHCI HID transfer ring), so it drains the USB keyboard with **interrupts MASKED** (SYSCALL/SFMASK clears IF on entry). No `sti`, no `hlt`, no scheduler preemption mid-syscall ‚Äî sidesteps the preemptive-ring-3 arc entirely. Works under the per-process CR3 (the xHCI BAR is mirrored in the superset map, same as exec's console writes).

### ABI
- **`read(fd=0)` moves üîí FROZEN** in `docs/development/agnos-userland-abi.md` ¬ß3.1 (was ‚úÖ DECIDED). The cyrius `CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` peer can now mirror a *live* stdin contract.

### Known follow-on (not a blocker)
- **Busy-spin while idle** ‚Äî a blocked `read(fd=0)` spins one core until a key arrives (functionally correct, not power-friendly). A `sti`+`hlt` wakeup (sleep until a timer/HID IRQ, then re-poll) needs interrupts enabled mid-syscall ‚Üí the **preemptive-ring-3** arc. Acceptable under the single-foreground-program model until then.

### Validation
- **Build clean** (production **1,050,824 B**, banner `v1.41.1`); `kbd_read_blocking` wired into `read`. **`read(fd=0)` is dormant in every existing smoke** (none read stdin) ‚Üí `scripts/sweep.sh` is the no-regression gate, not a functional stdin test. **No automated stdin test is possible** (can't be driven without real keystrokes; a seeded test would hang a headless QEMU ‚Äî the same reason the in-kernel keyboard loop was never headless-tested). Functional validation is **iron-burn-pending**, combined with the first `agnsh`-on-agnos boot once `CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` lands.

## [1.41.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-31 (**Shell-separation arc OPEN ‚Äî boundary audit + staging mechanism.** The interactive shell moves out of the kernel: the in-kernel ring-0 `shell()` REPL gives way to the userland **`agnsh`** (agnoshi) binary exec'd from disk via the 1.40.x exec path, with the in-kernel shell shrinking to a permanent emergency/recovery fallback. This cut is the arc-open ‚Äî **audit + scaffolding, no kernel code** ‚Äî and it surfaced the gating prerequisite. The first userland binary promoted to a system component; the kernel-slimming counterpart to 1.40.x exec, mirroring the 1.37.5 font‚Üí`kashi` move.)

### Added
- **`scripts/stage-agnsh.sh`** ‚Äî staging mechanism: builds/places the `agnsh` shell binary at `build/rootfs/bin/agnsh` (the agnos-fs staging tree consumed by QEMU-smoke fs-population, `install-usb.sh --update`, or iron mount-modify). Validates a static x86-64 ELF64. Output tree is gitignored (build product).

### Audit / planning (no behavior change)
- **Boundary audit** (agnosticos `shell-separation-prior-art.md`): inventoried all 35 in-kernel shell verbs and classified the boundary ‚Äî user-facing verbs (`cat`/`ls`/`cd`/`echo`/`touch`/`rm`/`mkdir`/`rmdir`/`mv`/`ln`/`sync`/`run`/`dns`/`ping`/`date`/‚Ä¶) move to `agnsh` via syscalls; deep kernel diagnostics (`lspci`/`blkread`/`disk`/`parts`/`bench`/`jbd2`) stay in the emergency shell where the data lives.
- **Syscall gap analysis**: `agnsh`'s builtins need a mount-routed FS surface that doesn't exist yet ‚Äî `open`(7) routes to `initrd_open` **only** (can't reach the agnos-fs); `getdents`/`unlink`/`rename`/`link`/`stat`/`create` are **missing**; `mkdir`(9)/`rmdir`(10)/`sync`(12) are **stubs returning 0**. Plus a **blocking keyboard `read(fd=0)`** (today fd 0 ‚Üí serial). CWD is decided **userland-owned** (no kernel `chdir`/`getcwd`). exec-by-path, network syscalls, and the AI/`hoosh` layer are explicitly deferred.
- **Gating prerequisite surfaced ‚Äî `CYRIUS_TARGET_AGNOS` (cyrius-side).** `agnsh` is an OS-agnostic shell (zsh/bash-class); the Cyrius stdlib syscall layer only targets `CYRIUS_TARGET_LINUX`/`_WIN` today (Linux was the host target available to build against), so the current `agnsh` speaks the **Linux** syscall ABI and won't run on AGNOS's sovereign 28-syscall surface. The port is "add a Cyrius agnos target" (a `lib/syscalls_*_agnos.cyr` peer emitting agnos numbers), **not** "agnos answers Linux syscalls" (which would break the sovereign-surface invariant). Cyrius-gated, driven with the cyrius agent ‚Äî same class as PIE/TLS. The agnos-side bites (1.41.1 stdin, 1.41.2 FS syscalls) are necessary but not sufficient until that target lands.
- **Emergency-shell trigger decided** (user): fires on **any** boot-to-shell failure (exec-fail of `/bin/agnsh` being the immediate case) **plus a future user key-chord** to summon it on demand.

### Notes
- No `VERSION`/`version.cyr` engineering change beyond the cycle-open bump (1.40.14 ‚Üí 1.41.0); production build byte-behavior unchanged (only the version banner strings differ). Bite ladder + dependencies: `docs/development/roadmap.md` ¬ß *1.41.x ‚Äî Shell Separation Arc*.

## [1.40.14] ‚Äî 2026-05-31 (**1.40.x arc closeout ‚Äî process teardown/reaping + hardening.** The `14013_final*` iron burn validated the whole arc at once: exec-from-disk (1.40.9), the scheduler-reset fix (1.40.10), the boot-stack relocation (1.40.12), and the mount-namespace routing (1.40.13) ‚Äî the box runs `/bin/prog2` + `/bin/argv` in ring 3, drives FAT shell verbs while ext2 owns `/`, and rides clean through `Activating scheduler...` ‚Üí DHCP lease ‚Üí `Launching kybernet`. This closeout reclaims the one resource the exec path still leaked: per-process memory + proc-table slots. **NOTE: touches the exec/address-space path ‚Äî needs an iron re-burn to declare iron-validated; folds into the already-pending 1.40.13 Track-B burn.**)

### Added ‚Äî process teardown / reaping (the deferred 1.40.10 follow-on)
- **`proc_reap(pid)` + `proc_free_address_space(cr3)` (`proc.cyr`).** An exited program's address space is now torn down and its proc-table slot reclaimed. `proc_free_address_space` walks the per-process PD and frees every user huge page (ELF segments + stack + any un-`munmap`'d anonymous `mmap`) plus the three page-table pages (PML4/PDPT/PD); `proc_reap` marks the slot dead **first** (scheduler-safe on the single-core model ‚Äî `sched_next` only selects `state==1`), detaches the stale CR3, frees the AS, and does a LIFO `proc_count` decrement (the interactive `run` path is run-to-completion and one-at-a-time, so the reaped proc is always the top slot). Wired into `sh_cmd_run` after the exit code is read.
- **Why it was load-bearing.** The PMM 2 MB allocator serves 6 usable regions (1‚Äì7, region 1 reserved); each `run` consumed 2 (code + stack) and never returned them, so the **4th `run` hit `pmm_alloc_2mb` exhaustion** (`run: not an executable`) ‚Äî a harder wall than the 16-slot proc-table limit noted at 1.40.10. Both are now unbounded.
- **User pages are identified by the U/S bit, not a divergence scan.** The per-process PD copies the kernel identity map (flag `0x83` ‚Äî supervisor) into entries [0..510]; the loader overwrites specific entries with user pages via `proc_map_page` (flag `0x87` ‚Äî user bit `0x04` set). So "present + user-bit" exactly selects the loader's pages **regardless of physical address** ‚Äî a divergence-from-kernel scan is unreliable because the first `pmm_alloc_2mb` commonly returns region 2 (`0x400000`), which equals the code segment's own identity vaddr. `munmap`'d entries are not-present (skipped); kernel-mirrored entries are supervisor (left untouched); PD[511] is the user-CR3 stash, not a mapping.

### Removed
- **The dead `u_pml4`/`u_pdpt`/`u_pd` allocation in `proc_create_address_space`** ‚Äî a silent 3-page-per-exec leak. These minimal KPTI user tables were orphaned the moment the 1.40.3 KPTI collapse made the PD[511] stash store `new_pml4` instead of `u_pml4`; nothing has read them since (`proc_get_user_cr3`/`proc_map_page` resolve PD[511] = `new_pml4`, the same table). `proc_reap` could never reclaim them (orphaned = unreachable from the CR3 chain), so removing the allocation is required for the no-leak property. Per-process AS construction now allocates exactly 3 page-table pages, all reachable and all freed on teardown.

### Changed ‚Äî hardening
- **`vfs_resolve_mount` now resets `vfs_mnt_reloff = 0` on entry** (`vfs.cyr`) so the mount-relative offset is deterministic on every exit path ‚Äî a caller that reads it after an `FS_NONE` return gets 0, not a stale offset from a prior resolve. (No live bug today ‚Äî callers only read `reloff` after a successful FAT/exFAT resolve, which always sets it ‚Äî but it makes the contract total.)

### Validation
- **Reap is exercised by the existing smokes** (no interactive harness needed): the EXEC_SELFTEST boot runs `/bin/prog2` + `/bin/argv` *through* `sh_exec`‚Üí`sh_cmd_run`‚Üí`proc_reap`, so `exec-smoke.sh`/`sweep.sh` cover it. Added two grep-visible assertions to the selftest: **`reap: slot reclaim OK (pc stable)`** (proc_count unchanged across create-then-reap of both programs ‚Äî LIFO reclaim) and **`reap: 6x AS+page cycle, free stable OK`** (a tight loop directly exercising `proc_free_address_space` on 3 PT pages + a mapped user huge page, asserting the PMM 4 KB-pool **and** 2 MB-region free counts return to baseline ‚Äî no leak).
- `scripts/exec-smoke.sh` **7/7** (both reap assertions print OK; boot reaches `AGNOS shell v1.40.14`). `scripts/sweep.sh` functional arcs **6/6** ‚Äî FAT read/write+subdir, exFAT read/write+subdir, ext2 WRITE regression (W1‚ÄìW5), exec-from-disk ‚Äî **zero regression**. `check.sh` 11/11. EXEC-selftest build **1,054,936 B**; production build **1,050,392 B**.

## [1.40.13] ‚Äî 2026-05-31 (**VFS mount-point namespace: shell verbs now route by MOUNT, so FAT/exFAT are reachable through the shell while ext2 owns `/`.** This is the routing half of the 1.39.x "VFS generic-write lift" ‚Äî the load-bearing piece that was deferred behind a `// non-goal ¬ß5` comment and wrongly papered over as "functionally COMPLETE," then exposed by the `14013_boot_cmd_fails` iron burn. Also in this cut: `fatfs_init` prefers a non-ESP FAT data partition over the boot ESP.)

### Added ‚Äî VFS mount-point namespace (the routing half of the 1.39.x lift, finally built)
- **Shell verbs now route by MOUNT, not by the `ext2_active` flag.** New `{prefix ‚Üí backend}` mount registry + longest-prefix resolver in `vfs.cyr` (`vfs_mount_init` / `vfs_resolve_mount` / the `vfs_*_on` dispatchers): **ext2 at `/`, FAT at `/mnt/fat`, exFAT at `/mnt/exfat`, all live at once.** Every write/read verb (`touch`/`echo >`/`rm`/`mkdir`/`rmdir`/`mv`/`cat`/`ls`) resolves the absolute path to its owning backend and dispatches ‚Äî ext2 inode-wise, FAT/exFAT via the mount-relative path (the `fatfs_resolve_parent`/`exfat_resolve_parent` walkers already strip a leading `/`). `mv` is guarded to a single filesystem. `sh_abspath` lazy-inits the CWD to `/` so a verb driven before `sh_cwd_init` can't build a slashless bare path.
