- **Why this was the load-bearing half.** Before it, the shell could not reach FAT/exFAT on any box where ext2 was mounted (every real boot ‚Äî ext2 carries the exec arc's `/bin`): every verb took the ext2 branch. The `14013_boot_cmd_fails` burn is exactly that ‚Äî raw `fatfs_*` (`fatw:`) backend tests all PASS, every shell-routed `vfsw:`/`vfsu:` verb FAILS with `no such directory`/`no such parent`/`no such path` (strings that only exist in the ext2 branch). The QEMU smokes hid it by booting with no ext2 partition (`ext2_active==0` ‚Üí the secondary fallthrough). `touch /mnt/fat/x` now writes the FAT partition with ext2 still mounted at `/`.

### Changed
- **FAT/exFAT write/read selftests now drive `/mnt/fat` and `/mnt/exfat` paths** (`main.cyr`) instead of bare names, so they reproduce the real iron config ‚Äî FAT/exFAT *through the shell while ext2 owns `/`* ‚Äî which the bare-name version structurally could not (bare names only resolved to FAT/exFAT when ext2 was absent, i.e. the ext2-less smoke). Files still land at each filesystem's root (`/mnt/fat` *is* the FAT root), so `fat-write-smoke.sh`/`exfat-write-smoke.sh`'s `mdir`/`fsck` filename + content checks are unchanged.

### Removed
- **The 8 dead `vfs_*_secondary` dispatch helpers** (`vfs_create/write/delete/mkdir/rmdir/rename/open/print_dir_secondary`), superseded by the mount-routed `vfs_*_on` helpers. They routed by `vfs_secondary_select` (the ext2-flag era). `vfs_sync_secondary` stays ‚Äî `sync` is a global durability barrier, not a path lookup. `vfs_secondary_select` stays ‚Äî still used by `vfs_mount_init` + the exec read path (`vfs_read_file`). Build: 117 ‚Üí **109 unreachable fns**, 1,052,824 ‚Üí **1,050,200 B**.

### Fixed
- **`fatfs_init` mounted the boot ESP as the FAT volume instead of a FAT data partition.** On a real AGNOS drive, `p1` is the FAT32 ESP (the boot volume ‚Äî write-guarded) and any FAT *data* lives in a later MSFT-Basic partition. The old single-pass scan took the *first* FAT GUID it saw (the ESP), so every FAT write hit the ESP guard and failed (caught while prepping the Track-B boot-drive burn: `fat: mounted ‚Ä¶ partition_lba=2048` = the ESP). Now a two-pass scan: **pass 1** mounts the first non-ESP MSFT-Basic FAT (writable data partition; `fatfs_probe_backend` self-excludes a sibling exFAT MSFT-Basic partition, so it's skipped automatically), **pass 2** falls back to the ESP only if no FAT data partition exists. Single-ESP layouts ‚Äî the QEMU smokes, boot-only USBs ‚Äî are **unaffected** (pass 1 finds nothing ‚Üí pass 2 mounts the ESP with `fat_on_esp=1`, exactly as before). With a non-ESP FAT data partition present, all FAT write gates now pass against it (no `FAT_ALLOW_ESP_WRITE` override needed).

### Validation
- **Mount-namespace:** full `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** across three checkpoints (registry-only / verb-rewire / dead-code-removal) ‚Äî FAT read+write+subdir, exFAT read+write+subdir, ext2 WRITE regression (W1‚ÄìW5), exec-from-disk, **zero regression** ‚Äî and the FAT/exFAT smokes now pass while driving the verbs through explicit `/mnt/...` paths.
- **`fatfs_init` ESP-preference:** full `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (the FAT smokes still mount the single ESP via the pass-2 fallback). `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Also validated against an iron-shaped layout in QEMU (one disk = ESP + FAT32 data + exFAT data): FAT now mounts the data partition (`partition_lba` = the data part, not 2048) and all `fatw:` gates pass; `fsck.fat`/`fsck.exfat -n` clean on both.

### Notes ‚Äî Track-B (FAT/exFAT) iron verification plan
- **Now ONE burn, not two** ‚Äî the mount-namespace change removes the constraint that forced two. With the FAT backend at `/mnt/fat` and the exFAT backend at `/mnt/exfat` both active in a single boot (separate singletons, separate `*_active` flags; `fatfs_init` self-excludes exFAT volumes and vice-versa), one kernel built with **both** `FATFS_WRITE_SELFTEST` and `EXFAT_WRITE_SELFTEST` exercises both filesystems' shell verbs in the same boot. Provision a FAT32 **and** an exFAT MSFT-Basic data partition in the boot drive's unallocated tail (`archaemenid` = `nvme0n1`, ~906 GB free after `agnos-fs`), flash the selftest kernel to the ESP via `install-usb.sh --update`, boot, photograph the `vfsw:`/`vfsu:` FB lines (which should now PASS, unlike `14013_boot_cmd_fails`), then reboot to Linux and run host `fsck.fat`/`fsck.exfat`. User-driven; rides alongside the still-pending 1.40.12 boot-stack re-burn (any boot-to-shell confirms that too). *(Superseded the earlier two-burn plan, which assumed the VFS could mount only one secondary FS at a time.)*

## [1.40.12] ‚Äî 2026-05-31 (**Boot-stack memory-safety fix: the kernel stack was growing down into its own `.rodata` string literals. Surfaced as the long-standing red exFAT-read `upcase-checksum` smoke row ‚Äî but the root cause was general kernel memory corruption near the 2 MB line, not anything exFAT-specific.** The exFAT read path was correct all along; its success message just happened to be one of the literals the stack was stomping. Full `sweep.sh` now **7/7** (was 6/7).)

### Fixed
- **The boot stack no longer corrupts kernel `.rodata`.** `boot_shim.cyr` set `rsp = 0x200000` (the 2 MB line) and the stack grows downward ‚Äî fine when the kernel image was small, but the image **crossed 2 MB** during the 1.40.x arc, pushing `.rodata` up to `~0x1faee8‚Äì0x1fff4c`, *immediately below* `0x200000`. Every `push`/call/local-variable spill therefore wrote into the string-literal pool. Because the whole image links as a single **RWE** segment, those rodata writes succeed silently. Any literal landing in the top ~600 B of `.rodata` was clobbered at runtime; the exFAT read self-test's `"exfatu: upcase-checksum OK"` was one of them, so `exfat-smoke.sh` reported a chain-read failure even though the up-case `TableChecksum` **matched** (the read path was always correct). Caught precisely with a QEMU GDB hardware watchpoint on the corrupted byte: the writer was a `push %rax` with `rsp = 0x1ffbf8` ‚Äî i.e. the boot stack itself. **Fix:** relocate the boot stack to `0x380000` (all three sites in `boot_shim.cyr` ‚Äî the 32-bit pre-paging stack and both 64-bit entry paths). `0x380000` sits in PMM-reserved region 1 (`0x200000‚Äì0x400000`), above the image top (~`0x210000`, ~1.4 MB of downward headroom) and below the fixed `0x3F0000` syscall kstack ‚Äî clear of both. **This was latent corruption affecting more than exFAT** (any rodata literal near the 2 MB boundary), so it's a general memory-safety fix, not an exFAT change.
- **`sweep.sh` exFAT-read row ‚Üí green.** The previously-tracked "pre-existing exFAT-read red" (noted across the 1.40.x cuts) is resolved by the above; it was never an exFAT-backend defect.

### Validation
- `scripts/exfat-smoke.sh` **PASS** (mount + multi-cluster upcase chain-read + shell-ls dispatch). `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** (FAT read/write, exFAT read/write, ext2 write W1‚ÄìW5, exec-from-disk ‚Äî all regression-free; the boot-stack move touches every subsystem's execution and none regressed). `check.sh` **11/11**, `test.sh` **4/4**. Production build **1,046,736 B** (byte-size unchanged ‚Äî only immediate operands in the shim changed).
- **Iron re-burn recommended (user-driven):** this moves the most fundamental boot-path invariant (the kernel stack base) on real Zen. QEMU validates it thoroughly (full sweep green, boots to shell), and the change is sound ‚Äî `0x380000` is identity-mapped via the shim's `PD[1]` and PMM-reserved ‚Äî but the boot-stack base is exactly the class of thing where QEMU-vs-Zen can differ, so a confirmation burn is warranted before declaring it iron-validated.

## [1.40.11] ‚Äî 2026-05-31 (**FAT/exFAT Track-B opener: the "exfatprogs 1.3.2 mkfs format drift" issue was a MISDIAGNOSIS ‚Äî there is no drift. The exFAT backend reads and writes 1.3.2-formatted volumes correctly; the smoke had been run against a leftover exec-iron `build/agnos` that doesn't compile in the write self-test.** No kernel/backend change ‚Äî this cut is a test-harness ergonomics fix so the failure mode can never be misfiled again.)

### Fixed
- **`exfat-write-smoke.sh` no longer cascades 25 misleading red gates when run against the wrong kernel build.** Root cause of the [`2026-05-31-exfat-write-mkfs-1.3.2-drift`](docs/development/issue/2026-05-31-exfat-write-mkfs-1.3.2-drift.md) report: the smoke verified only that `build/agnos` *exists*, not that it was built with `EXFAT_WRITE_SELFTEST=1`. A production / `EXEC_SELFTEST` build (the 1.40.9 exec-triage default) boots fine but emits **zero `exfatw:` lines** ‚Äî AGNOS never mutates the volume ‚Äî so `fsck.exfat -n` saw the pristine `mkfs` image (clean, 0 files) and the run ended in the misleading `FAIL: fsck clean but no file counted (set not recognized)`. The "every row red, including months-old ones" pattern was the tell (AGNOS-never-wrote, not format-not-recognized). New wrong-build guard: when the kernel boots but produces no `exfatw:` lines, the smoke exits 2 with an actionable message ("rebuild with `EXFAT_WRITE_SELFTEST=1 ./scripts/build.sh`") instead of the red cascade. **The exFAT backend itself was never broken** ‚Äî a kernel built with the flag passes all 25 gates against a `mkfs.exfat -c 512` (exfatprogs 1.3.2) image, `fsck.exfat -n` clean (directories 3 / files 19). Verified both directions: production build ‚Üí exit 2 + guard; selftest build ‚Üí full PASS.
- **Same wrong-build guard added to the sibling write-smokes `fat-write-smoke.sh` (`fatw:`) and `ext2-write-smoke.sh` (`ext2w:`)** ‚Äî they shared the identical gap (existence-only build check), and the FAT row matters for the upcoming Track-B FAT/exFAT iron burn.

### Notes
- The [`exfatprogs 1.3.2 mkfs format-drift`](docs/development/issue/2026-05-31-exfat-write-mkfs-1.3.2-drift.md) issue is **RESOLVED (misdiagnosed)** ‚Äî no backend teach-the-new-default work was needed (the issue's own "next steps #3" speculated either teaching the backend or pinning mkfs flags; neither applies, since the format isn't drifting). The original report is preserved at the bottom of the issue doc as the misdiagnosis-of-record.
- Separately tracked (NOT this cut): the `sweep.sh` exFAT-**read** red noted in [1.40.10] (`upcase-checksum` chain read) remains a pre-existing item for the Track-B FAT/exFAT work.

## [1.40.10] ‚Äî 2026-05-31 (**Scheduler no longer triple-faults when a boot reaches the DHCP hlt-wait with dead exec procs in the table.** The 1.40.9 final iron burn cleared the exec-from-disk dispositive bar ‚Äî `/bin/prog2` and `/bin/argv` run in ring 3 on real Zen (`run: exit 42` / `run: exit 90` / `exec: selftest done`), base-maturity exec leg **iron-validated**. But the boot then reset right after `Activating scheduler...` ‚Üí `dhcp: DISCOVER`. DHCP was innocent: the now-*working* exec selftest is the first thing to ever leave live process-table state before `sched_active=1`, exposing a latent scheduler bug. QEMU never reproduced it because SLIRP answers DISCOVER before a timer tick lands in the hlt-wait.)

### Fixed
- **The scheduler no longer context-switches into a dead exec proc after `sched_active=1`.** In the production (KTEST-off) build, `proc_create`/`proc_current` setup is all compiled out, so **proc 0 is never built** ‚Äî but `exec_disk_selftest` *does* run, leaving `proc_count ‚â• 2` and `proc_current` pointing at the last (dead, `state=0`) exec proc. The first timer tick after scheduler activation (the `dhcp_init()` `arch_wait()` hlt) cleared `do_context_switch`'s `proc_count < 2` early-return (the guard that kept DHCP working at 1.32.9, when no procs existed), so `sched_next()` ‚Äî finding no `state==1` proc ‚Äî fell back to `return 0` (a dead exec proc), `cr3_load`'d its stale per-process CR3 and `iretq`'d into a ring-3 RIP under CS=0x08 (ring 0) ‚Üí triple-fault ‚Üí silent CPU reset (the #PF handler can't run with the wrong CR3/CS, hence a reset rather than a 1.40.9-style halt-with-stamp). Fix (`main.cyr`, `#ifndef KTEST`, right before `sched_active=1`): register **proc 0 = the kernel main thread** (already running; its context is saved on the first switch-away) and **proc 1 = a hlt idle thread** (`sched.cyr` `kernel_idle_loop` + a module-scope 4 KB stack), **both carrying the boot CR3 `0x1000`** (where `kernel_resume` leaves CR3 after the last exec). Because every schedulable proc shares one CR3, `do_context_switch`'s `new_cr3 != old_cr3` guard never fires ‚Üí `cr3_load` is never invoked ‚Üí preemption is pure register save/restore in a single shared address space ‚Äî a kernel-threads model (per-process-address-space preemption is the future multi-threading arc). The KTEST proc_a/proc_b context-switch test is untouched (`#ifndef KTEST`).
- **An exited program no longer leaves `proc_current` on a dead proc the scheduler can resurrect** (bite 2). `sh_cmd_run` sets `proc_current = pid` for exit-code attribution, but after the program exits `proc_current` stayed on that now-dead (`state=0`) proc ‚Äî so the next timer tick's `do_context_switch` took it as `old` and `proc_set_state(old, 1)` (line 150, deliberately unconditional since iron Attempt 14 ‚Äî not made conditional here) marked it ready again, and `sched_next` could then switch into it: an `iretq` into ring-3 user code under CS=0x08. `kernel_resume` now resets `proc_current = 0` (the kernel main thread registered before `sched_active=1`) right after clearing the return-point globals, so every post-exit tick switches only kmain‚Üîidle. Exit-code attribution is unaffected ‚Äî `sh_cmd_run` reads the local `pid` (`shell.cyr:765`) and the code was stamped before `kernel_resume` ran; during the boot selftest `sched_active=0`, so it's a no-op there. Without bite 1 this couldn't bite (boot reset first); together they make scheduler activation **and** interactive `run` crash-safe.

### Added
- **`kernel_idle_loop` + `kernel_idle_stack` (`sched.cyr`)** ‚Äî a real, schedulable kernel idle thread (registered as proc 1 in the production boot path) that does nothing but `hlt`-and-yield, giving the round-robin scheduler a valid same-CR3 proc to fall back to instead of a dead exec proc. Module-scope `var kernel_idle_stack[512]` = 4096 B (N√óu64 at module scope).

### Validation
- `scripts/exec-smoke.sh` **7/7** (exec selftest unregressed); build advances `Activating scheduler...` ‚Üí `Launching kybernet...` in QEMU with no fault. `scripts/sweep.sh` **6/7**, byte-identical to the clean-tree baseline ‚Äî the one red is **pre-existing exFAT-read** (`upcase-checksum` chain read), confirmed by re-running after `git stash` of this change. EXEC-selftest burn artifact **1,050,592 B** (`burn-prep.sh`, exit 0), burn-ready.
- The DHCP reset is **iron-timing-specific** (QEMU SLIRP answers DISCOVER before a timer tick), so QEMU validates *no regression* only; the fix itself is confirmed by the user-driven 1.40.10 re-burn (rubric ‚Üí agnosticos `iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-14010-cycle`).

### Known follow-on (not in this cut)
- **No process teardown.** Each `run` consumes a `proc_table` slot (`proc_count` grows, dead procs are never reclaimed); after ~14 interactive runs `proc_create_full` returns -1 and `run` fails. Exited procs' per-process page tables also leak. Process reaping (free slot + AS on `waitpid`/exit) is a later exec bite, orthogonal to the scheduler-crash fixes above.

## [1.40.9] ‚Äî 2026-05-30 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî iron-reset triage: real CPU-exception handlers.** The 1408 combined VFS+exec iron burn passed 3/4 first try ‚Äî storage trio, ext2-write suite, jbd2-clean, shtest, and the ENOEXEC refusal (A1/A2) all green ‚Äî but A3 (the `/bin/prog2` ring-3 run) **spontaneously reset the box** with no panic and no `EXEC-DISK-OK`. CMOS bisection (existing `ring3.cyr` pre-iretq stamp, `CMOS[0x50]=0x20`) proved the **entire load path succeeded** ‚Äî the fault is the ring-3 transition itself, on real AMD Zen, where QEMU `-cpu max` passes 7/7. Root cause of the *reset* (vs a diagnosable fault): the IDT pointed all 256 vectors at a bare `iretq`, so any fault during the transition mis-returned ‚Üí re-faulted ‚Üí #DF ‚Üí #TF ‚Üí silent reset. This cut installs real handlers so the next burn reports the cause instead of rebooting.)

### Fixed
- **CPU exceptions no longer triple-fault into a silent reset.** `idt_init` left every vector on a 2-byte `iretq` stub; for an error-code fault (#GP/#PF/#DF/#SS/#NP) the bare iretq pops the wrong frame and returns to garbage ‚Üí guaranteed triple-fault ‚Üí CPU reset. New `exc_handlers_init` (`idt.cyr`) installs self-contained handlers on the seven protection/fault vectors a broken ring-3 transition raises and that cannot legitimately fire during normal boot: **#UD(6), #DF(8), #TS(10), #NP(11), #SS(12), #GP(13), #PF(14)**. Each stamps its vector to `CMOS[0x54]` + magic `0xE5` to `CMOS[0x55]`, then `cli; hlt` (pure port I/O ‚Äî CR3-independent, no memory deref, robust at fault time). Vectors 0-5/7/9/15-31 stay on the bare default (installing all 32 halts boot ‚Äî benign vectors like #NM/7 fire in normal boot). Correct permanent kernel behavior **and** the diagnostic the silent reset denied us. **The 1409 re-burn confirmed it works: the box HALTED at `exec: running /bin/prog2` (no reset) with `CMOS[0x55]=0xE5` + `CMOS[0x54]=0x0E` ‚Üí #PF (vector 14).** (The follow-on CR2 re-burn below disproved the initial "first user VA" read ‚Äî it's a *supervisor* fault.)
- **`ext2_create` / `ext2_mkdir` no longer create duplicate directory entries.** The 1409 host `e2fsck -fn` came back DIRTY ‚Äî "Duplicate entry 'w3a.txt'/'prog2'", ~5 copies of every selftest name, "Filesystem still has errors." Both functions called `ext2_dir_insert` **unconditionally**, with no existence check (unlike `ext2_link`/`ext2_rename`, which already refuse), so re-running a create with the same name (a selftest seed across reboots on the persistent NVMe FS, or a shell `touch` of an existing file) appended a *second* dirent with the same name. Now lookup-first: `ext2_create` returns the existing inode (POSIX `open(O_CREAT)`-without-`O_EXCL`); `ext2_mkdir` returns it when it's a directory, else refuses a file/dir name conflict. Idempotent **and** closes a real base-FS correctness gap. (This was the true cause of the 1409 ext2w `create+write`/`mkdir`/`Wren`/`Whard`/`Wsym` MISMATCHes ‚Äî readback resolved to a stale duplicate inode.)

- **Per-process address space is now a faithful superset of the kernel map** (`proc_create_address_space`, `proc.cyr`). It previously replicated only PML4[0]‚ÜíPDPT[0..3] (0‚Äì4 GB); any kernel mapping ‚â•4 GB ‚Äî `vmm_remap_*` installs device-MMIO/high BARs at arbitrary `pdpt_idx`/`pml4_idx` in the kernel tables ‚Äî was **absent while a program ran**, so a program's syscalls doing kernel-side work (console, device I/O) under the per-process CR3 could fault on a high mapping. This is the gap the 1.40.6 NVMe-BAR fault hit (worked around then by `kernel_resume` restoring the boot CR3 on *exit*; that workaround is no longer load-bearing). Now mirrors **all** present kernel `PDPT[1..511]` and `PML4[1..511]` entries; kernel entries carry U/S=0 so ring 3 stays walled off. On archaemenid all BARs happen to be <4 GB (GPU `0xd0000000`, NVMe `0xfcd00000`) so it wasn't *this* hardware's exec fault, but it's a real correctness gap and the leading behavioral fix for the still-open ring-3 #PF.

- **`ext2_truncate_zero` no longer frees phantom blocks from a fast symlink's inline target** ‚Äî a real `rm`/unlink FS-corruption bug. A *fast* symlink (`i_blocks==0`) stores its target string *inside* the `i_block[]` array; `ext2_truncate_zero` walked those words as block pointers and freed any non-zero one. For `sl_f ‚Üí "/hl_b.txt"`, `i_block[2]` = `"t\0\0\0"` = `0x74` = **block 116** ‚Äî an inode-table block ‚Äî so `ext2_free_block(116)` produced e2fsck's "multiply-claimed block 116, shared with filesystem metadata" plus a one-block free-count drift *per delete*. Dormant until now because nothing had ever deleted a fast symlink (the Wsym tests create them and leave them); the new write-suite cleanup is the first caller, and a real shell `rm` of a symlink hits it too. Now guarded: a symlink with `i_blocks==0` owns no data blocks, so the block-walk is skipped entirely (slow symlinks, `i_blocks!=0`, still free their real data block normally).
- **The ext2 write self-test is now idempotent on a persistent FS** (`ext2_wst_clean` pre-clean). It created fixed-name fixtures and left them on disk ("kept for host verify"); on a persistent FS re-mounted across boots (an `--update` flash, vs a pristine hard reflash) those leftovers made every create-side assertion read MISMATCH ‚Äî a name that already exists no-ops the (dup-guarded) `ext2_create`, so the `i_blocks`/`nlink`/refuse-on-dup deltas the subtests assert on never fire. **This was the visible cause of the 1409 ext2w `sparse-alloc` / `mkdir` / `Wren` / `Whard` / `Wsym` MISMATCHes** on the persistent NVMe FS. A pre-clean now wipes any prior-boot fixtures before the suite runs ‚Äî placed *after* the W1 identity + W2 allocator checks (those hold the block bitmap in a shared scratch buffer across a read‚Üíwrite-back pair; cleaning mid-sequence stamps a dir block over the bitmap ‚Üí allocator corruption), and *before* the first fixture-creating test. The stale "non-destructive / e2fsck stays clean" comment on the selftest ‚Äî false since the W3+ bites ‚Äî is corrected to describe the pre-clean idempotency contract.

### Added
- **Exception-cause CMOS slots 0x54 (vector) + 0x55 (0xE5 magic)** ‚Äî read via agnosticos `read-boot-log.sh --verbose`. On the next iron burn an A3 fault reads back as e.g. `0x54=0x0D` (#GP ‚Äî bad CS/SS descriptor at iretq/sysret), `0x0E` (#PF ‚Äî CR2 = the unmapped VA), or `0x06` (#UD ‚Äî bad opcode, e.g. SYSCALL w/ EFER.SCE clear) instead of a reset.
- **#PF CR2 + error-code capture** (`idt.cyr`, vector 14 only ‚Äî 1409 follow-on). With the box now halting on #PF (not resetting) and the textbook U/S cause statically exonerated, the missing datum is *which* page faulted. The #PF stub additionally stamps `CMOS[0x5C]`=CR2 bits 23:16 (the code-vs-stack discriminator ‚Äî `0x40`=code `~0x4xxxxx`, `0x80`/`0xC0`=user stack), `0x5D`=CR2 15:8, `0x5E`=CR2 7:0, `0x5F`=the on-stack #PF error code (bit0 P, bit1 W/R, bit2 U/S, bit3 RSVD, bit4 I/D), via the proven 0x70/0x71 bank (the extended 0x72/0x73 bank read back noise at 1409). Overloads the r8169 RX slots (r8169 runs at boot, before exec ‚Üí safe last-writer on a #PF-halt boot). `read-boot-log` updated to decode them.
- **#PF CR2 high-byte capture ‚Äî bits 24:47** (`idt.cyr`, vector 14, 1409 follow-on #2). The low-24 capture is ambiguous: the static audit proved low `0x6190` is mapped, so the real fault address must be ‚â•4 GB ‚Äî but `(k<<24)|0x6190` reads back identical for every `k`. The #PF stub now also stamps `CMOS[0x60]`=CR2 31:24, `0x61`=39:32, `0x62`=47:40, giving the **full 48-bit canonical fault address** (#PF addresses are always canonical, so bits 48:63 are sign-extension ‚Äî not stamped). Slots `0x60-0x62` = retired xhci PORTPMSC range on the trustworthy 0x70/0x71 low bank. `read-boot-log` prints the full `CR2 = 0x‚Ä¶` and flags ‚â•4 GB explicitly. Stub grows 65‚Üí104 B (`exc_stub[96]`=768 B has room).

### Validation
- `scripts/exec-smoke.sh` **7/7**, `scripts/ext2-write-smoke.sh` **19/19**, `scripts/check.sh` **11/11** ‚Äî handlers + CR2 capture are dormant in QEMU (no fault fires; the transition works there) and the dedup guard is a no-op on a fresh image, so zero regression. Production build **1,038,304 B**; EXEC selftest build **1,042,176 B** (the flash-ready 1409 re-burn artifact).
- **Fast-symlink free fix + write-suite idempotency**: single-boot `ext2-write-smoke.sh` PASS + `e2fsck -fn` clean, and a **two-boot persistent test** (boot twice on the *same* image ‚Äî the `--update` scenario the fresh-image smoke never exercises) ‚Üí boot-2 all 20 in-kernel asserts OK + host `e2fsck -fn` **exit 0 clean** (before the fix: "multiply-claimed block 116" + off-by-one free count after boot-2). Full `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7** ‚Äî the `truncate_zero` guard sits on the shared unlink path, so the FAT/exFAT/ext2/exec arcs were all re-swept; no regression. EXEC-selftest build **1,040,688 B**.
- Pre-existing red (NOT this change): `scripts/exfat-write-smoke.sh` fails on the **clean baseline** too ‚Äî host `exfatprogs 1.3.2` `mkfs.exfat` format drift the AGNOS exFAT backend no longer recognizes (same class as the jbd2 CSUM_V3 host-default surprise). Tracked separately; orthogonal to exec.

### Boot-pipeline note
- agnosticos `scripts/cyrius.cyml` graduated 5.11.59 ‚Üí 6.0.14 (the long-deferred boot-side sweep; rebuilding `read-boot-log` for this triage was its next touch).

### CR2 re-burn result (2026-05-31, boots `1409_second_attempt_*`) ‚Äî diagnosis rewritten
- Ran on a **clean FS** (`e2fsck -fn` = 38 files, no dup; `/bin/prog2` inode 33 intact). Captured `CMOS[0x5C-0x5F]` = CR2 low-24 `0x006190` + #PF error code **`0x00`** = **not-present ¬∑ read ¬∑ SUPERVISOR (CPL0) ¬∑ data**. This **disproves the "first user VA after the iretq" read** (a user fault would be `U/S=1`, CR2 ‚â• 0x400000). It's a kernel-mode read of an unmapped address whose low 24 bits are `0x6190`.
- **Static audit (this cut):** every address the exec‚Üí`iretq`‚ÜíSYSCALL‚Üí`vfs_write`‚Üíexit path touches is mapped under the per-process CR3 (kernel @ 1 MB, all RAM ‚â§16 MB pool, all BARs <4 GB, boot_info <4 GB; the `0x7000` iretq-frame write that *precedes* `CMOS[0x50]=0x20` proves PD[0] is present). So literal low `0x6190` cannot be not-present ‚Äî CR2's byte 2 = `0x00` means the real address is `(k<<24)|0x6190` and the only unmapped such values are **‚â•4 GB**. Nothing in the path legitimately computes a ‚â•4 GB address ‚áí a **wild/garbage ‚â•4 GB pointer**, unlocalizable from the 3-byte capture.
- **Next (both fixes now in the flash-ready binary):** the per-process-CR3 superset fix (leading behavioral candidate) AND the full-CR2 capture are folded into open 1.40.9. A re-burn now either (a) boots past `exec: running /bin/prog2` to `selftest done` (the superset fix was it) or (b) halts and `read-boot-log` prints the real ‚â•4 GB `CR2 = 0x‚Ä¶` ‚Äî the wild pointer's actual address ‚Äî instead of the truncated `0x6190`. Either way the next burn is dispositive. exec-smoke 7/7, no regression; EXEC-selftest build 1,043,440 B.

### Full-CR2 re-burn result (2026-05-31) ‚Äî ROOT CAUSE LOCALIZED + FIXED: gnoboot's high UEFI load address
- The dispositive re-burn halted again at `exec: running /bin/prog2` (no reset; the superset fix was **not** it) and `read-boot-log` printed the **full address**: `CR2 = 0x000140006190`, err `0x00` = not-present ¬∑ read ¬∑ **SUPERVISOR (CPL0)** ¬∑ data, **`RIP (low 32) = 0x00104fc8`**. The RIP is the new datum: it lands **inside the kernel image** (loaded at 1 MB), so the kstack/pmm fix worked ‚Äî no more `ret`-into-garbage (the prior burn's `RIP=0x22220202`). This is a legitimate kernel instruction doing a wild **data** read.
- **`0x104fc8` maps (via `objdump`) to `fb_fb_phys()`** (`fb_console.cyr`): `var bi = load64(&boot_info_ptr); if (bi == 0) return 0; return load64(bi + 0x48);`. The disassembly matches byte-for-byte ‚Äî the fault is the `load64(bi + 0x48)`. So `boot_info_ptr = CR2 ‚àí 0x48 = 0x140006148` (~5 GB), and the `== 0` null-guard passed (garbage ‚âÝ 0). The original truncated `0x6190` capture was exactly this `boot_info+0x48`.
- **Root cause: gnoboot is a UEFI PE app the firmware loads at an arbitrary base ‚Äî on archaemenid (RAM-rich Zen) that base is ‚â•4 GB**, so `RDI`/`&boot_info` ‚âà `0x140006148` (the low 32 bits `0x40006148` are the constant offset within gnoboot's image; the high bits are the load base, hence ‚â•4 GB). The kernel captures that pointer and reads `boot_info` **in place**. That works for the entire boot because **Path C inherits UEFI's all-RAM identity map** (`pt_init` does `mov cr3, cr3` ‚Äî a TLB flush of the *live UEFI* CR3, not a switch to its own 0x1000 tables), so every FB print up to `exec: running /bin/prog2` succeeds. But `exec_and_wait` switches to the **per-process CR3**, which maps only **0‚Äì4 GB** (`pt_init`'s ceiling ‚Äî and the kernel's own tables never mapped ‚â•4 GB, so the 1.40.9 superset-mirror had *nothing to mirror*). Under that CR3, prog2's first `write(1,‚Ä¶)` ‚Üí console ‚Üí `kputc` ‚Üí `fb_putc` ‚Üí `fb_fb_phys` reads `boot_info` at ‚â•4 GB ‚Üí unmapped ‚Üí #PF. QEMU/OVMF loads gnoboot **low** (<4 GB), so the bug never manifests there ‚Äî the exact QEMU-vs-Zen divergence. Same *class* as the 1.40.6 NVMe-BAR fault, but for an address no kernel table ever held.

### Fixed
- **`boot_info` is copied into a low (<4 GB) kernel buffer at capture; `boot_info_ptr` is repointed at the copy** (`mbi.cyr` `boot_info_capture_rdi`, new `boot_info_copy[16]` in `boot_data.cyr`). gnoboot's struct lives at the firmware's (possibly ‚â•4 GB) image base; reading it in place is fine on UEFI's identity map but faults under any kernel-built CR3 that stops at 4 GB. The capture now `memcpy`s the 120 B struct into `boot_info_copy` (resident in the kernel image, <2 MB, mapped under every CR3 via the per-process PD[0..510] copy) and stores `&boot_info_copy` into `boot_info_ptr`. The copy runs at the existing top-level capture call ‚Äî the first thing after the boot shim, while UEFI's all-RAM map is still the live CR3 and long before any per-process CR3 ‚Äî so the high-source read is safe. Every later `fb_fb_phys`/`fb_pitch`/`fb_width`/`fb_height` read is now **CR3-independent**. The framebuffer BAR itself (`0xd0000000`, 3.49 GB) is <4 GB and already mirrored into the per-process CR3, so pixel writes were never the fault ‚Äî reading `boot_info` to *find* it was.
- **Validation:** `scripts/exec-smoke.sh` **7/7** (`/bin/prog2` exit 42, argv exit 90, `selftest done`, `e2fsck -fn` clean), `scripts/sweep.sh` **6/7** ‚Äî the single red is the **pre-existing** FAT-write `mkfs`/LFN drift (red on a stashed clean tree; ext2-write + exFAT-write + exec all green). The fix touches only `boot_info` capture, so it's a no-op on QEMU (gnoboot loads low there) ‚Äî zero regression by construction. EXEC-selftest flash artifact **1,046,320 B**, burn-ready. **Next: dispositive re-burn ‚Äî expect `EXEC-DISK-OK` + `run: exit 42` on real Zen.** This is the last open leg of the base-maturity exit (FS-crash-safe ‚úì + exec-from-disk, iron-pending).

### Still open / deferred
- exFAT-write host-format drift (above) ‚Äî diagnose the `mkfs.exfat` 1.3.2 mismatch.
- Process teardown (free 2 MB pages on exit; lifts the 2-exec-per-boot ceiling) ‚Äî unchanged from 1.40.8.

## [1.40.8] ‚Äî 2026-05-29 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî bite 8 (arc harden + burn prep).** Fixes a real argv bug the 1.40.7 smoke could not catch, adds an argv-dereference gate, and ships a one-command iron burn-prep script. The 1.39.x VFS + 1.40.x exec arcs are now iron-ready.)

### Fixed
- **argv strings landed in the wrong buffer (1.40.7 regression).** `elf_load_from_file` wrote each argv string into `elf_file_stack` ‚Äî a *kernel* scratch buffer ‚Äî while `argv[i]` pointed at `user_base + wp` in the *user* stack. `argc` (`[rsp]`) was correct, but any program that dereferenced `argv[i]` read uninitialised user memory. Strings are now written to `stack_phys + wp` (the phys alias of the user stack the process sees at `user_base + wp`). The dead `elf_file_stack[512]` buffer is removed.
- **argv string-area overflow bound.** A token is emitted only when `str_o + tl + 1 < 0x4000` ‚Äî a long command line can no longer write past the reserved top `0x1000` of the stack region (this is on top of the existing `argc <= 8` cap).

### Added
- **argv-dereference smoke gate (replaces the 1.40.7 argc-count run).** The exec selftest's exec #2 now builds `/bin/argv` (`exit(argv[1][0])`) and runs `run /bin/argv Z` ‚Üí **exit 90** (0x5A), dereferencing `argv[1]` end-to-end. This is strictly stronger than the argc-count run it replaces: exit 90 requires `argc >= 2` AND `argv[1]` to point at the real `"Z"` string in the user stack ‚Äî exactly the wrong-buffer bug above, which argc-count alone could not catch. `exec-smoke.sh` is 7 gates (ENOEXEC, subdir dispatch, EXEC-DISK-OK, exit 42, exit 90, clean return, e2fsck).
- **`scripts/burn-prep.sh`** ‚Äî one command to get iron-ready: runs the full arc sweep (aborts on red), builds the EXEC selftest kernel as `build/agnos`, reports freshness, and prints the exact flash + FB-watch steps (track A exec + track B FAT/exFAT) pointing at `exec-iron-manual-tests.md`.

### Found (diagnosed, not yet fixed)
- **Third sequential `run` in one boot fails at load** (`run: not an executable`). `elf_load_from_file` ‚Üí `pmm_alloc_2mb()` returns 0: each exec allocates 2 MB pages for its segments + user stack and **never frees them on program exit** (no process teardown), so the 2 MB-page pool exhausts on the 3rd exec. Multi-run (1.40.6) was only ever validated at 2 execs. The selftest stays within that proven 2-exec envelope; **process teardown / page reclaim on exit** is now the top exec follow-on (it gates real interactive multi-program use).

### Validation
- `scripts/exec-smoke.sh` **7/7** (ENOEXEC, subdir dispatch, EXEC-DISK-OK, `run: exit 42`, `run: exit 90` argv deref, clean return, e2fsck), `check.sh` 11/11. `scripts/sweep.sh` **6/6** (ARC SWEEP: PASS ‚Äî its exec gate now ends on `exit 90`). Production build **1,034,704 B ‚Üí 1,034,768 B**.

### Still follow-on
- **Process teardown** (free 2 MB pages on exit ‚Äî lifts the 2-exec-per-boot ceiling; see Found above) ‚Äî top priority.
- The user-driven combined VFS + exec **iron burn** (per `exec-iron-manual-tests.md`) ‚Äî run `scripts/burn-prep.sh`, flash, watch the FB.
- Deferred: argv envp/auxv *contents*, preemptive ring 3 (interrupt-path KPTI), Meltdown-grade KPTI, FAT/exFAT exec, `$PATH` search, `#!` scripts.

## [1.40.7] ‚Äî 2026-05-29

## [1.40.6] ‚Äî 2026-05-29 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî bite 6 (post-arc follow-on): multi-`run` in one boot.** The shell can now run several programs sequentially ‚Äî each loads from disk, runs in ring 3, and returns its own exit code. Two fixes resolved the second-exec fault.)

### Fixed

- **CR3 not restored on program exit** ‚Äî the SYSCALL stub left CR3 = the per-process CR3 (mirrors 0-1 GB + PDPT[1..3] only, NOT the device MMIO). After the first program exited, the kernel kept running on that CR3, so the *next* `run`'s ext2 read faulted on the **NVMe BAR** (`CR2‚âà0xC0000010xx`). `kernel_resume` now restores the boot kernel CR3 (`0x1000`, maps everything incl. MMIO) before returning.
- **Exit code mis-attributed across runs** ‚Äî `proc_current` was never updated when a program ran, so a second `run`'s `exit(n)` recorded on the prior pid and came back 0. `sh_cmd_run` now sets `proc_current = pid` before `exec_and_wait` ‚Üí each run reports its true exit code.

### Changed

- **`scripts/sweep.sh`** ‚Äî each smoke now runs **once** per attempt (was running twice ‚Äî pass-check + display) with a single retry, so a transient host-load/QEMU-timing flake doesn't fail the whole sweep (a real failure still fails both attempts).

### Validation

- exec-smoke extended to run `/bin/prog2` **twice** in one boot: ENOEXEC (`/notelf`) ‚Üí run #1 `EXEC-DISK-OK` + `run: exit 42` ‚Üí run #2 `EXEC-DISK-OK` + `run: exit 42` ‚Üí `exec: selftest done`; `e2fsck` clean. exec-smoke 8/8 standalone. `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7 PASS**, `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **1,033,512 B ‚Üí 1,033,528 B**.

### Still follow-on

- **1.40.7** ‚Äî argv/env passing to programs. Then **1.40.8** ‚Äî final hardening + burn prep (updated scripts). Deferred: preemptive ring 3 (interrupt-path KPTI), Meltdown-grade KPTI, FAT/exFAT exec, the user-driven combined VFS+exec iron burn.


## [1.40.5] ‚Äî 2026-05-29 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî bite 5 (arc close): hardening + clean multi-run + automated arc sweep + manual iron plan.** Fixes the two 1.40.4 follow-ons (clean exec_and_wait return; the kstack-collision flake), adds a one-command sweep of the last two arcs, and writes the on-iron manual checklist. The 1.40.x exec arc + the 1.39.x VFS arc are now ready for the combined iron burn.)

### Fixed

- **Clean return-and-continue from `exec_and_wait`** ‚Äî replaced the partial frame-restore with a full setjmp/longjmp: `exec_and_wait` snapshots callee-saved regs (rbx/r12-r15) + the caller's rbp + return rsp/rip into `exec_ctx`; `kernel_resume` restores them all on the program's `exit()`. The ring-3 program clobbers callee-saved regs, so the old path resumed the kernel with garbage and halted after one run. Now the caller continues (a program runs, control returns to the shell-loop frame) ‚Äî proven: the exec selftest is a real function that runs `/bin/prog2` then prints `exec: selftest done`.
- **Exec flake (kstack VA collision)** ‚Äî the SYSCALL kernel stack was `pmm_alloc_2mb`'d late, so the heap (net/fs boot growth) had fragmented the low region and the stack sometimes landed at phys `0x400000`, whose identity VA overlaps the user-code page ‚Üí ~17 % intermittent triple-fault. Now **reserved right after `heap_init`** (`syscall_kstack_reserve`, the first `pmm_alloc_2mb`) ‚Üí pinned to phys `0x200000` / VA `0x3F0000`, safely below user VAs. Smoke now ~consistently green (residual rare host-load QEMU timing only; not on iron).

### Added

- **`scripts/sweep.sh`** ‚Äî rebuilds + runs every QEMU self-test smoke for the two latest arcs in one command: baseline `check.sh` (build/test/version/size), 1.39.x FAT/exFAT read+write+subdir, ext2-write regression bar, 1.40.x exec-from-disk; restores the plain build; tallies PASS/FAIL (**7/7 PASS**).
- **agnosticos `exec-iron-manual-tests.md`** ‚Äî the on-archaemenid manual checklist for both arcs (exec-from-disk A1‚ÄìA6: ENOEXEC ‚Üí ring-3 run `EXEC-DISK-OK` + `run: exit 42` + clean return + post-burn `e2fsck`; FAT/exFAT verbs B1‚ÄìB4) with the dispositive bars. Linked from `iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-139-cycle` (the two burns are now one hardware session).

### Validation

- `scripts/sweep.sh` **7/7 PASS**. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. exec-smoke green incl. the new `exec: selftest done` (clean-return) gate. Production build **1,033,448 B ‚Üí 1,033,512 B**.

### Known follow-ons (post-arc)

- **Multi-`run` in one boot**: a *second* sequential real exec has a per-process (pid-reuse) teardown issue ‚Üí its own bite. The single-exec-then-return path (the common shell case) is fixed.
- Deferred (unchanged): argv/env, preemptive ring 3 (interrupt-path KPTI), Meltdown-grade KPTI, FAT/exFAT exec, the combined VFS+exec **iron burn** (user-driven, per `exec-iron-manual-tests.md`).


## [1.40.4] ‚Äî 2026-05-29 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî bite 4: subdir/CWD program paths + ENOEXEC/E2BIG bounds.** `run` resolves a program by a subdirectory or CWD-relative path and refuses non-ELF / oversized files cleanly. The exec-smoke now loads + runs a program from `/bin/prog2` (a subdirectory) and refuses a non-ELF `/notelf`.)

### Added / Changed

- **Subdir + CWD program paths** ‚Äî `sh_cmd_run` already routes through `sh_abspath` (CWD-relative ‚Üí absolute) + `ext2_path_lookup` (multi-component), so a program in a subdirectory (`run /bin/prog2`, or relative from the CWD) resolves and runs. Validated end-to-end (load + ring-3 run + exit code) from `/bin/prog2`.
- **E2BIG** ‚Äî `elf_load_from_file` rejects files larger than 16 MB (`fsize > 0x1000000`) in addition to the existing `< 64`-byte floor; oversized images return -1 (`run: not an executable`).
- **ENOEXEC** ‚Äî a non-ELF file (no `0x7F 'E' 'L' 'F'` magic / 64-bit class) is already rejected by `elf_load_from_file`; verified `/notelf` is refused cleanly with no crash.

### Validation

- `exec-smoke.sh` restructured + PASS: ENOEXEC (`/notelf` refused) ‚Üí subdir program `/bin/prog2` loaded from a subdirectory, run in ring 3 (`EXEC-DISK-OK`), exit 42 captured; `e2fsck -fn` clean. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4; ext2-write regression green. Production build **1,033,304 B ‚Üí 1,033,448 B**.

### Known follow-ons

- **Multi-run continuation**: `exec_and_wait` returns into the kernel *main-body inline* frame imperfectly, so the boot selftest runs the real exec last (a second sequential exec from the inline body won't continue). The real shell loop is a normal frame; a clean multi-run/shell-loop validation + the **~17 % intermittent exec flake** (host-load / `pmm_alloc_2mb` timing) are hardening items for a later bite.
- Deferred (unchanged): argv/env, preemptive ring 3 (interrupt-path KPTI), Meltdown-grade KPTI, FAT/exFAT exec.


## [1.40.3] ‚Äî 2026-05-29 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî bite 3: ring-3 execution (the RUN half) ‚Äî WORKING.** `run /prog` now loads a static ELF64 off ext2, executes it in ring 3, and returns its exit code: the exec-smoke's hand-built program prints `EXEC-DISK-OK` (its `write(1,‚Ä¶)` reaches the console) and the shell reports `run: exit 42`. This brought up the entire ring-3 + SYSCALL path, which had never executed (the KTEST always bypassed it) ‚Äî ten distinct first-run bugs, below. Run-to-completion / single-threaded model: the program runs with interrupts masked (preemptive ring 3 is a later arc).)

### Fixed ‚Äî ring-3 + SYSCALL bring-up (`ring3.cyr`, `syscall_hw.cyr`, `proc.cyr`, `elf.cyr`, `vfs.cyr`)

- **`mov cr3, r10` mis-encoded** in the SYSCALL entry stub as `44 0F 22 DA` (REX.R ‚Üí selects nonexistent cr11 ‚Üí `#UD`); corrected to `41 0F 22 DA` (REX.B ‚Üí r10). Both the entry and exit CR3 switches. *This was the core blocker.*
- **LSTAR programmed via NX-stack bytecode**: `syscall_init` built the `wrmsr` in a local buffer and ran it via `call rax` ‚Äî with `EFER.NXE` set the stack is no-execute, so LSTAR was never written and `SYSCALL` vectored into data. Replaced with a direct `wrmsr` (`var‚Üírax` + `rdx:eax` split). **Not a Cyrius bug ‚Äî executing NX stack data.**
- **`EFER.SCE` not in effect** at the first ring-3 `SYSCALL` (`#UD`) ‚Äî re-asserted before entering ring 3.
- **KPTI dual-CR3 mismatch**: ring 3 ran under one CR3 while the stub switched to a different `exec_cr3_g` that didn't map the stub. Collapsed the split for now ‚Äî ring 3 + the stub run under the one full per-process CR3 (kernel pages supervisor, user U/S; protection holds, only Meltdown-defense deferred). `proc_create_address_space` stashes the kernel-side CR3 as the "user" CR3.
- **SYSCALL kernel stack** was a hardcoded `0x1F0000` (now overlapping the >1.9 MB kernel image) ‚Äî replaced with a `pmm_alloc_2mb` region, allocated **before** the user process so its identity VA can't collide with the user stack VA window.
- **SMAP** (CR4.SMAP, set on Zen / `-cpu max`) blocked the kernel reading the user buffer for `write()` ‚Äî added `STAC`/`CLAC` around the handler call (Linux user-copy model; SMAP stays enabled).
- **`tss_set_rsp0` read the Local APIC after the CR3 switch** (user CR3 lacks the APIC) ‚Üí moved before the switch.
- **Timer interrupt in ring 3** ran an ISR under the minimal CR3 ‚Üí masked `IF` in the ring-3 RFLAGS (fits the synchronous run-to-completion model; `SYSCALL` is unaffected by IF).
- **2 MB-page vs 4 KB `pmm_alloc`** in `elf_load_from_file` left loaded code at the wrong physical address (`#UD` in ring 3) ‚Üí `pmm_alloc_2mb`.
- **fd 1/2 not wired to the console** ‚Üí ring-3 `write(1,‚Ä¶)` went nowhere; `vfs_init` now maps stdin/stdout/stderr to the serial device.

### Validation

- **`exec-smoke.sh` PASS (6/6)**: `/prog` written to ext2 ‚Üí stream-loaded ‚Üí ring 3 ‚Üí `EXEC-DISK-OK` + `run: exit 42`; `e2fsck -fn` clean. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4; FAT/exFAT/ext2 write smokes all green (no regression). Production build **1,033,296 B ‚Üí 1,033,304 B**.

### Not yet (next)

- **1.40.4** ‚Äî CWD-relative + subdir program paths (rides the 1.39.9 resolver) + ENOEXEC/E2BIG bounds. **1.40.5+** ‚Äî hardening + the combined VFS+exec iron burn. Deferred: argv/env, preemptive ring 3 (interrupt-path KPTI), Meltdown-grade KPTI, FAT/exFAT exec.


## [1.40.2] ‚Äî 2026-05-29 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî bite 2: streaming ELF loader (the load half).** `run <path>` reads a real static ELF64 off ext2, validates it, and maps it into a new process ‚Äî proven end-to-end. The ring-3 *execution* step (`exec_and_wait`) is unproven pre-existing infra (the KTEST always bypassed it) and is its own bring-up at 1.40.3, so `run` currently loads + reports rather than hanging on an unvalidated ring-3 transition.)

### Added

- **`elf_load_from_file(path, namelen)`** (`elf.cyr`) ‚Äî streaming ELF64 loader from a filesystem path. Same hardened validation as `elf_load` (magic/class/entry/phdr/segment bounds), but the image is never held in one buffer: the header+phdrs are read into `elf_hdr_buf` (4 KB module-global), then **each `PT_LOAD` segment's file bytes are read directly into its physical pages via the kernel identity mapping** ‚Äî `pmm_alloc` a page, `vmm_map(phys,phys)`, `proc_map_page` it into the process, then `vfs_read_file_at(... phys+off ...)`. ext2 only (offset reads); FAT/exFAT exec is a follow-on.
- **`vfs_read_file_at(path, namelen, offset, dst, len)` + `vfs_file_size(path, namelen)`** (`vfs.cyr`) ‚Äî ext2 offset-read + size (the streaming primitives). FAT/exFAT return -1 (read-from-start only).
- **`run <path>` shell verb** (`shell.cyr`) ‚Äî resolves the path (CWD-relative), `elf_load_from_file`, reports `run: loaded pid=N entry=‚Ä¶ (ring3 exec: 1.40.3)`. The `exec_and_wait` call is deferred to 1.40.3.
- **`EXEC_SELFTEST` build flag** + **`exec-smoke.sh`** ‚Äî the kernel hand-builds a minimal static ELF64 (`write(1,"EXEC-DISK-OK\n"); exit(42)`), writes it to ext2 as `/prog`, then `run`s it; the smoke gates on the load (`run: loaded pid=`, `entry=0x400078`) + `e2fsck -fn` clean.

### Changed

- **No CR3 switch during load.** The first cut switched CR3 into the half-built process address space to `memcpy` segments (the idiom `elf_load` uses) ‚Äî it **hung** (KPTI + the fragile `var‚Üírax` CR3-load idiom). Rewritten to populate physical pages via their kernel identity address with no CR3 switch ‚Äî simpler and robust.

### Validation

- `exec-smoke` PASS: `/prog` written to ext2, stream-loaded, header parsed from disk (`entry=0x400078`), mapped into pid 0; `e2fsck -fn` clean. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4; FAT-write (incl. 1.40.1 `vfsrf`) + ext2-write regression green. Production build **1,024,256 B ‚Üí 1,033,296 B**.

### Not yet (next)

- **1.40.3 ‚Äî ring-3 execution bring-up**: make `exec_and_wait` actually run the loaded program in ring 3 and return its exit code (debug the iretq + KPTI user-CR3 mapping of the user pages ‚Äî never validated end-to-end). Then the test ELF prints `EXEC-DISK-OK` + `run: exit 42`.
- **1.40.4** ‚Äî CWD/subdir program paths + ENOEXEC/E2BIG bounds. **1.40.5+** ‚Äî hardening + the combined VFS+exec iron burn.


## [1.40.1] ‚Äî 2026-05-29 (**Exec-from-disk ‚Äî bite 1: `vfs_read_file` (whole-file read past the 4 KB cap).** The one genuinely new primitive the exec load path needs: read an *entire* file from a VFS path into a caller buffer, resolving whichever filesystem backs it. `cat`/`vfs_open_secondary` wrap a file as a ‚â§ 4 KB `VFS_MEMFILE`, but an ELF is far larger ‚Äî the readers themselves (`fatfs_read`/`exfat_read`/`ext2_read_at`) were never 4 KB-capped, only the `*_open` memfile wrappers were. No code change to the load/run path yet ‚Äî that's 1.40.2.)

### Added ‚Äî `vfs_read_file` (`vfs.cyr`)

- **`vfs_read_file(path, namelen, dst, cap)`** ‚Äî reads a whole file into `dst` (up to `cap`), returning bytes read or -1. ext2 is path‚Üíinode (regular-file only, via `ext2_path_lookup` + `ext2_read_at`); FAT/exFAT go through the 1.39.x resolver (`fatfs_resolve_parent`/`exfat_resolve_parent` + the `*_in_dir` finders) + a full cluster-chain read. Precedence mirrors `cat`: ext2 first when mounted, else the selected secondary FS. Reuses the `vfs_sec_name_ok` (1..255) ingress bound. initrd intentionally not covered (its programs already exec from memory).
- **`vfs_scratch_buf`** (`vfs.cyr`, module-global, 8 KB) ‚Äî a staging buffer larger than one page. `kmalloc` and `pmm_alloc` both cap at a single 4 KB page, so a >4 KB staging area must be a reserved region, not an allocator call (see below).

### Findings (shape 1.40.2)

- **No large-contiguous allocator.** `kmalloc`/`pmm_alloc` both top out at one 4 KB page ‚Äî so the exec load buffer cannot be `kmalloc`'d (the prior-art doc's original plan). The 1.40.2 load path will instead stream segments from disk and/or use a reserved window; design decision re-opened in [`exec-from-disk-prior-art.md`](https://github.com/MacCracken/agnosticos/blob/main/docs/development/prior-art/exec-from-disk-prior-art.md) ¬ß3.
- **`var X[N]` in the kernel main body is function-local-sized** (N bytes; the main body runs as a PARSE_PROG function context), not the `N√ó8` module-global form ‚Äî a true module-global (top-level in an included file, like `vfs_table`) is required for a large fixed buffer. Memory: [[cyrius-var-array-u64-units]].

### Validation

- New whole-file-read selftest in both write selftests: write a 6000-byte pattern file, clear the staging buffer, `vfs_read_file` it back, verify length + bytes at offset 5000/5999 (**past the old 4 KB cap**). `fat-write-smoke` + `exfat-write-smoke` each +1 gate, both **PASS**.
- No regression: `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4, FAT/exFAT read smokes + ext2-write regression all green. Production build **1,014,528 B ‚Üí 1,024,256 B** (+9,728 B: `vfs_read_file` + the 8 KB `vfs_scratch_buf`).


## [1.40.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**Exec-from-disk arc ‚Äî cycle open.** The second base-maturity exit leg: base = FS-crash-safe (done, 1.37‚Äì1.39) **+ exec-from-disk** (this arc). Today programs come only from the in-memory initrd; 1.40.x makes the kernel **read an ELF from a VFS path, load it, run it, and collect its exit code** ‚Äî using the writable + path-addressable filesystem the 1.39.x VFS lift earned. `elf_load` (a hardened static ELF64 *buffer* loader) + the process/scheduler plumbing (`proc`/`sched`/`spawn`/`waitpid`) already exist; the single gap is **disk ‚Üí contiguous buffer** (the 4 KB memfile cap blocks real binaries) + a shell `run` verb. Lean cycle-open: version + arc plan, no kernel code yet. **1.41.x remains shell‚Üíagnoshi** (kernel-slimming); the combined VFS+exec iron burn lands at the end of 1.40.x.)

### Added

- **Arc plan** ‚Äî agnosticos [`exec-from-disk-prior-art.md`](https://github.com/MacCracken/agnosticos/blob/main/docs/development/prior-art/exec-from-disk-prior-art.md): multi-source survey (xv6 `exec.c`, Linux `load_elf_binary`, FreeBSD `imgact_elf`, Plan 9), AGNOS current-state gap analysis, design decisions (slurp-then-load reusing `elf_load` unchanged; bounded `kmalloc` load buffer; `vfs_read_file` whole-file primitive; `run <path>` verb; static-only, argv deferred), and the bite ladder.

### Cycle plan (no code this cut)

- **1.40.1** ‚Äî `vfs_read_file`: arbitrary-size whole-file read into a bounded buffer (past the 4 KB memfile cap), FS-resolved across ext2/FAT/exFAT.
- **1.40.2** ‚Äî `run <path>`: `vfs_read_file` ‚Üí `elf_load` ‚Üí `waitpid` ‚Üí exit code; selftest seeds + runs a tiny static ELF off the FS.
- **1.40.3** ‚Äî CWD-relative + subdir program paths (rides the 1.39.9 resolver) + ENOEXEC/E2BIG bounds; per-FS coverage.
- **1.40.4+** ‚Äî arc-close hardening; **combined VFS (1.39.x) + exec (1.40.x) iron burn** (extends `#tracker-139-cycle`).


## [1.39.9] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 9: FAT/exFAT subdirectory paths.** Removes the last FAT/exFAT-vs-ext2 asymmetry ‚Äî ext2 verbs were already path/CWD-aware; FAT/exFAT were root-only. Pure backend work: the shell already passed full slashed paths to the secondary dispatch, the backends just ignored the slashes. A path-walk resolves all-but-last component to a directory cluster; the directory finders are generalized to take a start cluster. **`cat`/`touch`/`echo >`/`rm`/`mkdir`/`rmdir`/`mv` now operate inside subdirectories on both filesystems.** This completes the functional verb surface for the VFS lift; the arc closes pending the user-driven iron burn.)

### Added ‚Äî FAT subdir paths (`fatfs.cyr`)

- **`fatfs_resolve_parent(path, pathlen)`** ‚Äî walks a path's leading components, returning the parent directory's cluster (`0` = root) and setting `fatfs_leaf_off`/`len` for the final component. Rejects a missing/non-directory intermediate component or a trailing slash.
- **`fatfs_find_in_dir(dir_clus, ‚Ä¶)`** / **`fatfs_find_free_slot_in_dir(dir_clus)`** ‚Äî generalize the root finders to any directory. Sentinel **`dir_clus == 0` delegates to the existing `fatfs_find_root`/`fatfs_find_free_root_slot`**, so a bare name (no `/`) is byte-identical to pre-bite-9. A subdir chain is walked (and extended via the chain-generic `fat_root_extend`).
- Wired open/create/write/delete/mkdir/rmdir/rename to resolve the parent then operate on `(dir_clus, leaf)`. `mkdir`'s `..` entry now points at the real parent cluster (`0` = root, per spec).

### Added ‚Äî exFAT subdir paths (`exfat.cyr`)

- **`exfat_resolve_parent`** (mirror of the FAT walk) + **`_in(start_clus, ‚Ä¶)`** variants of `exfat_find` / `exfat_dir_end_index` / `exfat_dir_cluster_for_index` / `exfat_dir_append_set` / `exfat_emit_set`. The root-named functions are kept as thin wrappers delegating to `_in(0, ‚Ä¶)`, so every existing root caller is unchanged.
- Wired open/create/write/delete/mkdir/rmdir/rename to resolve + emit into the target directory.

### Changed

- **`mv` (rename)** on both backends is bounded to **same-parent** rename ‚Äî src and dst must share a parent directory. Cross-directory move is a documented follow-on (FAT does an in-place dirent rewrite; exFAT re-emits the set into the parent + soft-deletes the old). Differing parents are rejected.

### Validation

- Subdir scenarios added to both write selftests + smokes (create/write/`cat`/`rm`/`mkdir`/`mv` against slashed paths inside a subdirectory): `fat-write-smoke` +4 gates (incl. content readback + same-parent dir rename), `exfat-write-smoke` +4 gates (in-kernel `exfat_find_in_dir` find-back inside the subdir's cluster + `cat` output). Both **PASS**, host `fsck.fat`/`fsck.exfat` clean.
- No regression: FAT/exFAT read smokes green; **ext2-write regression bar** (W1‚ÄìW5) PASS ‚Äî the iron-validated ext2/jbd2 path is untouched. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4.
- Production build **1,007,696 B ‚Üí 1,014,528 B** (+6,832 B: subdir resolvers + per-directory finder generalization across both backends), multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (follow-ons)

- **Cross-directory `mv`** (different parent dirs) ‚Äî same-parent only for now.
- **`ls <subdir>`** listing ‚Äî `ls` lists the primary FS root; a path arg for the secondary listing is a small follow-on (the file/dir verbs all take subdir paths).
- **User-driven iron burn** per the `#tracker-139-cycle` rubric ‚Äî closes the VFS-lift arc on real hardware.


## [1.39.8] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 8 (arc close): mount-registry consolidation + ingress hardening.** The functional verb surface completed at 1.39.7; this cut consolidates the dispatch and hardens its ingress seam. The seven `vfs_*_secondary` verbs each carried a copy of the same four-line non-ESP-preference chain ‚Äî the single-primary-FS *policy* scattered across seven sites; it now lives once in `vfs_secondary_select()`. A bounds/ingress review of both writable backends added one guard at the generic seam. **No behavior change for any valid name.** FAT/exFAT *subdirectory* paths split out to bite 9 / 1.39.9 ‚Äî Large effort, ~14 functions across both backends.)

### Changed ‚Äî mount-registry consolidation (`vfs.cyr`)

- **`vfs_secondary_select()`** ‚Äî new single source of the single-primary-FS selection policy (prefer a non-ESP data partition; fall back to an ESP-resident FS only when it's the sole mount). The seven `vfs_*_secondary` verbs (`print_dir`/`create`/`write`/`delete`/`mkdir`/`rmdir`/`rename`) are now thin switches over its `SecFs` result instead of each re-stating the four-line `fatfs_active`/`fat_on_esp`/`exfat_active`/`exfat_on_esp` chain. **Byte-identical behavior** ‚Äî same precedence, same ESP-fallback-only-if-sole-mount rule. `vfs_sync_secondary` keeps flushing whichever backend is active *regardless of ESP* (a durability barrier must reach the boot ESP too), so it intentionally does not route through the non-ESP selector.

### Added ‚Äî ingress hardening (`vfs.cyr`)

- **`vfs_sec_name_ok(namelen)`** ‚Äî bounds the name length (1..255) at the generic dispatch seam, the layer every secondary write/dir verb funnels through. Bounds the one *unbounded* read on the path (`fatfs_build_83`'s dot-scan loops the full `namelen`) and backstops `exfat_create`/`exfat_mkdir`, which carried no namelen guard of their own. 255 is longer than any FAT (8.3 / LFN) or exFAT name. Defense-in-depth: the shell verbs already guard `sh_trim_len < 1`; this protects against a future non-shell caller. Behavior is unchanged for every valid name.

### Hardening review (no fix needed)

- Re-derived (not comment-trusted, per the audit discipline) every backend buffer against its worst case: `exfat_set_buf[80]`=640 B ‚â• the 608 B max 19-entry dir-set (the `total > 19` cap in `exfat_emit_set` is what protects it); `fatfs_dir_buf`/`exfat_dir_buf`/`exfat_bmp_buf`=512 B = one sector; `exfat_name_buf` 256 B with `< 255` write guards on every reconstruction loop. All sound; the only gap was the unbounded `namelen` read, fixed above.

### Validation

- `check.sh` **11/11**, `test.sh` **4/4**. All four FAT/exFAT smokes green through the consolidated selector ‚Äî `fat-smoke` / `fat-write-smoke` / `exfat-smoke` / `exfat-write-smoke` PASS, host `fsck.fat` / `fsck.exfat` clean. **ext2-write regression bar** (`ext2-write-smoke` W1‚ÄìW5) PASS ‚Äî the iron-validated ext2/jbd2 path is untouched.
- Production build **1,008,816 B ‚Üí 1,007,696 B** (‚àí1,120 B net: ‚àí1,760 B from folding the duplicated chains, +640 B for the guard fn + six call sites), multiboot2 OK.

### Iron pre-audit

- **1.39.x VFS-lift iron-burn rubric written** ‚Üí `agnosticos/docs/development/iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-139-cycle` (8-row falsification table; root-only verbs). Folds in the long-pending 1.34.x FAT/exFAT iron burn. Mechanism: the shell verbs only fire when `ext2_active == 0`, so the burn uses a `FATFS_WRITE_SELFTEST` / `EXFAT_WRITE_SELFTEST` self-driver (FB-readable). Test-surface fork (brick-safe USB FAT32/exFAT data stick vs. boot-ESP write) is the user's call. User-driven; no auto-run.

### Not yet (next)

- **1.39.9 ‚Äî bite 9**: FAT/exFAT *subdirectory* paths in the verbs (currently root-only). Every FAT/exFAT op hardcodes `var clus = fatfs_root_cluster` as the start directory; subdir support threads a start-directory cluster through ~14 functions in both backends (generic path-walk resolving all-but-last component) plus shell path-splitting. ext2 verbs are already path/CWD-aware ‚Äî this removes the last FAT/exFAT asymmetry.


## [1.39.7] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 7: `mv` (rename) + `sync` on FAT/exFAT.** The last functional verb. `mv` renames within a FAT/exFAT root; `sync` flushes the backend. With this, the full shell verb set ‚Äî `cat`/`ls`/`touch`/`echo >`/`rm`/`mkdir`/`rmdir`/`mv`/`sync` ‚Äî works on both secondary filesystems. Neither FS has POSIX-atomic rename, so each uses its own content-preserving approach (no copy).)

### Added ‚Äî FAT/exFAT rename + sync (`fatfs.cyr`, `exfat.cyr`, `vfs.cyr`)

- **`fatfs_rename`** (FAT32) ‚Äî in-place dirent **name rewrite** (keeps cluster chain + size + attr ‚Üí size-independent, works for files *and* directories). Refuses if the destination exists or source is missing. 8.3 names.
- **`exfat_rename`** ‚Äî re-emits a new dir-set carrying the new name but pointing at the **same clusters** (no content copy / realloc), then soft-deletes the old set (clears InUse, does **not** free clusters). exFAT has no in-place rename (name drives the File-Name entries + NameHash + SetChecksum).
- **`vfs_rename_secondary` / `vfs_sync_secondary`** ‚Äî dispatch (non-ESP-preferring). `sync` is a `blk_flush_on` durability barrier (FAT/exFAT writes already go straight to the block layer).

### Changed

- **`sh_cmd_mv` / `sh_cmd_sync`** ‚Äî ext2-absent branches wired to the dispatch (was `mv: no fs` / `sync: no fs`). Isolated to the `ext2_active == 0` branch; ext2 path untouched.

### Validation

- `fat-write-smoke.sh`: `touch SHMVA` ‚Üí `mv SHMVA SHMVB` ‚Üí SHMVB present, SHMVA gone, **`fsck.fat -n` clean**; `sync` runs clean ‚Üí **PASS**.
- `exfat-write-smoke.sh`: same; in-kernel `exfat_find` confirms dst present + src gone, **`fsck.exfat -n` clean** (the re-emit left no cross-link / orphan) ‚Üí **PASS**.
- No regression: FAT 31+/exFAT full smoke green; bites 1‚Äì6 green; ext2 untouched. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **1,008,816 B**, multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (next ‚Äî arc close)

- **1.39.8 ‚Äî arc-close**: mount-registry consolidation (replace the non-ESP heuristic with a real primary/mount-point model), FAT/exFAT *subdirectory* paths in the verbs (currently root-only), hardening, + the user-driven iron burn. The functional verb surface is complete; 1.39.8 is consolidation + iron.


## [1.39.6] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 6: `mkdir`/`rmdir` on exFAT.** Completes directory create/remove across both secondary filesystems (FAT landed at 1.39.5). exFAT directories have a different shape than FAT ‚Äî **no `.`/`..` entries**, a dir-set with the Directory `FileAttributes`, and a NoFatChain single cluster ‚Äî so this is its own bite.)

### Added ‚Äî exFAT directory create/remove (`kernel/core/exfat.cyr`)

- **`exfat_mkdir`** ‚Äî allocates + zeros one cluster (an empty exFAT dir is a cluster of `0x00` entries) then emits a dir-set with `FileAttributes=Directory (0x10)` and a Stream Extension flagged `AllocationPossible|NoFatChain (0x03)`, `DataLength` = one cluster. Crash-safe order: cluster allocated + zeroed *before* the dir-set is published (a crash leaks at worst a bitmap cluster).
- **`exfat_rmdir`** ‚Äî verifies the target is a directory (`0x85` `FileAttributes & 0x10`) **and** empty (its cluster holds no `0x85` set-primary entries), then reuses `exfat_delete`'s teardown (clear InUse bits + free the cluster).
- **`exfat_zero_cluster`**, **`exfat_dir_is_empty`** helpers.

### Changed

- **`exfat_emit_set`** gained a `fattr` (FileAttributes) parameter ‚Äî was hardcoded to `0x20` (archive). The two existing callers (`exfat_create`, `exfat_write_file`) pass `0x20` (behavior unchanged); `exfat_mkdir` passes `0x10`.
- **`vfs_mkdir_secondary` / `vfs_rmdir_secondary`** ‚Äî exFAT arm added (was FAT-only since 1.39.5). Non-ESP-preferring.

### Validation

- `exfat-write-smoke.sh`: kernel runs `mkdir SHEXDIR` (in-kernel find-back OK), `mkdir SHEXRMD` + `rmdir SHEXRMD` (find-miss), and **`fsck.exfat -n` clean** (the Directory dir-set, DataLength, NoFatChain flag, and cluster all spec-valid ‚Äî fsck validates SetChecksum/NameHash + directory structure) ‚Üí **PASS**.
- No regression: `exfat_create`/`write_file` (the `emit_set` signature change) still green across the full exfat-write smoke; FAT side untouched (`fat-write-smoke` 31/31); bites 1‚Äì5 green; ext2 untouched. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **1,005,184 B**, multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (next bites)

- `mv` (rename) + `sync` dispatch (1.39.7).
- Mount-registry consolidation + arc-close hardening + iron burn (1.39.8). FAT/exFAT *subdirectory* paths in the verbs (currently root-only) fold in around there.


## [1.39.5] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 5: `mkdir`/`rmdir` on FAT (new backend capability).** The first bite that *adds* a filesystem capability rather than dispatching existing code ‚Äî FAT directory create/remove didn't exist before. `mkdir`/`rmdir` now work on a FAT primary. exFAT directory-create has a different shape (no `.`/`..`, dir-set with the Directory attr) and lands at 1.39.6.)

### Added ‚Äî FAT directory create/remove (`kernel/core/fatfs.cyr`)

- **`fatfs_mkdir`** (FAT32) ‚Äî allocates one cluster, initialises it with `.` (‚Üí self) + `..` (‚Üí parent; cluster 0 for root) then zeros, and publishes a DIRECTORY-attr (`0x10`) dirent in the root. Crash-safe ordering: cluster allocated + written *before* the parent dirent is published (a crash leaks at worst a cluster, never a live dirent ‚Üí free chain).
- **`fatfs_rmdir`** (FAT32) ‚Äî verifies the target is a directory (parent dirent `attr & 0x10`) **and** empty, then reuses `fatfs_delete`'s teardown (mark dirent `0xE5` + free the cluster chain).
- **`fatfs_dir_is_empty`** ‚Äî walks a directory's cluster chain; any live entry (not `0x00`/`0xE5`/LFN/volume/the `.`/`..` dots) means non-empty.
- **`vfs_mkdir_secondary` / `vfs_rmdir_secondary`** (`vfs.cyr`) ‚Äî dispatch (FAT only this cut; exFAT returns -1 until 1.39.6). Non-ESP-preferring.

### Changed

- **`sh_cmd_mkdir` / `sh_cmd_rmdir`** ‚Äî ext2-absent branches route through the dispatch (was `mkdir: no fs` / `rmdir: no fs`). Isolated to the `ext2_active == 0` branch; ext2 path untouched.

### Validation

- `fat-write-smoke.sh`: kernel runs `mkdir SHKEEP`, `mkdir SHRMD`, `rmdir SHRMD`. **`mdir` can descend into `SHKEEP`** (valid `.`/`..` + cluster), `SHRMD` is **gone**, and **`fsck.fat -n` clean** (directory structure + chains sound) ‚Üí **PASS** (31/31 smoke gates green).
- No regression: bites 1‚Äì4 (`cat`/`ls`/`touch`/`echo >`/`rm`) still green; `exfat-write-smoke` PASS; ext2 path untouched. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **1,002,800 B** (crossed 1 MB; ceiling 1.2 MB), multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (next bites)

- **exFAT `mkdir`/`rmdir`** (1.39.6) ‚Äî needs the exFAT directory dir-set (Directory `FileAttributes`, a cluster of zeroed entries, no `.`/`..`).
- `mv` (rename) + `sync` dispatch (1.39.7); mount-registry consolidation + arc-close hardening + iron burn (1.39.8).


## [1.39.4] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 4: `rm` removes FAT/exFAT files.** The shell `rm` verb now deletes files on a FAT/exFAT primary via generic dispatch. Scope note: FAT/exFAT have `delete` but **no directory-create** (`mkdir`/`rmdir` don't exist in either backend), so this bite ships `rm` only; `mkdir`/`rmdir` over FAT/exFAT need a new dir-create implementation and are split into a follow-on bite. Single-primary-FS, root-level, non-ESP-preferring.)

### Added

- **`vfs_delete_secondary(name, namelen)`** (`kernel/core/vfs.cyr`) ‚Äî generic delete dispatch over `fatfs_delete` / `exfat_delete`, same non-ESP-preferring selection as create/write. Returns 0 on success, -1 on miss-or-failure.

### Changed

- **`sh_cmd_rm`** ‚Äî ext2-absent branch deletes a bare-named file in the FAT/exFAT root via `vfs_delete_secondary` (was `rm: no fs`). Isolated to the `ext2_active == 0` branch; ext2 path untouched.

### Validation

- `fat-write-smoke.sh`: kernel runs `touch SHRMTGT.TXT` then `rm SHRMTGT.TXT`; the target is **absent on disk** (`mdir`) and **`fsck.fat -n` clean** (chain/dirent freed, no leak) ‚Üí **PASS**.
- `exfat-write-smoke.sh`: same; in-kernel `exfat_find` misses after `rm` (`exfatw: shell rm gone OK`) + **`fsck.exfat -n` clean** ‚Üí **PASS**.
- No regression: bite 1‚Äì3 gates (`cat`/`ls`/`touch`/`echo >`) still green; ext2 path untouched. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **998,312 B**, multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (next bites)

- **`mkdir`/`rmdir` over FAT/exFAT** (1.39.5) ‚Äî requires implementing directory-create in both backends (allocate a dir cluster, init `.`/`..`, add the parent dirent / dir-set with the directory flag). The verbs stay ext2-only until then.
- `mv` (rename) + `sync` dispatch (1.39.6); mount-registry consolidation + arc-close hardening + iron burn (1.39.7).

## [1.39.3] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 3: `touch` + `echo >` write to FAT/exFAT.** The write side. The shell's create/write verbs were ext2-only; they now create + write files on a FAT/exFAT primary, completing read+write reach for the secondary filesystems. The FAT/exFAT write code (1.34.x) was `fsck`-clean but unreachable from the shell until now. Single-primary-FS, root-level; the dispatch prefers the non-ESP (real data) FS when multiple mount.)

### Added

- **`vfs_create_secondary` / `vfs_write_secondary`** (`kernel/core/vfs.cyr`) ‚Äî generic create/write dispatch over the non-ext2 backends (`fatfs_create`/`exfat_create`, `fatfs_write_file`/`exfat_write_file`). The write analog of bite 1/2's read/list dispatch.
- **Non-ESP-preferring secondary selection.** When a boot FAT **ESP** *and* a FAT/exFAT **data** partition both mount, the write + list helpers (`vfs_create_secondary`/`vfs_write_secondary`/`vfs_print_dir_secondary`) now target the **non-ESP** FS first (the ESP is boot-only and write-guarded), falling back to an ESP FS only as the sole mount. The single-primary-FS heuristic until the 1.39.6 mount-registry bite. (Surfaced by the exfat-write-smoke, which mounts both.)

### Changed

- **`sh_cmd_touch`** ‚Äî ext2-absent branch creates a bare-named file in the FAT/exFAT root via `vfs_create_secondary` (was `touch: no fs`).
- **`sh_echo_redirect`** ‚Äî ext2-absent branch assembles `text + "\n"` into one buffer and writes it via `vfs_write_secondary` (FAT/exFAT writes are whole-file/overwrite). Was `echo: no fs for redirect`.
- Both changes are isolated to the `ext2_active == 0` branch ‚Äî the ext2 write path is untouched.

### Validation

- `fat-write-smoke.sh` (`FAT_ALLOW_ESP_WRITE=1`): drives `touch SHTOUCH.TXT` + `echo SHELL-FAT-WROTE > SHECHO.TXT` via `sh_exec`; both land on disk (`mdir`/`mtype`) and **`fsck.fat -n` clean** ‚Üí **PASS**.
- `exfat-write-smoke.sh`: same verbs over exFAT; in-kernel find-back + content round-trip (`SHELL-EXFAT-WR`, len=15) + **`fsck.exfat -n` clean** ‚Üí **PASS**.
- No regression: `fat-smoke`/`exfat-smoke` (bite 1/2 `cat`+`ls`) still green; ext2 path untouched. `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **997,560 B**, multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (next bites)

- `rm` / `mkdir` / `rmdir` across FAT/exFAT ‚Äî bite 4 (1.39.4; confirm FAT/exFAT dir-create surface first).
- `mv` (rename) + `sync` dispatch ‚Äî bite 5.
- Mount-registry consolidation (replacing the non-ESP heuristic with a real primary/mount-point model) + arc-close hardening + iron burn ‚Äî bite 6.

## [1.39.2] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 2: `ls` lists FAT/exFAT.** Read-side, second rung. `sh_cmd_ls` was ext2-only (`ls: no ext2 filesystem mounted`); the FAT lister emitted via `serial_print` (invisible on iron per the no-serial constraint) and the exFAT lister only *counted* entries. This cut makes `ls` list a FAT/exFAT primary's root with FB-visible names + sizes, via a generic directory-dispatch seam. Single-primary-FS: ext2 still wins when mounted; FAT/exFAT root lists when it's the primary. Additive, zero-risk to the ext2 path.)

### Added

- **`vfs_print_dir_secondary()`** (`kernel/core/vfs.cyr`) ‚Äî generic directory-listing dispatch for the non-ext2 primary: `fatfs_ls` (FAT) or `exfat_print_dir` (exFAT), returning the entry count or -1 when no secondary FS is mounted. The `ls` analog of bite 1's `vfs_open_secondary`.
- **`exfat_print_dir()`** (`kernel/core/exfat.cyr`) ‚Äî FB-visible exFAT root lister with **name reconstruction**: mirrors the validated `exfat_find` typed dir-set walk (`0x85` File ‚Üí `0xC0` Stream Extension ‚Üí N √ó `0xC1` File Name, 15 UTF-16 chars each), printing `  <name>  <size> bytes` per completed set. Previously `exfat_ls` only counted entries.

### Changed

- **`fatfs_print_name` / `fatfs_ls_sector` / `fatfs_ls`** (`kernel/core/fatfs.cyr`) ‚Äî output converted from `serial_*` (iron-invisible) to FB (`kputc`/`kprint`/`kprintln`). The count return is unchanged, so the FATFS selftest's `lscount` contract is preserved; names are now visible on real hardware.
- **`sh_cmd_ls`** (`kernel/user/shell.cyr`) ‚Äî the ext2-absent branch now falls through to `vfs_print_dir_secondary` instead of erroring. `ls` lists a FAT/exFAT root for the first time. The ext2-active path is untouched (change isolated to the `ext2_active == 0` branch).

### Validation

- `fat-smoke.sh`: drives `ls` via `sh_exec`; the staged `CATTEST.TXT` 8.3 name appears in the boot log ‚Üí **PASS** (`shell 'ls' lists FAT root`). FAT mount + chain-read + bite-1 `cat` gates still green.
- `exfat-smoke.sh`: `ls` dispatch runs clean (marker printed + boot reaches the shell, so `exfat_print_dir` returned) ‚Üí **PASS**. exFAT name reconstruction is logic-mirrored from the e2e-validated `exfat_find` and gets a full name assertion under `EXFAT_SEED=1` (the seeded-file path; default volume is empty as exFAT has no userspace injector).
- `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **994,824 B** (+1,736 vs 1.39.1 for the exFAT lister + dispatch), multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (next bites)

- Generic create/write (`touch`/`echo >`) ‚Äî bite 3 (1.39.3).
- unlink/mkdir/rmdir (1.39.4), rename + `sync` (1.39.5), mount-registry consolidation + arc-close hardening + iron burn (1.39.6).
- FAT/exFAT *subdirectory* paths in `cat`/`ls` (currently root-only) ‚Äî folds into the write bites or a later refinement.

## [1.39.1] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**VFS generic-write lift ‚Äî bite 1: `cat` reaches FAT/exFAT.** The 1.39.x arc's first engineering rung. Until now every shell read/write verb was hardwired to ext2 ‚Äî `cat` tried ext2 then initrd and **never** touched the FAT/exFAT volumes, even though their read/write code (1.34.x) is `fsck`-clean. This cut adds the generic read-dispatch seam: `cat` now falls through to FAT, then exFAT, then initrd. Read-side first by design ‚Äî additive, zero-risk to the iron-validated ext2 write/journal path. Arc plan: agnosticos [`vfs-generic-write-prior-art.md`](https://github.com/MacCracken/agnosticos/blob/main/docs/development/prior-art/vfs-generic-write-prior-art.md).)

### Added

- **`vfs_open_secondary(name, namelen)`** (`kernel/core/vfs.cyr`) ‚Äî generic read-dispatch over the non-ext2 backends: tries `fatfs_open` ‚Üí `exfat_open` ‚Üí `initrd_open` in priority order, each self-guarding on its own mount flag, returning a ready VFS fd (FAT/exFAT via `vfs_create_memfile`) or -1. First hit wins. This is the dispatch seam the write bites (1.39.4+) extend; the dispatch lives in the VFS layer, not scattered across shell verbs.

### Changed

- **`sh_cmd_cat`** (`kernel/user/shell.cyr`) ‚Äî the ext2-miss fallback now routes through `vfs_open_secondary` instead of `initrd_open` directly. `cat` reaches a FAT/exFAT volume's root files for the first time; ext2 (path/CWD-aware) is still tried first, initrd remains the final fallback. No change to the ext2 read path.

### Validation

- New `fat-smoke.sh` gate: the FATFS selftest stages a `CATTEST.TXT` text file and drives the **real shell verb** `cat CATTEST.TXT` via `sh_exec` ‚Üí `sh_cmd_cat` ‚Üí `vfs_open_secondary` ‚Üí `fatfs_open`; its content `VFS-CAT-FAT-OK` in the boot log proves end-to-end reach. **PASS** (alongside FAT32 mount + multi-cluster chain-read, no regression).
- **No ext2 regression**: `ext2-write-smoke.sh` W4b (`shell echo-redirect + cat`) still **PASS** ‚Äî the ext2 `cat` arm is unchanged.
- `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4. Production build **993,088 B** (+256 B vs 1.39.0 for the dispatch helper), multiboot2 OK.

### Not yet (next bites)

- `ls` over FAT/exFAT (1.39.2) ‚Äî needs a generic readdir: `fatfs_ls` currently emits via `serial_print` (invisible on iron per the no-serial-on-iron constraint) and `exfat_ls` only *counts* entries (no name reconstruction). That's its own bite.
- Generic create/write (`touch`/`echo >`), unlink/mkdir/rmdir, rename ‚Äî bites 1.39.4‚Äì1.39.6.

## [1.39.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**Cyrius toolchain re-pin `6.0.3` ‚Üí `6.0.14` ‚Äî language bump.** A standalone language-version cut: the deferred re-pin to current Cyrius plus the VERSION roll that opens the 1.39.x line. No kernel engineering in this tag ‚Äî the 1.39.x **VFS generic-write lift** arc (shared write path for the FAT/exFAT shell verbs + the two now-crash-safe writable filesystems) lands in subsequent 1.39.x cuts, with specific roadmap tags assigned then.)

### Changed

- **Cyrius toolchain pin `6.0.3` ‚Üí `6.0.14`** (`cyrius.cyml`). The pin had been held conservatively on 6.0.3 since the 1.35.5 cycle-open and through the 1.37/1.38 big-write arcs; this cut re-pins to current. **Validated byte-identical**: the production kernel is unchanged at **992,832 B** (multiboot2 ELF64 OK) on 6.0.14, `check.sh` 11/11, `test.sh` 4/4, and the jbd2 integration write-path smoke PASSes on the new toolchain ‚Äî 6.0.14 has zero codegen impact on agnos. The "pin drift" build warning is now cleared.
- VERSION `1.38.11` ‚Üí `1.39.0`.

## [1.38.11] ‚Äî 2026-05-28 (**1.38.x jbd2 journaling arc CLOSE ‚Äî crash-safe journaling iron-validated on archaemenid.** The 1.38.10 CSUM_V2/V3 write+replay code, QEMU-green and Linux-`e2fsck`-oracle-validated, was confirmed on real NAND by the `13810_*` re-burn: the write side commits to the unmodified CSUM_V3 agnos-fs journal, a 100-tx stress loop runs clean, and a mid-cycle power cut recovers to a clean journal with host `e2fsck -fn` clean. This is the cycle-close housekeeping cut ‚Äî the engineering landed at 1.38.10; 1.38.11 records the iron receipt, tidies stale in-code arc markers, and re-verifies the whole gate suite stays green. No behavioral change ‚Äî production build byte-identical to 1.38.10 at **992,832 B**.)

### Iron validation ‚Äî the `13810_*` re-burn (archaemenid, 2026-05-28)

- **Write side PROVEN on real NAND.** The integration path's `commit_tx` ‚Äî which the 1389 burn showed refusing every commit under the pre-1.38.10 CSUM_V3 guard ‚Äî now reads `jbd2-int: commit_tx: COMMITTED seq=4 n_blocks=1 -- checkpoint applied + journal clean` ‚Üí `integration selftest PASS` against the *unmodified* archaemenid agnos-fs journal (CSUM_V3 + 64BIT, `incompat=0x12`).
- **Stress + crash recovery PROVEN.** The 100-commit stress loop closed on `jbd2-crash: 100/100 done` ‚Üí `stress loop PASS (clean shutdown)`; a deliberate **mid-cycle power cut** was recovered on the next boot to `jbd2: clean journal ino=8 size=32760 seq=142`. The journal advanced seq 4 ‚Üí 142 across commit + stress + crash + recovery, with host `e2fsck -fn` clean and the on-disk journal SB `CLEAN` at every checkpoint.
- Iron evidence (photos + per-boot validate verdicts) catalogued in the **agnosticos** repo: `iron-nuc-zen-log.md#tracker-138-cycle` + `iron-nuc-zen-photos/` (`13810-agnos-1.38.10-boot{1,2,3,5}-*.jpg`). This closes the only open carry-forward item the 1.38.10 entry left ("re-burn pending").

### Changed ‚Äî stale in-code arc markers (`ext2.cyr`, comments only ‚Äî codegen unaffected)

- `ext2_metadata_write_or_log` routing-scope note: the "bitmap / group desc / extent-tree nodes to follow in **1.38.7+**" marker (a past version) re-pointed to the **1.39.x VFS generic-write lift**, where the journaled-metadata set actually widens.
- `ext2_jbd2_abort_tx` header comment: replaced the stale "no on-disk effect at 1.38.4 ‚Ä¶ 1.38.5+ will need to ‚Ä¶" future-tense note with the current on-disk-safety invariant (a stray descriptor/data block from a mid-write abort is never replayed because the journal SB is left clean and its sequence un-advanced ‚Äî only a fully-committed tx is ever replayed).

### Validation

- Production build byte-identical to 1.38.10 (**992,832 B**) ‚Äî the version bump + comment tidies introduce zero codegen change. multiboot2 (ELF64) OK.
- `check.sh` **11/11**, `test.sh` **4/4**.
- All five jbd2 smokes **PASS** on a CSUM_V3 journal (each built with its selftest gate): `tx`, `writepath` (`COMMITTED` + SB `s_sequence` 1‚Üí2 clean), `integration` (same), `crash` (4/4 e2fsck-clean across staggered kill points), `replay` (V3 tag parse + tail/commit/data csum gates).
